general.g_text: 내가 운영하는 회사의 사정이 점점 더 어려워졌다. 공장에 불이 나서 피해를 입은 데다가 매출까지 크게 감소한 것이다. 어쩔 수 없이 직원 수를 줄여야 했다. <u>나는 직원들을 한자리에 불러 모아 놓고도 한참을 아무 말도 할 수 없었다. </u> 눈을 지그시 감고 겨우 입을 떼려는 순간 “사장님!”한 직원이 나를 불렀다. 그 직원은 흰 봉투를 내 앞으로 내밀면서 말했다. “그동안 어려운 회사 살림 때문에 고생 많으셨습니다. 어려워진 회사에 도움이 될까 싶어서 저희들이 월급의 10퍼센트씩을 모았습니다. 적은 돈이지만 회사 운영에 도움이 되었으면 합니다. ”순간 나는 마치 천하를 얻은 듯 마음이 든든해졌다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tình hình ở công ty do tôi điều hành ngày càng khó khăn hơn. Nhà máy đã bị hư hại do hỏa hoạn, và doanh số bán hàng đã giảm đáng kể. Việc không thể tránh khỏi là phải cắt giảm nhân viên. <u>Tôi đã gọi tất cả các nhân viên lại nhưng một lúc lâu chẳng thể nói được gì.</u> Khi tôi chuẩn bị nhắm mắt và chưa kịp mở miệng, một nhân viên đã nói: "Ông chủ!". Cậu nhân viên vừa nói vừa chìa ra trước mặt tôi một phong bì màu trắng. “Anh đã phải chịu đựng rất nhiều vì tình hình khó khăn của công ty. Vì muốn giúp đỡ công ty đang gặp khó khăn, chúng tôi đã góp 10% tiền lương hàng tháng. Đây chỉ là một số tiền nhỏ, nhưng tôi hy vọng nó sẽ giúp công ty hoạt động." Trong khoảnh khắc, tôi cảm thấy vững tâm như thể tôi đã giành được cả thiên hạ.
g_text_translate.en: The situation at the company I run is getting more and more difficult. The factory was damaged by the fire, and sales fell dramatically. It is inevitable that staff will have to be cut. <u>I called all the staff but for a long time nothing could be said.</u> When I was about to close my eyes and before I could open my mouth, an employee said, "Mr. CEO!". The employee said as he held out a white envelope in front of me. “You suffered a lot because of the company's difficult situation. Because we want to help the company in difficulty, we have contributed 10% of our monthly salary. It's only a small amount, but I hope it keeps the company going." For a moment, I felt as confident as if I'd won the world.
g_text_translate.fr:
La situation dans l'entreprise que je dirige devient de plus en plus difficile. L'usine a été endommagée par l'incendie et les ventes ont chuté de façon spectaculaire. Il est inévitable que le personnel doive être réduit. <u>J'ai appelé tout le personnel mais pendant longtemps rien n'a pu être dit.</u> Alors que j'étais sur le point de fermer les yeux et avant que je puisse ouvrir la bouche, un employé a dit : "Monsieur le PDG !". dit l'employé en tendant une enveloppe blanche devant moi. « Vous avez beaucoup souffert de la situation difficile de l'entreprise. Parce que nous voulons aider l'entreprise en difficulté, nous avons cotisé 10% de notre salaire mensuel. Ce n'est qu'une petite somme, mais j'espère que cela permettra à l'entreprise de continuer." Pendant un moment, je me suis senti aussi confiant que si j'avais gagné le monde.


#######

The situation at the company I run is getting more and more difficult. The factory was damaged by the fire, and sales fell dramatically. It is inevitable that staff will have to be cut. <u>I called all the staff but for a long time nothing could be said.</u> When I was about to close my eyes and before I could open my mouth, an employee said, "Mr. CEO!". The employee said as he held out a white envelope in front of me. “You suffered a lot because of the company's difficult situation. Because we want to help the company in difficulty, we have contributed 10% of our monthly salary. It's only a small amount, but I hope it keeps the company going." For a moment, I felt as confident as if I'd won the world.
g_text_translate.es:
La situación en la empresa que dirijo es cada vez más difícil. La fábrica resultó dañada por el incendio y las ventas cayeron drásticamente. Es inevitable que haya que recortar personal. <u>Llamé a todo el personal pero durante mucho tiempo no se pudo decir nada.</u> Cuando estaba a punto de cerrar los ojos y antes de que pudiera abrir la boca, un empleado dijo: "¡Señor CEO!". Dijo el empleado mientras sostenía un sobre blanco frente a mí. “Usted sufrió mucho por la difícil situación de la empresa. Porque queremos ayudar a la empresa en dificultades, hemos aportado el 10% de nuestro salario mensual. Es solo una pequeña cantidad, pero espero que mantenga a la empresa en marcha.” Por un momento, me sentí tan seguro como si hubiera ganado el mundo.


#######

g_text_translate.id:
Situasi di perusahaan yang saya jalankan semakin sulit. Pabrik rusak karena kebakaran, dan penjualan turun drastis. Tidak dapat dihindari bahwa staf harus dipotong. <u>Saya memanggil semua staf tetapi untuk waktu yang lama tidak ada yang bisa dikatakan.</u> Ketika saya akan menutup mata dan sebelum saya bisa membuka mulut, seorang karyawan berkata, "Tuan CEO!". Kata karyawan itu sambil mengulurkan amplop putih di depanku. “Anda sangat menderita karena situasi perusahaan yang sulit. Karena kami ingin membantu perusahaan yang kesulitan, kami telah memberikan kontribusi 10% dari gaji bulanan kami. Itu hanya jumlah yang kecil, tapi saya harap itu membuat perusahaan terus berjalan." Untuk sesaat, saya merasa percaya diri seolah-olah saya telah memenangkan dunia.

g_text_translate.ja:
私が経営する会社の状況はますます厳しくなっています。火事で工場が焼失し、売り上げが激減。人員削減は避けられない。 <u>スタッフ全員に電話をかけましたが、長い間何も言えませんでした。</u>目を閉じようとしたとき、口を開く前に、従業員が「社長さん！」と言いました。従業員は白い封筒を私の前に差し出しながら言った。 「あなたは会社の困難な状況のために多くの苦しみを味わいました。困っている会社を助けたいので、月給の10％を寄付しました。微々たる額ですが、会社を存続させたいと思います」 一瞬、世界を制したような自信が湧いた。

g_text_translate.de:
Die Situation in der Firma, die ich leite, wird immer schwieriger. Die Fabrik wurde durch das Feuer beschädigt und der Umsatz ging dramatisch zurück. Es ist unvermeidlich, dass Personal abgebaut werden muss. <u>Ich habe alle Mitarbeiter angerufen, aber es war lange Zeit nichts zu sagen.</u> Als ich gerade meine Augen schließen wollte und bevor ich meinen Mund öffnen konnte, sagte ein Mitarbeiter: „Herr CEO!“. Sagte der Angestellte, als er mir einen weißen Umschlag hinhielt. „Sie haben sehr unter der schwierigen Situation des Unternehmens gelitten. Da wir dem Unternehmen in Schwierigkeiten helfen wollen, haben wir 10 % unseres Monatsgehalts beigesteuert. Es ist nur ein kleiner Betrag, aber ich hoffe, er hält das Unternehmen am Laufen.“ Für einen Moment fühlte ich mich so zuversichtlich, als hätte ich die Welt gewonnen.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我经营的公司的情况越来越困难。工厂被大火烧毁，销售额急剧下降。裁员是不可避免的。 <u>我给所有员工打了电话，但很长一段时间都说不出话来。</u> 当我快要闭上眼睛，还没来得及张开嘴的时候，一个员工说：“总裁先生！”。工作人员边说边把一个白色信封递到我面前。 “因为公司的困境，你受了很多苦。因为我们想帮助困难的公司，我们贡献了我们月薪的10%。这只是一小笔钱，但我希望它能让公司继续运转。” 有那么一刻，我感到自信，好像我赢得了世界。

g_text_translate.it:
La situazione nell'azienda che dirigo sta diventando sempre più difficile. La fabbrica è stata danneggiata dall'incendio e le vendite sono diminuite drasticamente. È inevitabile che il personale debba essere tagliato. <u>Ho chiamato tutto il personale ma per molto tempo non si è potuto dire nulla.</u> Quando stavo per chiudere gli occhi e prima che potessi aprire bocca, un impiegato ha detto: "Signor CEO!". Disse l'impiegato mentre mi tendeva una busta bianca. “Hai sofferto molto per la difficile situazione dell'azienda. Poiché vogliamo aiutare l'azienda in difficoltà, abbiamo contribuito con il 10% del nostro stipendio mensile. È solo una piccola somma, ma spero che faccia andare avanti l'azienda." Per un momento, mi sono sentito fiducioso come se avessi vinto il mondo.

g_text_translate.ru:
Ситуация в компании, которой я руковожу, становится все более сложной. Фабрика пострадала от пожара, а продажи резко упали. Сокращать персонал неизбежно. <u>Я обзвонил весь персонал, но долгое время ничего не мог сказать.</u> Когда я уже собирался закрыть глаза и не успел открыть рот, сотрудник сказал: «Господин генеральный директор!». Сказал сотрудник, протягивая передо мной белый конверт. «Вы очень пострадали из-за сложной ситуации в компании. Поскольку мы хотим помочь компании, оказавшейся в затруднительном положении, мы внесли 10% от нашей ежемесячной зарплаты. Это всего лишь небольшая сумма, но я надеюсь, что она поможет компании выжить». На мгновение я почувствовал такую ​​уверенность, как если бы я выиграл мир.

g_text_translate.th:
สถานการณ์ในบริษัทที่ฉันดูแลเริ่มยากขึ้นเรื่อยๆ ไฟไหม้โรงงานเสียหาย ยอดขายลดลงอย่างมาก หลีกเลี่ยงไม่ได้ที่จะต้องตัดพนักงานออก <u>ฉันโทรหาพนักงานทุกคนแต่ไม่สามารถพูดอะไรได้เป็นเวลานาน</u> ตอนที่ฉันกำลังจะหลับตาและก่อนที่จะอ้าปากได้ พนักงานคนหนึ่งก็พูดว่า "คุณซีอีโอ!" พนักงานพูดพร้อมกับยื่นซองสีขาวออกมาข้างหน้าฉัน “คุณต้องทนทุกข์ทรมานมากเพราะสถานการณ์ที่ยากลำบากของบริษัท เนื่องจากเราต้องการช่วยเหลือบริษัทในยามยากลำบาก เราจึงบริจาค 10% ของเงินเดือนของเรา แม้ว่าจะเป็นเพียงเล็กน้อย แต่ฉันหวังว่ามันจะทำให้บริษัทดำเนินต่อไปได้” ฉันรู้สึกมั่นใจชั่วครู่หนึ่งราวกับว่าฉันจะชนะโลก

g_text_translate.pt:
A situação na empresa que administro está ficando cada vez mais difícil. A fábrica foi danificada pelo incêndio e as vendas caíram drasticamente. É inevitável que o pessoal tenha de ser cortado. <u>Liguei para todos os funcionários, mas por muito tempo nada foi dito.</u> Quando eu estava prestes a fechar os olhos e antes que pudesse abrir a boca, um funcionário disse: "Sr. CEO!". O funcionário disse enquanto estendia um envelope branco na minha frente. “Vocês sofreram muito por causa da situação difícil da empresa. Porque queremos ajudar a empresa em dificuldade, contribuímos com 10% do nosso salário mensal. É apenas uma quantia pequena, mas espero que mantenha a empresa funcionando." Por um momento, me senti tão confiante como se tivesse ganhado o mundo.

g_text_translate.hi:
मैं जिस कंपनी को चलाता हूं उसकी स्थिति और अधिक कठिन होती जा रही है। आग से फैक्ट्री क्षतिग्रस्त हो गई और बिक्री में नाटकीय रूप से गिरावट आई। यह अवश्यंभावी है कि कर्मचारियों की कटौती करनी पड़ेगी। <u>मैंने सभी कर्मचारियों को बुलाया लेकिन काफी देर तक कुछ भी नहीं कहा जा सका।</u> जब मैं अपनी आंखें बंद करने वाला था और इससे पहले कि मैं अपना मुंह खोल पाता, एक कर्मचारी ने कहा, "मिस्टर सीईओ!"। कर्मचारी ने मेरे सामने एक सफेद लिफाफा बढ़ाते हुए कहा। “कंपनी की कठिन परिस्थिति के कारण आपको बहुत कष्ट सहना पड़ा। क्योंकि हम कंपनी को मुश्किल में मदद करना चाहते हैं, इसलिए हमने अपने मासिक वेतन का 10% योगदान दिया है। यह केवल एक छोटी राशि है, लेकिन मुझे उम्मीद है कि यह कंपनी को आगे बढ़ायेगी।" एक पल के लिए, मुझे ऐसा आत्मविश्वास महसूस हुआ जैसे मैंने दुनिया जीत ली हो।

g_text_translate.tr:
Yönettiğim şirketteki durum giderek zorlaşıyor. Fabrika yangından zarar gördü ve satışlar önemli ölçüde düştü. Personelin işten çıkarılması kaçınılmazdır. <u>Tüm personeli aradım ama uzun süre hiçbir şey söyleyemedim.</u> Gözlerimi kapatmak üzereyken ve ağzımı açamadan bir çalışan, "Sayın CEO!" dedi. Çalışan önüme beyaz bir zarf uzatırken şunları söyledi. “Şirketin zor durumundan dolayı çok sıkıntı çektiniz. Zor durumdaki şirkete yardım etmek istediğimiz için aylık maaşımızın %10'unu katkıda bulunduk. Bu sadece küçük bir miktar ama umarım şirketin ayakta kalmasını sağlar." Bir an için sanki dünyayı kazanmışım gibi kendime güven duydum.

Because we want to help the company in difficulty, we have contributed 10% of our monthly salary. It's only a small amount, but I hope it keeps the company going." content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 화재가 발생한 후 회사의 사정이 좋아졌다.
나는 회사에서 일할 새로운 직원들을 뽑았다.
직원들이 월급의 일부를 걷어 회사를 도왔다.
흰 봉투 안에는 직원들이 쓴 편지가 들어 있었다.

explain.vi:

2. Tôi đã thuê nhân viên mới làm việc cho công ty.
3. Nhân viên đã giúp công ty bằng cách góp một phần tiền lương của họ.
4. Bên trong phong bì trắng là một lá thư do nhân viên viết.
--------------------
월급의 일부를 걷다: Gom góp một phần tiền lương

general.g_text: 학창시절 내내 서울 생활을 꿈꿨던 나는 대학에 합격하면서 그토록 바라던 서울에서 살게 되었다. 시간제 아르바이트를 하는 것만으로는 생활 하기가 빠듯해서 늘 허덕거려야 했다. 당연히 어머니와의 전화 통화는 주로 돈 이야기가 되어 버렸다. “아휴, 여기서 대학 다녔으면 그렇게 돈 걱정은 안 해도 됐을걸. 서울까지 가서 마음 편히 공부도 못하고. 엄마가 제대로 도와주지 못해 미안해. ” 어머니가 고생하는 딸이 안쓰러워서 하는 말에 <u>눈물이 핑 돈 적도 있었다</u>. 전액 장학금을 주겠다는 지방 대학을 포기하고 택한 서울행이었다. 그런데 매번 돈타령이나 하면서 가뜩이나 형편이 어려운 부모님에게 마음의 부담만 더한 꼴이 된 것 같았다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi đã mơ ước được sống ở Seoul suốt thời còn đi học và khi đỗ đại học, tôi đã được sống ở Seoul, nơi mình từng ao ước. Tôi đã phải chật vật vì thật khó để sống với số tiền ít ỏi kiếm được từ công việc bán thời gian theo giờ. Và đương nhiên, tiền đã trở thành chủ đề chính trong những cuộc nói chuyện giữa tôi và mẹ. "Ôi trời, nếu con học đại học ở đây thì chắc đã không cần phải lo lắng về tiền bạc như vậy. Con đến tận Seoul và rồi không thể học một cách thoải mái được. Xin lỗi vì mẹ đã không thể giúp con." <u>Mắt tôi rưng rưng</u> khi nghe những lời bà nói bà cảm thấy thật xót xa cho đứa con gái khổ sở của mình. Tôi đã từ bỏ học bổng toàn phần của một trường học địa phương và chọn lên Seoul để học đại học. Dường như lòng tôi chỉ thêm nặng trĩu mỗi lần ca cẩm về tiền bạc hay hoàn cảnh vốn đã khó khăn của gia đình mình với bố mẹ.
g_text_translate.en: I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.fr:
J'ai toujours rêvé de vivre à Séoul quand j'étais à l'école, et quand j'ai obtenu mon diplôme universitaire, j'ai pu vivre à Séoul, l'endroit dont j'ai toujours rêvé. J'ai lutté parce qu'il était difficile de vivre avec le peu d'argent que je gagnais grâce à mon travail horaire à temps partiel. Et bien sûr, l'argent est devenu le principal sujet de conversation entre moi et ma mère. "Oh mon dieu, si vous alliez à l'université ici, vous n'auriez pas à vous soucier de l'argent comme ça. Vous êtes venu à Séoul et vous ne pouviez pas étudier confortablement. Désolé de ne pas pouvoir vous aider." <u>Mes yeux se sont remplis de larmes</u> quand j'ai entendu ses paroles alors qu'elle se sentait tellement désolée pour sa misérable fille. J'ai renoncé à une bourse complète dans une école locale et j'ai choisi d'aller à Séoul pour fréquenter l'université. Cependant, il semblait qu'à chaque fois que je parlais d'argent, je ne faisais qu'ajouter un fardeau à mes parents qui étaient déjà dans des circonstances difficiles.

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.es:
Soñaba con vivir en Seúl todo el tiempo cuando estaba en la escuela, y cuando me gradué de la universidad, pude vivir en Seúl, el lugar con el que siempre soñé. Luché porque era difícil vivir con el poco dinero que ganaba con mi trabajo de medio tiempo por horas. Y, por supuesto, el dinero se convirtió en el principal tema de conversación entre mi madre y yo. "Oh, si fueras a la universidad aquí, no tendrías que preocuparte por dinero como ese. Viniste a Seúl y no pudiste estudiar cómodamente. Lo siento, no pude ayudarte". <u>Mis ojos se llenaron de lágrimas</u> cuando escuché sus palabras mientras sentía tanta pena por su miserable hija. Renuncié a una beca completa para una escuela local y elegí ir a Seúl para asistir a la universidad. Sin embargo, parecía que cada vez que hablaba de dinero, solo agregaba una carga a mis padres que ya estaban en circunstancias difíciles.

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.id:
Saya bermimpi tinggal di Seoul sepanjang waktu ketika saya masih sekolah, dan ketika saya lulus dari universitas, saya bisa tinggal di Seoul, tempat yang selalu saya impikan. Saya berjuang karena sulit untuk hidup dengan sedikit uang yang saya hasilkan dari pekerjaan paruh waktu saya. Dan tentu saja, uang menjadi topik pembicaraan utama antara saya dan ibu saya. "Ya ampun, jika kamu kuliah di sini, kamu tidak perlu khawatir tentang uang seperti itu. Kamu datang ke Seoul dan kamu tidak bisa belajar dengan nyaman. Maaf aku tidak bisa membantumu." <u>Mataku berlinang air mata</u> ketika aku mendengar kata-katanya saat dia merasa sangat kasihan pada putrinya yang menyedihkan. Saya menyerahkan beasiswa penuh ke sekolah lokal dan memilih pergi ke Seoul untuk kuliah. Namun, sepertinya setiap kali saya berbicara tentang uang, saya hanya menambah beban orang tua saya yang sudah dalam keadaan sulit.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
在学中からずっとソウルに住むことを夢見ていた私は、大学卒業後、ずっと夢見ていたソウルに住むことができました。時間給のアルバイトで得たわずかなお金で生活するのは大変でした。そしてもちろん、お金は私と母の間の会話の主な話題になりました。 「ああ、ここの大学に行けば、そんなお金の心配はいらないよ。ソウルに来て、快適に勉強できなかった。助けられなくてごめんね」 <u>私の目は涙でいっぱいになりました</u>。彼女が惨めな娘をとても気の毒に思った彼女の言葉を聞いたとき。私は地元の学校への全額奨学金をあきらめ、大学に通うためにソウルに行くことを選びました。しかし、お金の話をするたびに、すでに大変な状況にある両親の負担を増やすだけだったようです。

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.de:
Ich träumte die ganze Zeit davon, in Seoul zu leben, als ich in der Schule war, und als ich die Universität abschloss, konnte ich in Seoul leben, dem Ort, von dem ich immer geträumt hatte. Ich kämpfte, weil es schwer war, von dem wenigen Geld zu leben, das ich mit meinem Teilzeitjob verdiente. Und natürlich wurde Geld zum Hauptgesprächsthema zwischen mir und meiner Mutter. „Oh mein Gott, wenn du hier aufs College gehen würdest, müsstest du dir keine Sorgen um Geld machen. Du bist nach Seoul gekommen und konntest nicht bequem studieren. Tut mir leid, dass ich dir nicht helfen konnte.“ <u>Meine Augen füllten sich mit Tränen</u>, als ich ihre Worte hörte, als sie ihre elende Tochter so sehr bedauerte. Ich habe ein Vollstipendium für eine örtliche Schule aufgegeben und mich entschieden, nach Seoul zu gehen, um die Universität zu besuchen. Es schien jedoch, dass ich jedes Mal, wenn ich über Geld sprach, meine Eltern, die sich bereits in schwierigen Umständen befanden, nur zusätzlich belastete.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
上学的时候一直梦想住在首尔，大学毕业后，我就可以住在我一直梦想的首尔了。我很挣扎，因为我很难靠兼职小时工赚来的一点钱生活。当然，金钱成了我和妈妈之间的主要话题。 “哦，天哪，如果你在这里上大学，你就不用担心那种钱了。你来到首尔，学习也不是很舒服。对不起，我帮不了你。” <u>当我听到她的话时，我的眼睛充满了泪水</u>，因为她为她可怜的女儿感到非常抱歉。我放弃了当地学校的全额奖学金，选择去首尔上大学。然而，似乎每次谈钱，都只是给已经处境困难的父母增加了负担。

#######

Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. g_text_translate.it:
Ho sempre sognato di vivere a Seoul quando ero a scuola, e quando mi sono laureato all'università, ho potuto vivere a Seoul, il posto che ho sempre sognato. Ho lottato perché era difficile vivere con i pochi soldi che guadagnavo con il mio lavoro orario part-time. E, naturalmente, il denaro è diventato l'argomento principale della conversazione tra me e mia madre. "Oh cielo, se andassi al college qui, non dovresti preoccuparti di soldi del genere. Sei venuto a Seoul e non hai potuto studiare comodamente. Mi dispiace di non averti potuto aiutare." <u>I miei occhi si sono riempiti di lacrime</u> quando ho sentito le sue parole mentre si sentiva così dispiaciuta per la sua miserabile figlia. Ho rinunciato a una borsa di studio completa per una scuola locale e ho scelto di andare a Seoul per frequentare l'università. Tuttavia, sembrava che ogni volta che parlavo di soldi, aggiungevo solo un peso ai miei genitori che si trovavano già in circostanze difficili.

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
Я мечтал жить в Сеуле все время, когда учился в школе, а когда я окончил университет, я смог жить в Сеуле, месте, о котором я всегда мечтал. Я боролся, потому что было трудно прожить на те небольшие деньги, которые я зарабатывал на почасовой работе с частичной занятостью. И конечно, деньги стали главной темой разговора между мной и моей мамой. «О боже, если бы вы поступили в колледж здесь, вам бы не пришлось так беспокоиться о деньгах. Вы приехали в Сеул и не могли комфортно учиться. Извините, я не смог вам помочь». <u>Мои глаза наполнились слезами</u>, когда я услышал ее слова, когда ей было так жаль свою несчастную дочь. Я отказался от полной стипендии в местной школе и решил поехать в Сеул, чтобы поступить в университет. Однако казалось, что каждый раз, когда я говорил о деньгах, я только добавлял бремя своим родителям, которые и без того находились в трудных обстоятельствах.

#######

However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.th:
ฉันใฝ่ฝันที่จะใช้ชีวิตในกรุงโซลตลอดเวลาตอนที่ยังเรียนอยู่ และเมื่อเรียนจบมหาวิทยาลัย ฉันก็สามารถที่จะอาศัยอยู่ในกรุงโซล สถานที่ที่ฉันใฝ่ฝันมาตลอด ฉันต้องดิ้นรนเพราะมันยากที่จะใช้ชีวิตด้วยเงินเพียงเล็กน้อยที่ฉันได้มาจากงานพาร์ทไทม์รายชั่วโมง และแน่นอนว่าเงินกลายเป็นหัวข้อหลักของการสนทนาระหว่างฉันกับแม่ “โอ้ ถ้าคุณเรียนวิทยาลัยที่นี่ คุณจะไม่ต้องกังวลเรื่องเงินแบบนั้น คุณมาโซลแล้วเรียนได้ไม่สบาย ขอโทษด้วยที่ช่วยคุณไม่ได้” <u>ดวงตาของฉันเต็มไปด้วยน้ำตา</u> เมื่อฉันได้ยินคำพูดของเธอ ขณะที่เธอรู้สึกเสียใจอย่างมากต่อลูกสาวที่น่าสังเวชของเธอ ฉันมอบทุนการศึกษาเต็มจำนวนให้กับโรงเรียนในท้องถิ่นและเลือกที่จะไปโซลเพื่อเข้าเรียนมหาวิทยาลัย อย่างไรก็ตาม ดูเหมือนว่าทุกครั้งที่ฉันพูดถึงเรื่องเงิน ฉันมีแต่เพิ่มภาระให้พ่อแม่ที่กำลังตกอยู่ในสถานการณ์ที่ยากลำบากอยู่แล้ว

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.pt:
Sempre sonhei em morar em Seul quando estava na escola e, quando me formei na universidade, pude morar em Seul, o lugar que sempre sonhei. Lutei porque era difícil viver com o pouco dinheiro que ganhava com meu trabalho de meio período por hora. E, claro, o dinheiro se tornou o principal tema de conversa entre mim e minha mãe. "Nossa, se você fosse para a faculdade aqui, não teria que se preocupar com dinheiro assim. Você veio para Seul e não poderia estudar confortavelmente. Desculpe, não pude ajudá-lo." <u>Meus olhos se encheram de lágrimas</u> quando ouvi suas palavras enquanto ela sentia muita pena de sua filha miserável. Desisti de uma bolsa integral para estudar em uma escola local e optei por ir para Seul para cursar a universidade. No entanto, parecia que cada vez que falava sobre dinheiro, apenas acrescentava um fardo aos meus pais, que já se encontravam em circunstâncias difíceis.

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.hi:
जब मैं स्कूल में था, तब मैं हर समय सियोल में रहने का सपना देखता था, और जब मैंने विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक की उपाधि प्राप्त की, तो मैं सियोल में रहने में सक्षम हो गया, वह जगह जिसका मैं हमेशा सपना देखता था। मैंने संघर्ष किया क्योंकि अंशकालिक प्रति घंटे की नौकरी से मिलने वाले थोड़े से पैसे पर गुजारा करना कठिन था। और हां, पैसा मेरे और मेरी मां के बीच बातचीत का मुख्य विषय बन गया। "हे भगवान, यदि आप यहां कॉलेज जाते, तो आपको पैसे के बारे में इस तरह चिंता नहीं करनी पड़ती। आप सियोल आए और आप आराम से पढ़ाई नहीं कर सके। क्षमा करें, मैं आपकी मदद नहीं कर सका।" <u>मेरी आँखें भर आईं</u> जब मैंने उसकी बातें सुनीं क्योंकि उसे अपनी दुखी बेटी के लिए बहुत अफ़सोस हो रहा था। मैंने एक स्थानीय स्कूल की पूरी छात्रवृत्ति छोड़ दी और विश्वविद्यालय में दाखिला लेने के लिए सियोल जाने का फैसला किया। हालाँकि, ऐसा लगता था कि हर बार जब मैं पैसे के बारे में बात करता था, तो मैं केवल अपने माता-पिता पर बोझ डालता था जो पहले से ही कठिन परिस्थितियों में थे।

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.
g_text_translate.tr:
Okuldayken sürekli Seul'de yaşamanın hayalini kuruyordum ve üniversiteden mezun olduğumda hep hayalini kurduğum yer olan Seul'de yaşayabiliyordum. Mücadele ettim çünkü yarı zamanlı, saatlik işimden kazandığım azıcık parayla geçinmek zordu. Ve tabii ki annemle aramızdaki konuşmanın ana konusu para oldu. "Aman tanrım, eğer burada üniversiteye gitseydin, para konusunda bu kadar endişelenmene gerek kalmazdı. Seul'e geldin ve rahatça ders çalışamadın. Üzgünüm sana yardım edemedim." Sefil kızı için çok üzülürken sözlerini duyduğumda <u>gözlerim yaşlarla doldu</u>. Yerel bir okulun tam bursunu bıraktım ve üniversiteye gitmek için Seul'e gitmeyi seçtim. Ancak sanki ne zaman paradan bahsetsem, zaten zor durumda olan aileme sadece bir yük katıyordum.

#######

I dreamed of living in Seoul all the time when I was in school, and when I graduated from university, I was able to live in Seoul, the place I always dreamed of. I struggled because it was hard to live on the little money I made from my part-time hourly job. And of course, money became the main topic of conversation between me and my mother. "Oh my, if you went to college here, you wouldn't have to worry about money like that. You came to Seoul and you couldn't study comfortably. Sorry I couldn't help you." <u>My eyes filled with tears</u> when I heard her words as she felt so sorry for her miserable daughter. I gave up a full scholarship to a local school and chose to go to Seoul to attend university. However, it seemed that every time I talked about money, I only added a burden to my parents who were already in difficult circumstances.

Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn.

2. Tôi đã sống ở Seoul trước khi tôi vào đại học.
3. Bố mẹ gửi cho tôi rất nhiều tiền mặc dù hoàn cảnh gia đình tôi rất khó khăn.
4. Tôi đã từ bỏ học bổng để thực hiện ước mơ sống ở Seoul.

학창시절: Thời còn đi học

<g>포기하다:</g> To give up

<g>V/A + 았/었던:</g> Recall a past event that has not continued to the present.

general.g_text: 정신없이 세 아이를 키우면서 내가 미처 생각하지 못한 것이 있었다. 그것을 깨닫게 된 것은 얼마 전 세 딸을 목욕시키면서였다. 나는 늘 그랬듯이 씻기기 편한 막내부터 씻겨 욕실에서 내보냈고 그 다음에는 둘째를 씻겼다. 그리고 나서 첫째를 씻기려고 하는데 아이가 고개를 푹 숙인 채 앉아서 꼼짝도 하지 않았다. 내가 몇 번이나 좋은 말로 타이르자 그제야 ‘왜 내가 항상 마지막이야?’라고 울먹이며 말했다. <u>순간 머리를 한 대 얻어맞은 것 같았다</u>. 어린이집에 보내려고 옷을 입히고 머리를 빗겨 줄 때 항상 “동생들 하고 나서 해 줄게. ”라고 하며 첫째를 기다리게 했던 나의 모습이 떠올랐다.

g_text_translate.vi: Có một điều mà tôi đã không nghĩ ra khi bận tối tăm mặt mũi nuôi ba đứa con. Và cách đây không lâu, tôi đã nhận ra điều đó khi tắm cho ba đứa con gái. Như mọi khi, tôi tắm cho đứa con út, người dễ tắm rửa cho nhất rồi đưa đứa trẻ ra khỏi phòng tắm và đến đứa thứ hai. Sau đó, tôi chuẩn bị tắm cho đứa con đầu tiên nhưng đứa bé cứ ngồi cúi gục đầu xuống mà không nhúc nhích. Tôi dỗ ngọt mấy lần nhưng đến lúc đó nó lại mếu máo vào nói "Tại sao con luôn là người cuối cùng". <u>Tôi cảm thấy như bị đánh một cái vào đầu trong khoảnh khắc đó</u>. Khi tôi mặc quần áo và chải tóc cho đứa bé để gửi đến nhà trẻ, tôi luôn nói "Mẹ làm cho các em xong rồi sẽ tới lượt con", và tôi nhớ lại hình ảnh mình đã bắt đứa con đầu tiên của mình phải chờ đợi.
g_text_translate.en: There was something I hadn't thought of while frantically raising three children. I realized that while bathing my three daughters not long ago. As usual, I bathed the youngest, who was easy to bathe, and sent the youngest out of the bathroom, and then I bathed the second. After that, I was about to give my eldest baby a bath, but the baby just sat with her head down and wouldn't budge. I coaxed her a few times, but at that point she said, "Why am I always the last?" <u>I felt like I was hit in the head in that moment</u>. I recall the image of me forcing my eldest child to wait by saying "I will do it for your sisters first and then do it for you later." when I dressed and combed their hair to send them to the daycare center.
g_text_translate.fr:
Il y avait quelque chose à laquelle je n'avais pas pensé en élevant frénétiquement trois enfants. Je m'en suis rendu compte en baignant mes trois filles il n'y a pas si longtemps. Comme d'habitude, j'ai baigné le plus jeune, qui était facile à baigner, et j'ai renvoyé le plus jeune de la salle de bain, puis j'ai baigné le second. Après cela, j'étais sur le point de donner un bain à mon bébé aîné, mais le bébé était juste assis, la tête baissée et ne bougeait pas. Je l'ai persuadée plusieurs fois, mais à ce moment-là, elle a dit : "Pourquoi suis-je toujours la dernière ?" <u>J'ai eu l'impression d'avoir été frappé à la tête à ce moment-là</u>. Je me souviens de l'image de moi forçant mon enfant aîné à attendre en disant "Je le ferai pour tes sœurs d'abord et je le ferai pour toi plus tard." quand je les ai habillés et peignés pour les envoyer à la garderie.

#######

There was something I hadn't thought of while frantically raising three children. I realized that while bathing my three daughters not long ago. As usual, I bathed the youngest, who was easy to bathe, and sent the youngest out of the bathroom, and then I bathed the second. After that, I was about to give my eldest baby a bath, but the baby just sat with her head down and wouldn't budge. I coaxed her a few times, but at that point she said, "Why am I always the last?" <u>I felt like I was hit in the head in that moment</u>. I recall the image of me forcing my eldest child to wait by saying "I will do it for your sisters first and then do it for you later." when I dressed and combed their hair to send them to the daycare center.
g_text_translate.es:
Había algo en lo que no había pensado mientras criaba frenéticamente a tres niños. Me di cuenta de eso mientras bañaba a mis tres hijas no hace mucho. Como de costumbre, bañé al más pequeño, que era fácil de bañar, y saqué del baño al más pequeño, y luego bañé al segundo. Después de eso, estaba a punto de bañar a mi bebé mayor, pero el bebé se quedó sentado con la cabeza hacia abajo y no se movía. La convencí un par de veces, pero en ese momento ella dijo: "¿Por qué siempre soy la última?" <u>Sentí como si me hubieran golpeado en la cabeza en ese momento</u>. Recuerdo la imagen de mí obligando a mi hijo mayor a esperar diciendo: "Lo haré por tus hermanas primero y luego lo haré por ti". cuando los vestía y peinaba para enviarlos a la guardería.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Ada sesuatu yang tidak saya pikirkan saat membesarkan tiga anak dengan panik. Saya menyadari itu saat memandikan ketiga putri saya belum lama ini. Seperti biasa, saya memandikan si bungsu, yang mudah dimandikan, dan menyuruh si bungsu keluar dari kamar mandi, lalu saya memandikan si bungsu. Setelah itu, saya akan memandikan bayi tertua saya, tetapi bayi itu hanya duduk dengan kepala tertunduk dan tidak mau bergerak. Saya membujuknya beberapa kali, tetapi pada saat itu dia berkata, "Mengapa saya selalu menjadi yang terakhir?" <u>Saya merasa seperti dipukul di kepala pada saat itu</u>. Saya ingat gambar saya memaksa anak tertua saya untuk menunggu dengan mengatakan "Saya akan melakukannya untuk saudara perempuan Anda terlebih dahulu dan kemudian melakukannya untuk Anda nanti." ketika saya berpakaian dan menyisir rambut mereka untuk mengirim mereka ke pusat penitipan anak.

g_text_translate.ja:
3人の子供を必死に育てながら、思いもよらなかったことがありました。つい先日、3 人の娘をお風呂に入れているときに気づきました。いつものように、入りやすい末っ子を風呂に入れ、末っ子を浴室から送り出し、次に二人目を入浴させた。その後、長男をお風呂に入れようとしたが、頭を下げて座っているだけで動こうとしなかった。私は何度か彼女をなだめましたが、その時点で彼女は「どうして私がいつも最後なの?」と言いました。 <u>その瞬間、頭を殴られたような気がしました</u>。長子を「先にお姉さんたちにしてから、あとでやります」と待たせていたイメージを思い出します。保育園に送るために服を着せて髪をとかしたとき。

g_text_translate.de:
Es gab etwas, an das ich nicht gedacht hatte, während ich hektisch drei Kinder großzog. Das ist mir vor nicht allzu langer Zeit beim Baden meiner drei Töchter aufgefallen. Wie üblich badete ich den Jüngsten, der leicht zu baden war, und schickte den Jüngsten aus dem Badezimmer, und dann badete ich den Zweiten. Danach wollte ich mein ältestes Baby baden, aber das Baby saß nur mit gesenktem Kopf da und rührte sich nicht. Ich habe sie ein paar Mal überredet, aber an diesem Punkt sagte sie: "Warum bin ich immer die Letzte?" <u>Ich fühlte mich in diesem Moment, als hätte ich einen Schlag auf den Kopf bekommen</u>. Ich erinnere mich an das Bild, wie ich mein ältestes Kind zum Warten zwang, indem ich sagte: "Ich werde es zuerst für deine Schwestern tun und dann später für dich." als ich sie angezogen und gekämmt habe, um sie in die Kita zu schicken.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在疯狂抚养三个孩子的过程中，有些事情是我没有想到的。不久前，我在给三个女儿洗澡时意识到了这一点。像往常一样，我给最容易洗澡的最小的那个洗澡，然后把最小的从浴室里送出来，然后我给第二个洗澡。之后，我正要给大宝宝洗澡，宝宝却只是低着头坐着，一动不动。我哄了她几次，但她说：“为什么我总是最后一个？” <u>那一刻我感觉自己的头部被击中了</u>。我记得我强迫我的大孩子等待的形象，说“我会先为你的姐妹做，然后再为你做”。当我穿好衣服并梳理他们的头发并将他们送到日托中心时。

在瘋狂撫養三個孩子的過程中，有些事情是我沒有想到的。不久前，我在給三個女兒洗澡時意識到了這一點。像往常一樣，我給最容易洗澡的最小的那個洗澡，然後把最小的從浴室里送出來，然後我給第二個洗澡。之後，我正要給大寶寶洗澡，寶寶卻只是低著頭坐著，一動不動。我哄了她幾次，但她說：“為什麼我總是最後一個？” <u>那一刻我感覺自己的頭部被擊中了</u>。我記得我強迫我的大孩子等待的形象，說“我會先為你的姐妹做，然後再為你做”。當我穿好衣服並梳理他們的頭髮並將他們送到日託中心時。

g_text_translate.it:
C'era qualcosa a cui non avevo pensato mentre crescevo freneticamente tre figli. Me ne sono reso conto mentre facevo il bagno alle mie tre figlie non molto tempo fa. Come al solito, ho fatto il bagno al più giovane, che era facile da fare, e ho mandato il più giovane fuori dal bagno, e poi ho fatto il bagno al secondo. Dopodiché, stavo per fare il bagno al mio bambino più grande, ma il bambino si è seduto con la testa bassa e non si è mosso. L'ho persuasa un paio di volte, ma a quel punto lei ha detto: "Perché sono sempre l'ultima?" <u>In quel momento mi sono sentito come se fossi stato colpito alla testa</u>. Ricordo l'immagine di me che costringo il mio figlio maggiore ad aspettare dicendo "Lo farò prima per le tue sorelle e poi lo farò per te più tardi". quando li vestivo e li pettinavo per mandarli all'asilo nido.

g_text_translate.ru:
Было кое-что, о чем я не подумал, лихорадочно воспитывая троих детей. Я понял это не так давно, когда купал трех своих дочерей. Как обычно, я купала младшего, которого было легко купать, и отправила младшего из ванной, а затем искупала второго. После этого я собиралась купать старшего ребенка, но ребенок просто сидел, опустив голову, и не двигался с места. Я несколько раз уговаривал ее, но в этот момент она сказала: «Почему я всегда последняя?» <u>В тот момент у меня было ощущение, будто меня ударили по голове</u>. Я вспоминаю, как я заставлял своего старшего ребенка ждать, говоря: «Сначала я сделаю это для твоих сестер, а потом сделаю это для тебя». когда я одевал и причесывал их, чтобы отправить в детский сад.

g_text_translate.th:
มีบางอย่างที่ฉันไม่เคยคิดมาก่อนในขณะที่เลี้ยงลูกสามคนอย่างเมามัน ฉันตระหนักว่าในขณะที่อาบน้ำลูกสาวทั้งสามของฉันเมื่อไม่นานมานี้ เหมือนเช่นเคย ฉันอาบน้ำให้น้องคนเล็กที่อาบง่าย แล้วส่งน้องเล็กออกจากห้องน้ำ และจากนั้นก็อาบน้ำคนที่สอง หลังจากนั้นฉันก็กำลังจะอาบน้ำให้ลูกคนโต แต่ลูกน้อยกลับนั่งก้มหน้าไม่ยอมขยับเขยื่อน ฉันเกลี้ยกล่อมเธอสองสามครั้ง แต่เมื่อถึงจุดนั้นเธอก็พูดว่า "ทำไมฉันถึงเป็นคนสุดท้ายเสมอ" <u>ฉันรู้สึกเหมือนถูกตีที่หัวในขณะนั้น</u> ฉันจำภาพที่ฉันบังคับให้ลูกคนโตรอโดยพูดว่า "ฉันจะทำเพื่อน้องสาวของคุณก่อนแล้วค่อยทำเพื่อคุณทีหลัง" เมื่อฉันแต่งตัวและหวีผมเพื่อส่งพวกเขาไปที่ศูนย์รับเลี้ยงเด็ก

g_text_translate.pt:
Havia algo em que não pensei enquanto criava freneticamente três filhos. Percebi isso enquanto dava banho em minhas três filhas, não faz muito tempo. Como de costume, dei banho no mais novo, que era fácil de dar banho, e mandei o mais novo sair do banheiro, e depois dei banho no segundo. Depois disso, eu estava prestes a dar banho no meu bebê mais velho, mas o bebê ficou sentado com a cabeça baixa e não se movia. Eu a persuadi algumas vezes, mas naquele momento ela disse: "Por que sou sempre a última?" <u>Eu senti como se tivesse levado um golpe na cabeça naquele momento</u>. Lembro-me da imagem de mim forçando meu filho mais velho a esperar, dizendo: "Farei isso primeiro pelas suas irmãs e depois farei isso por você". quando eu vestia e penteava seus cabelos para mandá-los para a creche.

g_text_translate.hi:
ऐसा कुछ था जिसके बारे में मैंने तीन बच्चों का पालन-पोषण करते समय नहीं सोचा था। कुछ समय पहले ही मुझे अपनी तीन बेटियों को नहलाते समय इसका एहसास हुआ। हमेशा की तरह, मैंने सबसे छोटे को नहलाया, जिसे नहलाना आसान था, और सबसे छोटे को बाथरूम से बाहर भेज दिया, और फिर मैंने दूसरे को नहलाया। उसके बाद, मैं अपने सबसे बड़े बच्चे को नहलाने वाली थी, लेकिन बच्चा अपना सिर नीचे करके बैठ गया और हिल नहीं रहा था। मैंने उसे कई बार मनाया, लेकिन उस समय उसने कहा, "मैं हमेशा आखिरी क्यों होती हूं?" <u>उस पल मुझे ऐसा लगा जैसे मेरे सिर पर चोट लगी है</u>। मुझे अपने सबसे बड़े बच्चे को यह कहकर प्रतीक्षा करने के लिए मजबूर करने की छवि याद आती है कि "मैं इसे पहले तुम्हारी बहनों के लिए करूंगा और फिर बाद में तुम्हारे लिए करूंगा।" जब मैंने उन्हें डेकेयर सेंटर में भेजने के लिए कपड़े पहने और उनके बालों में कंघी की।

After that, I was about to give my eldest baby a bath, but the baby just sat with her head down and wouldn't budge. I coaxed her a few times, but at that point she said, "Why am I always the last?" <u>I felt like I was hit in the head in that moment</u>. I recall the image of me forcing my eldest child to wait by saying "I will do it for your sisters first and then do it for you later." when I dressed and combed their hair to send them to the daycare center.
g_text_translate.tr:
Çılgınca üç çocuğu büyütürken düşünmediğim bir şey vardı. Bunu kısa bir süre önce üç kızıma banyo yaptırırken fark ettim. Her zamanki gibi, yıkanması kolay olan en küçüğünü yıkadım ve en küçüğünü banyodan gönderdim, sonra ikincisini yıkadım. Ondan sonra en büyük bebeğime banyo yaptırmak üzereydim ama bebek başı aşağıda oturdu ve kıpırdamadı. Onu birkaç kez ikna ettim ama o noktada şöyle dedi: "Neden hep sonuncu oluyorum?" <u>O anda kafama vurulmuş gibi hissettim</u>. En büyük çocuğumu "Önce kız kardeşlerin için yapacağım, sonra senin için yapacağım" diyerek beklemeye zorladığım imajı hatırlıyorum. Onları kreşe göndermek için giydirip saçlarını taradığımda.

#######

There was something I hadn't thought of while frantically raising three children. I realized that while bathing my three daughters not long ago. As usual, I bathed the youngest, who was easy to bathe, and sent the youngest out of the bathroom, and then I bathed the second. After that, I was about to give my eldest baby a bath, but the baby just sat with her head down and wouldn't budge. I coaxed her a few times, but at that point she said, "Why am I always the last?" <u>I felt like I was hit in the head in that moment</u>. I recall the image of me forcing my eldest child to wait by saying "I will do it for your sisters first and then do it for you later." when I dressed and combed their hair to send them to the daycare center.

4. I was angry because my eldest child didn't want to take a bath.
--------------------
정신없이: Frantically, hectically
양보하다: To yield
꼼짝하다: To wriggle, to wiggle
explain.tr:
Bu paragrafın içeriğine uygun cevabı seçin.
1. En büyük çocuğumdan başlayarak kızlarımı yıkamayı planlıyorum.
2. En büyük çocuk her zaman küçük kardeşlerine boyun eğmek zorundadır.
3. En büyük çocuk sıklıkla duygularını ifade eder.
4. Büyük çocuğum banyo yapmak istemediği için kızıyordum.
--------------------
정신없이: Çılgınca, telaşlı bir şekilde
양보하다: Teslim olmak
꼼짝하다: Kıpırdamak, kıpırdamak

#######

Choose the answer that matches the content of this paragraph.
1. I plan to bathe my daughters starting with the eldest child.
2. The eldest child always has to yield to her younger siblings.
3. The eldest child often expresses  feelings.
4. I was angry because my eldest child didn't want to take a bath.
--------------------
정신없이: Frantically, hectically
양보하다: To yield
꼼짝하다: To wriggle, to wiggle

general.g_text: 할머니를 시골에 두고 혼자 서울로 올라오는 발걸음은 가볍지 않았다. 하지만 무거웠던 마음은 며칠 가지 않았다. 할머니는 날마다 전화를 하더니 급기야 서울로 올라오시고 말았다. 할머니의 손자 사랑은 어쩔 수 없나 보다. 할머니는 청소며 빨래며 나에게는 안 보이던 온갖 집안일들을 찾아서 하기 시작했다. 그냥 쉬다가 내려가시라고 <u>아무리 말해도 들은 척도 하지 않았다</u>. 서른이 넘은 나는 할머니가 보기엔 여전히 아이에 불과했다. 서울살이 몇 주 만에 낯선 동네에서 친구까지 사귄 할머니는 친구를 따라 시장에 갔다가 넘어지시고 말았다. 병원에서 온 연락을 받고 걱정이 되어 정신없이 달려갔더니 할머니는 같은 병실 사람들을 모아 놓고 환하게 웃으며 이야기하고 있었다. 다리에 붕대를 감고서 말이다. 그 광경을 보고 난 할 말을 잃었다.

g_text_translate.vi: Để bà ở quê và lên lên Seoul một mình thật không dễ dàng gì. Nhưng tâm trạng nặng trĩu này không kéo dài được mấy ngày. Bà tôi gọi cho tôi mỗi ngày và cuối cùng thì bà cũng đã lên Seoul. Thật không còn cách nào khác vì tình yêu của bà dành cho cháu trai. Bà tôi bắt đầu dọn dẹp, giặt giũ và tìm ra đủ thứ những việc nhà mà tôi không nhìn thấy. Tôi bảo bà cứ nằm xuống nghỉ ngơi đi nhưng <u>dù tôi nói gì thì bà vẫn vờ như không nghe thấy</u>. Mặc dù đã quá ba mươi tuổi nhưng trong mắt bà, tôi vẫn chỉ là một đứa trẻ. Bà tôi mới chỉ ở Seoul vài tuần thôi nhưng đã kết bạn được với những người hàng xóm trong khu phố xa lạ này. Bà theo bạn đi chợ và bị ngã. Khi nhận được điện thoại từ bệnh viện, tôi vô cùng lo lắng cắm đầu cắm cổ chạy tới bệnh viện thì thấy bà đang tụ tập và tươi cười nói chuyện với những người chung phòng bệnh. Miếng băng gạc vẫn được băng trên chân bà. Tôi không thốt nên lời khu nhìn thấy cảnh tượng đó.
g_text_translate.en: It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.fr:
Ce n'était pas facile de laisser ma grand-mère à la campagne et de monter seule à Séoul. Mais cette humeur pesante ne dura pas quelques jours. Ma grand-mère m'appelait tous les jours et elle est finalement venue à Séoul. Il semble que l'amour de ma grand-mère pour son petit-fils soit irrésistible. Ma grand-mère a commencé à nettoyer, à faire la lessive et à s'occuper de toutes sortes de tâches ménagères que je ne pouvais pas voir. Je lui ai dit de s'allonger et de se reposer mais <u>peu importe ce que je disais, elle a fait semblant de ne pas entendre</u>. Même si j'ai plus de trente ans, à ses yeux, je ne suis encore qu'un enfant. Ma grand-mère n'est à Séoul que depuis quelques semaines, mais s'est déjà fait des amis dans ce quartier inconnu. Elle a suivi son amie au marché et est tombée. Lorsque j'ai reçu un appel téléphonique de l'hôpital, j'étais extrêmement inquiète et j'ai couru à l'hôpital pour la trouver rassemblant les gens dans la même pièce qu'elle et parlant avec un sourire éclatant. Le bandage était toujours sur sa jambe. La scène m'a laissé sans voix.

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.es:
No fue fácil dejar a mi abuela en el campo e ir sola a Seúl. Pero este estado de ánimo pesado no duró unos días. Mi abuela me llamaba todos los días y finalmente vino a Seúl. Parece que el amor de mi abuela por su nieto es irresistible. Mi abuela comenzó a limpiar, lavar la ropa y resolver todo tipo de tareas domésticas que yo no podía ver. Le dije que se acostara y descansara pero <u>sin importar lo que dijera ella fingió no escuchar</u>. A pesar de que tengo más de treinta años, a sus ojos, sigo siendo solo un niño. Mi abuela solo ha estado en Seúl por unas pocas semanas, pero ya ha hecho amigos en este vecindario desconocido. Siguió a su amiga al mercado y se cayó. Cuando recibí una llamada telefónica del hospital, estaba extremadamente preocupada y corrí al hospital para encontrarla reunida a las personas en la misma habitación que ella y estaba hablando con una sonrisa brillante. El vendaje todavía estaba en su pierna. La escena me dejó sin palabras.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Tidak mudah meninggalkan nenekku di pedesaan dan pergi ke Seoul sendirian. Tapi suasana hati yang berat ini tidak berlangsung selama beberapa hari. Nenek saya menelepon saya setiap hari dan dia akhirnya datang ke Seoul. Tampaknya cinta nenek saya untuk cucunya tak tertahankan. Nenek saya mulai membersihkan, mencuci pakaian, dan mencari tahu segala macam pekerjaan rumah tangga yang tidak bisa saya lihat. Aku menyuruhnya berbaring dan beristirahat tapi <u>tidak peduli apa yang kukatakan, dia pura-pura tidak mendengar</u>. Meskipun saya berusia lebih dari tiga puluh tahun, di matanya, saya masih anak-anak. Nenek saya baru berada di Seoul selama beberapa minggu, tetapi sudah berteman di lingkungan yang tidak dikenal ini. Dia mengikuti temannya ke pasar dan jatuh. Ketika saya menerima panggilan telepon dari rumah sakit, saya sangat khawatir dan berlari ke rumah sakit untuk menemukannya mengumpulkan orang-orang di ruangan yang sama dengannya dan berbicara dengan senyum cerah. Perban masih menempel di kakinya. Pemandangan itu membuatku tak bisa berkata-kata.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
祖母を田舎に残して一人でソウルに上がるのは簡単なことではありませんでした。しかし、この重い気分は数日続きませんでした。祖母は毎日私に電話をかけ、やがてソウルに来ました。祖母の孫への愛情はたまらないようです。祖母は掃除を始め、洗濯をし、私には見えないあらゆる種類の家事を考え出しました。私は彼女に横になって休むように言いましたが、<u>私が何を言っても彼女は聞こえないふりをしました</u>。 30歳を超えた私でも、彼女の目には私はまだまだ子供です。私の祖母はソウルに来て数週間しか経っていませんが、このなじみのない地域ですでに友達を作っています。彼女は友達を追って市場に行き、転んだ。病院から電話がかかってきて、とても心配になって病院に行くと、同じ部屋に人を集めて明るい笑顔で話していた。包帯はまだ彼女の足にありました。その光景は言葉を失いました。

#######

My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. g_text_translate.de:
Es war nicht einfach, meine Großmutter auf dem Land zu lassen und allein nach Seoul zu gehen. Aber diese schwere Stimmung hielt nicht ein paar Tage an. Meine Großmutter rief mich jeden Tag an und sie kam schließlich nach Seoul. Es scheint, dass die Liebe meiner Großmutter zu ihrem Enkel unwiderstehlich ist. Meine Großmutter fing an zu putzen, Wäsche zu waschen und alle möglichen Hausarbeiten zu erledigen, die ich nicht sehen konnte. Ich sagte ihr, sie solle sich hinlegen und ausruhen, aber <u>egal was ich sagte, sie tat so, als würde sie es nicht hören</u>. Obwohl ich über dreißig Jahre alt bin, bin ich in ihren Augen immer noch ein Kind. Meine Großmutter ist erst seit ein paar Wochen in Seoul, hat aber in dieser fremden Gegend schon Freunde gefunden. Sie folgte ihrer Freundin zum Markt und stürzte. Als ich einen Anruf aus dem Krankenhaus erhielt, war ich sehr besorgt und rannte zum Krankenhaus, um zu sehen, dass sie die Leute im selben Raum wie sie versammelte und mit einem strahlenden Lächeln sprach. Der Verband war noch an ihrem Bein. Die Szene machte mich sprachlos.

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
把祖母留在乡下，一个人去首尔并不容易。但这种沉重的心情并没有持续几天。我的祖母每天都给我打电话，她最终来到了首尔。看来外婆对孙子的爱是无法抗拒的。我的祖母开始打扫卫生，洗衣服，做各种我看不到的家务。我让她躺下休息，但<u>不管我说什么她都假装没听见</u>。虽然我已经三十多岁了，但在她眼里，我还是个孩子。我的祖母才来首尔几个星期，但已经在这个陌生的街区结交了朋友。她跟着她的朋友到市场上摔倒了。接到医院打来的电话，我非常着急，跑到医院发现她把人都聚集在同一个房间里，笑眯眯的说话。绷带还在她腿上。场面让我无语。

#######

把祖母留在鄉下，一個人去首爾並不容易。但這種沉重的心情並沒有持續幾天。我的祖母每天都給我打電話，她最終來到了首爾。看來外婆對孫子的愛是無法抗拒的。我的祖母開始打掃衛生，洗衣服，做各種我看不到的家務。我讓她躺下休息，但<u>不管我說什麼她都假裝沒聽見</u>。雖然我已經三十多歲了，但在她眼裡，我還是個孩子。我的祖母才來首爾幾個星期，但已經在這個陌生的街區結交了朋友。她跟著她的朋友到市場上摔倒了。接到醫院打來的電話，我非常著急，跑到醫院發現她把人都聚集在同一個房間裡，笑瞇瞇的說話。繃帶還在她腿上。場面讓我無語。

g_text_translate.it:
Non è stato facile lasciare mia nonna in campagna e salire a Seul da sola. Ma questo stato d'animo pesante non durò per alcuni giorni. Mia nonna mi chiamava tutti i giorni e alla fine è venuta a Seoul. Sembra che l'amore di mia nonna per suo nipote sia irresistibile. Mia nonna ha iniziato a pulire, fare il bucato e capire ogni sorta di faccende domestiche che non riuscivo a vedere. Le ho detto di sdraiarsi e riposare, ma <u>non importa quello che ho detto lei ha fatto finta di non sentire</u>. Anche se ho più di trent'anni, ai suoi occhi sono ancora solo una bambina. Mia nonna è a Seoul solo da poche settimane, ma ha già fatto amicizia in questo quartiere sconosciuto. Ha seguito la sua amica al mercato ed è caduta. Quando ho ricevuto una telefonata dall'ospedale, ero estremamente preoccupato e sono corso in ospedale per trovarla riunita nella sua stessa stanza e parlava con un sorriso smagliante. La benda era ancora sulla sua gamba. La scena mi ha lasciato senza parole.

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
Было нелегко оставить бабушку в деревне и поехать одной в Сеул. Но это тяжелое настроение не продлилось и нескольких дней. Моя бабушка звонила мне каждый день и в конце концов приехала в Сеул. Кажется, любовь моей бабушки к внуку неодолима. Моя бабушка начала убираться, стирать и заниматься всякими домашними делами, которых я не мог видеть. Я сказал ей лечь и отдохнуть, но <u>что бы я ни говорил, она делала вид, что не слышит</u>. Хоть мне и больше тридцати лет, в ее глазах я все еще ребенок. Моя бабушка приехала в Сеул всего несколько недель, но уже обзавелась друзьями в этом незнакомом районе. Она последовала за подругой на рынок и упала. Когда мне позвонили из больницы, я очень волновалась и побежала в больницу и обнаружила, что она собрала людей в одной комнате с собой и разговаривала с яркой улыбкой. Повязка все еще была на ее ноге. Эта сцена лишила меня дара речи.

The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.th:
มันไม่ง่ายเลยที่จะทิ้งคุณย่าไว้ที่ชนบทและขึ้นไปโซลตามลำพัง แต่อารมณ์ที่หนักหน่วงนี้ไม่ได้คงอยู่สองสามวัน คุณยายโทรหาฉันทุกวันและในที่สุดเธอก็มาถึงโซล ดูเหมือนว่าความรักที่ยายของฉันมีต่อหลานชายของเธอนั้นไม่อาจต้านทานได้ คุณยายของฉันเริ่มทำความสะอาด ซักผ้า และทำงานบ้านทุกประเภทที่ฉันมองไม่เห็น ฉันบอกให้เธอนอนพักผ่อน แต่ <u>ไม่ว่าฉันพูดอะไร เธอก็แสร้งทำเป็นไม่ได้ยิน</u> แม้ว่าฉันจะอายุเกินสามสิบปีแล้ว แต่ในสายตาของเธอ ฉันยังเป็นเพียงเด็ก คุณยายของฉันเพิ่งไปโซลได้ไม่กี่สัปดาห์ แต่ได้รู้จักเพื่อนในย่านที่ไม่คุ้นเคยนี้แล้ว เธอตามเพื่อนของเธอไปตลาดแล้วล้มลง เมื่อได้รับโทรศัพท์จากโรงพยาบาล ฉันก็กังวลมากจึงรีบวิ่งไปโรงพยาบาลพบว่าเธอรวบรวมคนอยู่ห้องเดียวกับเธอและพูดคุยด้วยรอยยิ้มสดใส ผ้าพันแผลยังคงอยู่ที่ขาของเธอ ฉากนั้นทำให้ฉันพูดไม่ออก

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.pt:
Não foi fácil deixar minha avó no campo e ir sozinha para Seul. Mas esse clima pesado não durou alguns dias. Minha avó me ligava todos os dias e finalmente veio para Seul. Parece que o amor da minha avó pelo neto é irresistível. Minha avó começou a limpar, lavar roupa e resolver todo tipo de tarefas domésticas que eu não conseguia ver. Eu disse a ela para se deitar e descansar, mas <u>não importa o que eu dissesse, ela fingiu não ouvir</u>. Embora eu tenha mais de trinta anos, aos olhos dela, ainda sou apenas uma criança. Minha avó está em Seul há apenas algumas semanas, mas já fez amigos neste bairro desconhecido. Ela seguiu a amiga até o mercado e caiu. Quando recebi um telefonema do hospital, fiquei extremamente preocupado e corri para o hospital para encontrá-la reunida com as pessoas na mesma sala que ela e conversando com um sorriso brilhante. A bandagem ainda estava em sua perna. A cena me deixou sem palavras.

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.hi:
अपनी दादी को ग्रामीण इलाकों में छोड़कर अकेले सियोल तक जाना आसान नहीं था। लेकिन ये भारी मूड कुछ दिनों तक नहीं रहा. मेरी दादी मुझे हर दिन बुलाती थीं और आख़िरकार वह सियोल आ गईं। ऐसा लगता है कि मेरी दादी का अपने पोते के प्रति प्यार अनूठा है। मेरी दादी ने सफ़ाई करना, कपड़े धोना और घर के सभी प्रकार के काम करना शुरू कर दिया जो मैं नहीं देख सकती थी। मैंने उसे लेटने और आराम करने के लिए कहा, लेकिन <u>चाहे मैंने कुछ भी कहा हो, उसने न सुनने का नाटक किया</u>। भले ही मेरी उम्र तीस साल से अधिक है, लेकिन उसकी नज़र में मैं अभी भी एक बच्चा ही हूँ। मेरी दादी को सियोल में केवल कुछ सप्ताह ही हुए हैं, लेकिन उन्होंने पहले ही इस अपरिचित पड़ोस में दोस्त बना लिए हैं। वह अपने दोस्त के पीछे-पीछे बाजार तक गई और गिर गई। जब मुझे अस्पताल से फोन आया, तो मैं बेहद चिंतित हो गया और अस्पताल की ओर भागा और देखा कि वह उसी कमरे में लोगों को इकट्ठा कर रही थी और उज्ज्वल मुस्कान के साथ बात कर रही थी। उसके पैर पर अभी भी पट्टी बंधी हुई थी. इस दृश्य ने मुझे निःशब्द कर दिया।

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
g_text_translate.tr:
Büyükannemi kırsalda bırakıp Seul'e tek başıma gitmek kolay olmadı. Ancak bu ağır ruh hali birkaç gün sürmedi. Büyükannem her gün beni aradı ve sonunda Seul'e geldi. Görünüşe göre büyükannemin torununa olan sevgisi karşı konulmaz. Büyükannem temizlik yapmaya, çamaşır yıkamaya ve benim göremediğim her türlü ev işini halletmeye başladı. Ona uzanıp dinlenmesini söyledim ama <u>ne söylersem söyleyeyim duymuyormuş gibi yaptı</u>. Otuz yaşımı geçmiş olmama rağmen onun gözünde hâlâ bir çocuğum. Büyükannem sadece birkaç haftadır Seul'deydi ama bu yabancı mahallede şimdiden arkadaşlar edinmişti. Arkadaşını markete kadar takip etti ve düştü. Hastaneden bir telefon aldığımda son derece endişelendim ve hastaneye koştum ve onu insanları kendisiyle aynı odada toplamış ve parlak bir gülümsemeyle konuşurken buldum. Bandaj hâlâ bacağındaydı. Bu sahne beni suskun bıraktı.

#######

It was not easy to leave my grandmother in the countryside and go up to Seoul alone. But this heavy mood did not last for a few days. My grandmother called me every day and she eventually came to Seoul. It seems that my grandmother's love for her grandson is irresistible. My grandmother started cleaning, doing laundry and figuring out all sorts of household chores that I couldn't see. I told her to lie down and rest but <u>no matter what I said she pretended not to hear</u>. Even though I'm over thirty years old, in her eyes, I'm still just a child. My grandmother has only been in Seoul for a few weeks, but has already made friends in this unfamiliar neighborhood. She followed her friend to the market and fell. When I received a phone call from the hospital, I was extremely worried and ran to the hospital to find her gathered the people in the same room as her and was talking with a bright smile. The bandage was still on her leg. The scene made me speechless.
