general.g_text: 나는 매일 지하철로 등교한다. 지하철을 타고 가다 보면 여러 사람들의 다양한 모습을 보게 된다. 그런데 일주일에 서너 번 <u>눈살을 찌푸리게 만드는 광경을 본다</u>. 젊은 사람들이 자신의 자리인 양 '노약자석’에 앉아 신문이나 잡지를 읽고 있고, 노약자들은 그 앞에서 비를 맞은 나무처럼 힘겹게 서 있는 모습이다. 그런 광경을 볼 때마다 나는 얼굴이 화끈거린다. 오늘도 지하철에 앉아 신문과 잡지로 세상을 읽는 당신, 당신이 무심코 앉은 자리가 혹시 노약자나 장애인을 위한 자리는 아닌지 확인해 보라. 노약자석은 우리 이웃을 위한 최소한의 배려이다. 신문이나 잡지로 세상을 보기 전에 주변을 먼저 보는 마음을 가지는 것이 어떨까?

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi đến trường bằng tàu điện ngầm mỗi ngày. Khi đi trên tàu điện ngầm, tôi nhìn thấy những dáng vẻ đa dạng của những người khác nhau. Nhưng ba hoặc bốn lần một tuần, <u>tôi nhìn thấy một cảnh tượng khiến tôi phải cau mày</u>. Những người trẻ tuổi đang đọc báo và tạp chí trong khi ngồi ở 'ghế dành cho người già và người yếu thế' như thể họ là ghế của chính họ, và những người già và yếu thế đang đứng trước mặt họ chật vật như cây trong mưa. Mỗi lần nhìn thấy cảnh tượng như vậy, mặt tôi lại nóng lên. Hôm nay, khi bạn ngồi trên tàu điện ngầm và đọc thế giới qua báo và tạp chí, hãy cũng kiểm tra để chắc rằng chiếc ghế bạn đang ngồi không phải là ghế dành cho người già hay người tàn tật. Ghế cho người già và người yếu thế là một sự quan tâm tối thiểu dành cho những người xung quanh chúng ta. Trước khi nhìn thế giới qua báo chí hoặc tạp chí, bạn nghĩ thế nào về việc nhìn xung quanh trước?
g_text_translate.en: I go to school by subway every day. When I ride the subway, I see many different faces of people. But three or four times a week <u>I see sights that make me frown</u>. Young people are sitting in the 'priority seats' as if they were their own seats, reading newspapers or magazines, and the elderly and the weak are struggling like trees in the rain in front of them. Whenever I see such a sight, my face heats up. Those of you who read the world through newspapers and magazines while sitting on the subway today, make sure that the seat you are sitting on is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. Seats for the elderly or the weak are the least consideration for people around us. Before looking at the world through newspapers or magazines, how about having a mind that looks at the surroundings first?
g_text_translate.it:
Vado a scuola tutti i giorni con la metropolitana. Quando prendo la metropolitana, vedo molti volti diversi di persone. Ma tre o quattro volte alla settimana <u>vedo cose che mi fanno accigliare</u>. I giovani sono seduti nei “posti prioritari” come se fossero i propri posti, leggendo giornali o riviste, e gli anziani e i deboli si dibattono come alberi sotto la pioggia davanti a loro. Ogni volta che vedo uno spettacolo del genere, la mia faccia si scalda. Quelli di voi che oggi leggono il mondo attraverso giornali e riviste mentre sono seduti in metropolitana, assicuratevi che il posto su cui siete seduti non sia un posto per anziani o deboli. I posti per anziani o deboli sono l'ultima considerazione per le persone che ci circondano. Prima di guardare il mondo attraverso giornali o riviste, che ne dici di avere una mente che guardi prima ciò che ci circonda?

#######

Young people are sitting in the 'priority seats' as if they were their own seats, reading newspapers or magazines, and the elderly and the weak are struggling like trees in the rain in front of them. Whenever I see such a sight, my face heats up. Those of you who read the world through newspapers and magazines while sitting on the subway today, make sure that the seat you are sitting on is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. Before looking at the world through newspapers or magazines, how about having a mind that looks at the surroundings first?
g_text_translate.fr:
Je vais à l'école en métro tous les jours. Quand je prends le métro, je vois de nombreux visages différents. Mais trois ou quatre fois par semaine, <u>je vois des spectacles qui me font froncer les sourcils</u>. Les jeunes sont assis sur les « sièges prioritaires » comme s'il s'agissait de leurs propres sièges, lisant des journaux ou des magazines, et les personnes âgées et les faibles se débattent comme des arbres sous la pluie devant eux. Chaque fois que je vois un tel spectacle, mon visage se réchauffe. Ceux d'entre vous qui lisent le monde à travers les journaux et les magazines aujourd'hui assis dans le métro, assurez-vous que le siège sur lequel vous êtes assis n'est pas un siège pour les personnes âgées ou les faibles. Les sièges pour personnes âgées ou faibles sont la moindre considération pour les personnes qui nous entourent. Avant de regarder le monde à travers les journaux ou les magazines, que diriez-vous d’avoir d’abord un esprit qui regarde ce qui l’entoure ?

#######

g_text_translate.es:
Voy a la escuela en metro todos los días. Cuando viajo en metro, veo muchas caras diferentes de personas. Pero tres o cuatro veces por semana <u>veo cosas que me hacen fruncir el ceño</u>. Los jóvenes están sentados en los "asientos prioritarios" como si fueran sus propios asientos, leyendo periódicos o revistas, y los ancianos y los débiles luchan como árboles bajo la lluvia frente a ellos. Cada vez que veo algo así, mi cara se calienta. Aquellos de ustedes que leen el mundo a través de periódicos y revistas mientras están sentados en el metro hoy, asegúrese de que el asiento en el que están sentados no sea un asiento para personas mayores o débiles. Los asientos para personas mayores o débiles son lo que menos consideran las personas que nos rodean. Antes de mirar el mundo a través de periódicos o revistas, ¿qué tal tener una mente que mire primero el entorno?

g_text_translate.id:
Saya pergi ke sekolah dengan kereta bawah tanah setiap hari. Saat saya naik kereta bawah tanah, saya melihat banyak wajah orang yang berbeda. Tapi tiga atau empat kali seminggu <u>Saya melihat pemandangan yang membuat saya cemberut</u>. Kaum muda duduk di 'kursi prioritas' seolah-olah mereka adalah kursi mereka sendiri, membaca koran atau majalah, dan kaum tua serta kaum lemah berjuang seperti pohon di tengah hujan di depan mereka. Setiap kali aku melihat pemandangan seperti itu, wajahku memanas. Anda yang membaca dunia melalui koran dan majalah sambil duduk di kereta bawah tanah saat ini, pastikan bahwa kursi yang Anda duduki bukanlah kursi untuk orang tua atau orang lemah. Kursi untuk orang lanjut usia atau orang lemah adalah hal yang paling tidak diperhatikan oleh orang-orang di sekitar kita. Sebelum melihat dunia melalui surat kabar atau majalah, bagaimana kalau memiliki pikiran yang melihat sekeliling terlebih dahulu?

g_text_translate.ja:
私は毎日地下鉄で学校に行きます。地下鉄に乗っていると、いろんな人の顔が見えます。しかし週に3～4 回は<u>眉をひそめるような光景を目にする</u>ことがあります。若者たちは「優先席」に自分の席のように座って新聞や雑誌を読み、その前では高齢者や弱者が雨に降る木のように奮闘している。そんな光景を見るたびに顔が熱くなる。今、地下鉄に乗りながら新聞や雑誌で世の中の情報を読んでいる人は、自分が座っている席が高齢者や体の弱い人が座る席ではないことを確認してください。お年寄りや体の弱い人のための席は周囲への配慮が最低です。新聞や雑誌で世の中を見る前に、まずは周囲に目を向ける心を持ってみてはいかがでしょうか。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Ich fahre jeden Tag mit der U-Bahn zur Schule. Wenn ich mit der U-Bahn fahre, sehe ich viele verschiedene Gesichter von Menschen. Aber drei- oder viermal in der Woche <u>erlebe ich Dinge, die mich zum Stirnrunzeln bringen</u>. Junge Menschen sitzen auf den „Prioritätsplätzen“, als wären es ihre eigenen Plätze, und lesen Zeitungen oder Zeitschriften, und die Älteren und Schwachen kämpfen wie Bäume im Regen vor ihnen. Immer wenn ich so einen Anblick sehe, wird mir ganz heiß im Gesicht. Diejenigen unter Ihnen, die heute in der U-Bahn die Welt anhand von Zeitungen und Zeitschriften lesen, stellen Sie sicher, dass der Sitz, auf dem Sie sitzen, kein Sitz für ältere oder schwache Menschen ist. Sitze für ältere oder gebrechliche Menschen nehmen für die Menschen in unserer Umgebung am wenigsten Rücksicht. Wie wäre es mit einem Geist, der zuerst die Umgebung betrachtet, bevor er die Welt durch Zeitungen oder Zeitschriften betrachtet?

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我每天乘地铁去学校。当我乘坐地铁时，我看到许多不同面孔的人。但每周都会有三四次<u>看到让我皱眉的景象</u>。年轻人像自己的座位一样坐在“优先席”上看报纸或杂志，而前面的老人和弱者像树木一样在雨中挣扎。每当看到这样的景象，我的脸就会发热。今天乘坐地铁通过报纸杂志阅读世界的人们，请确保您所坐的座位不是老年人或体弱者的座位。对于我们周围的人来说，给老人或弱者的座位是最不考虑的。在通过报纸杂志看世界之前，先有一颗观察周围环境的心怎么样？

g_text_translate.ru:
Я езжу в школу на метро каждый день. Когда я еду в метро, ​​я вижу много разных лиц людей. Но три или четыре раза в неделю <u>я вижу зрелища, которые заставляют меня хмуриться</u>. Молодые люди сидят на «приоритетных местах», как будто это свои собственные места, и читают газеты или журналы, а пожилые и слабые борются перед ними, как деревья под дождем. Всякий раз, когда я вижу такое зрелище, мое лицо горит. Те из вас, кто сегодня читает мир через газеты и журналы, сидя в метро, ​​убедитесь, что место, на котором вы сидите, не предназначено для пожилых или слабых людей. Места для пожилых людей и слабых людей вызывают меньше всего внимания у окружающих нас людей. Прежде чем смотреть на мир через газеты или журналы, как насчет того, чтобы сначала взглянуть на окружающую среду?

ฉันไปโรงเรียนโดยรถไฟใต้ดินทุกวัน เมื่อฉันนั่งรถไฟใต้ดิน ฉันเห็นใบหน้าของผู้คนมากมาย แต่สามหรือสี่ครั้งต่อสัปดาห์ <u>ฉันเห็นสถานที่ท่องเที่ยวที่ทำให้ฉันรู้สึกขมวดคิ้ว</u> คนหนุ่มสาวกำลังนั่งอยู่ใน 'ที่นั่งพิเศษ' ราวกับว่าพวกเขาเป็นที่นั่งของตัวเอง อ่านหนังสือพิมพ์หรือนิตยสาร และผู้สูงอายุและผู้อ่อนแอกำลังดิ้นรนเหมือนต้นไม้ท่ามกลางสายฝนที่อยู่ตรงหน้าพวกเขา ทุกครั้งที่เห็นภาพเช่นนี้ ใบหน้าของฉันก็ร้อนขึ้น บรรดาผู้ที่อ่านโลกผ่านหนังสือพิมพ์และนิตยสารขณะนั่งอยู่บนรถไฟใต้ดินในวันนี้ ตรวจสอบให้แน่ใจว่าที่นั่งที่คุณนั่งอยู่นั้นไม่ใช่ที่นั่งสำหรับผู้สูงอายุหรือผู้อ่อนแอ ที่นั่งสำหรับผู้สูงอายุหรือผู้อ่อนแอถือเป็นสิ่งที่คนรอบข้างคำนึงถึงน้อยที่สุด ก่อนจะมองโลกผ่านหนังสือพิมพ์หรือนิตยสาร การมีจิตใจมองสิ่งรอบข้างก่อนล่ะ?

g_text_translate.pt:
Vou para a escola de metrô todos os dias. Quando ando de metrô, vejo muitos rostos diferentes de pessoas. Mas três ou quatro vezes por semana <u>vejo coisas que me fazem franzir a testa</u>. Os jovens estão sentados nos “assentos prioritários” como se fossem os seus próprios lugares, lendo jornais ou revistas, e os idosos e os mais fracos debatem-se como árvores à chuva à sua frente. Sempre que vejo tal visão, meu rosto esquenta. Aqueles de vocês que hoje leem o mundo através de jornais e revistas enquanto estão sentados no metrô, certifiquem-se de que o assento em que estão sentados não seja um assento para idosos ou fracos. Assentos para idosos ou fracos são os que menos consideram as pessoas ao nosso redor. Antes de olhar o mundo através de jornais ou revistas, que tal ter uma mente que olha primeiro para o entorno?

Before looking at the world through newspapers or magazines, how about having a mind that looks at the surroundings first?
g_text_translate.hi:
मैं हर दिन मेट्रो से स्कूल जाता हूं। जब मैं मेट्रो में यात्रा करता हूं, तो मुझे लोगों के कई अलग-अलग चेहरे दिखाई देते हैं। लेकिन सप्ताह में तीन या चार बार <u>मैं ऐसे दृश्य देखता हूं जिन्हें देखकर मैं भौंचक्का रह जाता हूं</u>। युवा लोग 'प्राथमिकता वाली सीटों' पर ऐसे बैठे हैं जैसे कि वे उनकी अपनी सीटें हों, समाचार पत्र या पत्रिकाएँ पढ़ रहे हों, और बुजुर्ग और कमज़ोर लोग उनके सामने बारिश में पेड़ों की तरह संघर्ष कर रहे हों। जब भी मैं ऐसा नजारा देखता हूं तो मेरा चेहरा गर्म हो जाता है. आपमें से जो लोग आज मेट्रो में बैठकर अखबारों और पत्रिकाओं में दुनिया पढ़ते हैं, वे यह सुनिश्चित कर लें कि आप जिस सीट पर बैठे हैं वह बुजुर्गों या कमजोर लोगों के बैठने की सीट न हो। बुज़ुर्गों या कमज़ोर लोगों के लिए सीटें हमारे आस-पास के लोगों के लिए सबसे कम विचार की बात हैं। अखबारों या पत्रिकाओं के माध्यम से दुनिया को देखने से पहले, एक ऐसे दिमाग का होना कैसा रहेगा जो पहले आसपास के वातावरण को देखता हो?

#######

I go to school by subway every day. When I ride the subway, I see many different faces of people. But three or four times a week <u>I see sights that make me frown</u>. Young people are sitting in the 'priority seats' as if they were their own seats, reading newspapers or magazines, and the elderly and the weak are struggling like trees in the rain in front of them. Whenever I see such a sight, my face heats up. Those of you who read the world through newspapers and magazines while sitting on the subway today, make sure that the seat you are sitting on is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. Seats for the elderly or the weak are the least consideration for people around us. Before looking at the world through newspapers or magazines, how about having a mind that looks at the surroundings first?
g_text_translate.tr:
Her gün okula metroyla gidiyorum. Metroya bindiğimde birçok farklı insan yüzü görüyorum. Ancak haftada üç veya dört kez <u>kaşlarımı çattıran manzaralar görüyorum</u>. Gençler 'öncelikli koltuklarda' sanki kendi koltuklarıymış gibi oturuyor, gazete ya da dergi okuyor, yaşlılar ve zayıflar ise önlerinde yağmurda ağaçlar gibi mücadele ediyor. Ne zaman böyle bir manzara görsem yüzüm kızarır. Bugün metroda otururken dünyayı gazete ve dergilerden okuyanlar, oturduğunuz koltuğun yaşlılara veya zayıflara uygun olmadığından emin olun. Yaşlılar veya zayıflar için olan koltuklar etrafımızdaki insanlar için en az dikkate alınan koltuklardır. Dünyaya gazete ya da dergilerden bakmadan önce, önce çevreye bakan bir zihne sahip olmaya ne dersiniz?

#######

I go to school by subway every day. When I ride the subway, I see many different faces of people. But three or four times a week <u>I see sights that make me frown</u>. Young people are sitting in the 'priority seats' as if they were their own seats, reading newspapers or magazines, and the elderly and the weak are struggling like trees in the rain in front of them. Whenever I see such a sight, my face heats up. Those of you who read the world through newspapers and magazines while sitting on the subway today, make sure that the seat you are sitting on is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. Seats for the elderly or the weak are the least consideration for people around us. Before looking at the world through newspapers or magazines, how about having a mind that looks at the surroundings first?

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 노약자들은 주변을 먼저 보는 마음을 가지고 있다.
젊은 사람들이 노약자석에 앉아 있는 것을 매일 볼 수 있다.
지하철에서 무심코 앉은 자리가 노약자석일 수도 있다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Người già và người yếu thế có tấm lòng nhìn xung quanh trước.
2. Tôi đọc báo và tạp chí trên tàu điện ngầm.
3. Có thể thấy việc những người trẻ tuổi ngồi trên ghế dành cho người già và yếu thế hàng ngày.
4. Vị trí bạn ngồi vô tình trên tàu điện ngầm cũng có thể là ghế dành cho người già và yếu thế.
--------------------
V + 는 양, A + (으)ㄴ 양: Như thể, cứ như là.

Người viết kêu gọi rằng khi đi trên tàu điện ngầm, hãy kiểm tra để chắc rằng chiếc ghế bạn đang ngồi không phải là ghế dành cho người già hay người tàn tật. 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. The elderly and the weak have a mind to look at their surroundings first.
2. I read newspapers and magazines on the subway.
3. You can see young people every day sitting in the priority seats.
4. The seat you sit inadvertently on the subway may be a seat for the elderly and the weak.
--------------------
<g>V + 는 양, A + (으)ㄴ 양:</g> A word used to indicate that one seems to be in a certain state or likely to do a certain thing.

The writer urges that when riding the subway, check to make sure that the seat you are sitting in is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. 4 is the correct answer.

4. Il posto in cui ti siedi inavvertitamente nella metropolitana può essere un posto per anziani e deboli.

1. Orang tua dan orang lemah mempunyai pikiran untuk melihat sekelilingnya terlebih dahulu.

3. Anda dapat melihat anak muda setiap hari duduk di kursi prioritas.
4. Kursi yang Anda duduki secara tidak sengaja di kereta bawah tanah mungkin merupakan kursi untuk orang tua dan orang lemah.
--------------------
<g>V + 는 양, A + (으)ㄴ 양:</g>Sebuah kata yang digunakan untuk menunjukkan bahwa seseorang tampaknya berada dalam keadaan tertentu atau kemungkinan besar akan melakukan hal tertentu.

Penulis menghimbau agar saat menaiki kereta bawah tanah, pastikan bahwa kursi yang Anda duduki bukanlah kursi untuk orang tua atau orang lemah. 4 adalah jawaban yang benar.

4. The seat you sit inadvertently on the subway may be a seat for the elderly and the weak.
--------------------
<g>V + 는 양, A + (으)ㄴ 양:</g> A word used to indicate that one seems to be in a certain state or likely to do a certain thing.

The writer urges that when riding the subway, check to make sure that the seat you are sitting in is not a seat for the elderly or the weak. 4 is the correct answer.

explain.hi:
वह चुनें जो अनुच्छेद की सामग्री से मेल खाता हो।

general.g_text: 나는 필요 없게 된 물건도 버리지 못하고 모아 두는 습관이 있다. 그러다 보니 나도 모르게 온 집안이 크고 작은 상자들로 가둑 차게 되었다. 그런데 한 달 뒤면 나는 이사를 해야 한다. 그래서 며칠을 고민한 끝에 상자 속의 물건들을 모두 버리기로 결정했다. 가만히 생각해 보니 내가 지금까지 상자 속의 물건들을 버리지 못한 이유는 언젠가 그 물건이 다시 필요할지도 모른다는 불안감 때문이었을 것이다. 아니면 거기에 담긴 특별한 추억 때문에 그것을 버리려고 할 때마다 <u>마음이 흔들렸는지도 모른다</u>. 그러나 이제는 버릴 것은 확실히 버리고 그 자리에 채워 넣을 새로운 것을 찾는 데 더 많은 노력을 기울여야겠다는 생각이 든다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi có thói quen thu thập cả những thứ mình không cần nữa nhưng lại không thể vứt đi. Kết quả là toàn bộ ngôi nhà được lấp đầy bởi các hộp lớn nhỏ mà tôi cũng không nhận ra. Nhưng trong một tháng nữa, tôi phải chuyển đi. Vì vậy, sau khi suy nghĩ về chúng trong vài ngày, tôi quyết định vứt bỏ tất cả các món đồ trong hộp. Nghĩ lại, lý do tôi không thể vứt những món đồ trong hộp cho đến nay có lẽ là vì tôi lo lắng rằng một ngày nào đó tôi có thể cần chúng một lần nữa. Hoặc có thể do những ký ức đặc biệt mà nó chứa đựng đã làm <u>trái tim tôi dao động</u> mỗi khi tôi muốn vứt bỏ chúng. Tuy nhiên, bây giờ tôi nghĩ mình phải nỗ lực nhiều hơn nữa để thực sự vứt bỏ những thứ phải bỏ đi và tìm cái mới để lấp đầy vị trí đó.
g_text_translate.en: I have a habit of hoarding things that I no longer need. As a result, the whole house was filled with big and small boxes without realizing it. But in a month I have to move. So after thinking about it for a few days, I decided to throw away all the items in the box. Thinking about it, the reason I haven't thrown away the items in the box until now is probably because of anxiety that I might need them again someday. Or maybe the special memories they hold <u>have shaken my heart</u> every time I try to throw them away. But now I feel like I need to put more effort into throwing out what I'm going to throw away and finding something new to fill in its place.
g_text_translate.it:
Ho l'abitudine di accumulare cose di cui non ho più bisogno. Di conseguenza, tutta la casa si riempì di scatole grandi e piccole senza rendersene conto. Ma tra un mese devo trasferirmi. Quindi, dopo averci pensato per qualche giorno, ho deciso di buttare via tutti gli oggetti presenti nella scatola. Pensandoci, il motivo per cui fino ad ora non ho buttato via gli oggetti nella scatola è probabilmente l'ansia di poterne aver bisogno di nuovo un giorno. O forse i ricordi speciali che custodiscono <u>mi hanno scosso il cuore</u> ogni volta che provo a buttarli via. Ma ora sento che devo impegnarmi di più per buttare via ciò che butterò via e trovare qualcosa di nuovo da riempire al suo posto.

#######

I have a habit of hoarding things that I no longer need. As a result, the whole house was filled with big and small boxes without realizing it. But in a month I have to move. So after thinking about it for a few days, I decided to throw away all the items in the box. Thinking about it, the reason I haven't thrown away the items in the box until now is probably because of anxiety that I might need them again someday. Or maybe the special memories they hold <u>have shaken my heart</u> every time I try to throw them away. But now I feel like I need to put more effort into throwing out what I'm going to throw away and finding something new to fill in its place.
g_text_translate.fr:
J'ai l'habitude d'accumuler des choses dont je n'ai plus besoin. Du coup, toute la maison était remplie de petits et grands cartons sans s’en rendre compte. Mais dans un mois je dois déménager. Alors après quelques jours de réflexion, j’ai décidé de jeter tous les objets contenus dans la boîte. En y réfléchissant, la raison pour laquelle je n'ai pas jeté les objets dans la boîte jusqu'à présent est probablement due à l'anxiété d'en avoir à nouveau besoin un jour. Ou peut-être que les souvenirs spéciaux qu'ils contiennent <u>ont secoué mon cœur</u> à chaque fois que j'essaie de les jeter. Mais maintenant, j'ai l'impression que je dois faire plus d'efforts pour jeter ce que je vais jeter et trouver quelque chose de nouveau pour le remplacer.

#######

I have a habit of hoarding things that I no longer need. As a result, the whole house was filled with big and small boxes without realizing it. But in a month I have to move. So after thinking about it for a few days, I decided to throw away all the items in the box. Thinking about it, the reason I haven't thrown away the items in the box until now is probably because of anxiety that I might need them again someday. Or maybe the special memories they hold <u>have shaken my heart</u> every time I try to throw them away. But now I feel like I need to put more effort into throwing out what I'm going to throw away and finding something new to fill in its place.
g_text_translate.es:
Tengo la costumbre de acumular cosas que ya no necesito. Como resultado, toda la casa se llenó de cajas grandes y pequeñas sin darnos cuenta. Pero en un mes tengo que mudarme. Entonces, después de pensarlo durante unos días, decidí tirar todos los artículos de la caja. Pensándolo bien, la razón por la que no he tirado los artículos de la caja hasta ahora es probablemente por la ansiedad de volver a necesitarlos algún día. O tal vez los recuerdos especiales que guardan <u>han sacudido mi corazón</u> cada vez que intento desecharlos. Pero ahora siento que necesito esforzarme más para desechar lo que voy a tirar y encontrar algo nuevo para ocupar su lugar.

#######

Thinking about it, the reason I haven't thrown away the items in the box until now is probably because of anxiety that I might need them again someday. Or maybe the special memories they hold <u>have shaken my heart</u> every time I try to throw them away. But now I feel like I need to put more effort into throwing out what I'm going to throw away and finding something new to fill in its place.
g_text_translate.id:
Saya mempunyai kebiasaan menimbun barang-barang yang sudah tidak diperlukan lagi. Alhasil, tanpa disadari seluruh rumah dipenuhi kotak-kotak besar dan kecil. Tapi dalam sebulan saya harus pindah. Jadi setelah memikirkannya selama beberapa hari, saya memutuskan untuk membuang semua barang yang ada di dalam kotak. Kalau dipikir-pikir, alasan aku belum membuang barang-barang di dalam kotak sampai sekarang mungkin karena khawatir aku akan membutuhkannya lagi suatu hari nanti. Atau mungkin kenangan istimewa yang mereka simpan <u>telah menggoncangkan hatiku</u> setiap kali aku mencoba membuangnya. Tapi sekarang saya merasa perlu lebih berupaya membuang apa yang akan saya buang dan mencari sesuatu yang baru untuk menggantikannya.

#######

I have a habit of hoarding things that I no longer need. As a result, the whole house was filled with big and small boxes without realizing it. But in a month I have to move. So after thinking about it for a few days, I decided to throw away all the items in the box. Thinking about it, the reason I haven't thrown away the items in the box until now is probably because of anxiety that I might need them again someday. Or maybe the special memories they hold <u>have shaken my heart</u> every time I try to throw them away. But now I feel like I need to put more effort into throwing out what I'm going to throw away and finding something new to fill in its place.
g_text_translate.ja:
私には要らなくなったものを溜め込む癖があります。その結果、いつのまにか家中が大小の箱で埋め尽くされてしまいました。でも一ヶ月後には引っ越ししなければなりません。そこで数日考えた結果、箱の中のものをすべて捨てることにしました。考えてみると、今まで箱の中のものを捨てられなかったのは、いつかまた必要になるかもしれないという不安からかもしれません。あるいは、 その特別な思い出が<u>捨てようとするたびに私の心を揺さぶる</u>のかもしれません。でも今は、捨てるものを捨てて、その代わりとなる新しいものを見つけることにもっと努力する必要があると感じています。

g_text_translate.de:
Ich habe die Angewohnheit, Dinge zu horten, die ich nicht mehr brauche. Infolgedessen war das ganze Haus voller großer und kleiner Kisten, ohne dass man es merkte. Aber in einem Monat muss ich umziehen. Nachdem ich ein paar Tage darüber nachgedacht hatte, beschloss ich, alle Gegenstände in der Schachtel wegzuwerfen. Wenn ich darüber nachdenke, liegt der Grund dafür, dass ich die Gegenstände in der Schachtel bisher nicht weggeworfen habe, wahrscheinlich in der Angst, dass ich sie eines Tages wieder brauchen könnte. Oder vielleicht haben die besonderen Erinnerungen, die sie bewahren, jedes Mal, wenn ich versuche, sie wegzuwerfen, mein Herz erschüttert. Aber jetzt habe ich das Gefühl, dass ich mich mehr anstrengen muss, das, was ich wegwerfen werde, wegzuwerfen und etwas Neues zu finden, um es zu ersetzen.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我有囤积不再需要的东西的习惯。结果不知不觉间整个屋子就堆满了大大小小的箱子。但一个月后我必须搬家。于是我想了几天，决定把盒子里的东西全部扔掉。想来，我之所以到现在才把盒子里的东西扔掉，大概是担心有一天会再次需要它们。或者也许每次我试图扔掉它们时，它们所承载的特殊记忆<u>都震动了我的心</u>。但现在我觉得我需要付出更多的努力来扔掉我要扔掉的东西并寻找新的东西来填补它的位置。

g_text_translate.ru:
У меня есть привычка копить вещи, которые мне больше не нужны. В результате весь дом был заставлен большими и маленькими коробками, даже не осознавая этого. Но через месяц мне придется переехать. Поэтому, подумав об этом несколько дней, я решил выбросить все предметы из коробки. Если подумать, причина, по которой я до сих пор не выбросил предметы из коробки, вероятно, связана с тревогой, что они мне когда-нибудь снова понадобятся. Или, может быть, особые воспоминания, которые они хранят, <u>потрясали мое сердце</u> каждый раз, когда я пытался их выбросить. Но теперь я чувствую, что мне нужно приложить больше усилий, чтобы выбросить то, что я собираюсь выбросить, и найти что-то новое, чтобы заменить это.

g_text_translate.pt:
Tenho o hábito de acumular coisas que não preciso mais. Como resultado, toda a casa ficou cheia de caixas grandes e pequenas sem perceber. Mas em um mês tenho que me mudar. Então, depois de pensar alguns dias, decidi jogar fora todos os itens da caixa. Pensando bem, o motivo pelo qual não joguei fora os itens da caixa até agora é provavelmente por causa da ansiedade de poder precisar deles novamente algum dia. Ou talvez as memórias especiais que eles guardam <u>abalem meu coração</u> toda vez que tento jogá-los fora. Mas agora sinto que preciso me esforçar mais para jogar fora o que vou jogar fora e encontrar algo novo para preencher o seu lugar.

g_text_translate.hi:
मुझे उन चीज़ों को जमा करने की आदत है जिनकी मुझे अब ज़रूरत नहीं है। नतीजा यह हुआ कि पता ही नहीं चला कि पूरा घर छोटे-बड़े बक्सों से भर गया। लेकिन एक महीने में मुझे आगे बढ़ना होगा. इसलिए कुछ दिनों तक इसके बारे में सोचने के बाद, मैंने बक्से में मौजूद सभी वस्तुओं को फेंकने का फैसला किया। इसके बारे में सोचते हुए, मैंने अब तक बक्से में मौजूद वस्तुओं को नहीं फेंका है, शायद यह चिंता के कारण है कि किसी दिन मुझे फिर से उनकी आवश्यकता हो सकती है। या हो सकता है कि जब भी मैं उन्हें फेंकने की कोशिश करता हूं तो उनके पास मौजूद विशेष यादें <u>मेरे दिल को झकझोर देती हैं</u>। लेकिन अब मुझे लगता है कि जो मैं फेंकने जा रहा हूं उसे बाहर फेंकने और उसकी जगह भरने के लिए कुछ नया ढूंढने के लिए मुझे और अधिक प्रयास करने की जरूरत है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Artık ihtiyacım olmayan şeyleri biriktirme alışkanlığım var. Bunun sonucunda bütün ev farkında olmadan irili ufaklı kutularla doldu. Ama bir ay içinde taşınmam gerekiyor. Birkaç gün düşündükten sonra kutudaki tüm eşyaları atmaya karar verdim. Düşününce, şu ana kadar kutudaki eşyaları atmamamın nedeni muhtemelen bir gün onlara tekrar ihtiyacım olabileceği endişesinden kaynaklanıyor. Ya da belki de sahip oldukları özel anılar, onları her atmaya çalıştığımda <u>kalbimi sarsıyordu</u>. Ama artık atacaklarımı atmak ve yerini dolduracak yeni bir şey bulmak için daha fazla çaba harcamam gerektiğini hissediyorum.

explain.ru: Выберите правильный вариант, основываясь на ощущении «Я», показанном в подчеркнутой части. 1. Я беспокоюсь, что мои воспоминания исчезнут вместе с ними. 2. Я чувствую фрустрацию, потому что не могу вспомнить, какие именно воспоминания у меня есть. 3. Я беспокоюсь, что воспоминания будут продолжать возвращаться ко мне. 4. Я чувствую сожаление, потому что у меня не так много особенных воспоминаний. --------------------Этот человек волнуется, пытаясь избавиться от вещей, хранящих особые воспоминания. 1 – правильный ответ.####### Choose the right one based on the feeling of 'I' shown in the underlined part. 1. I'm worried that my memories will disappear along with them. 2. I feel frustrated because I can't remember what kind of memories I have. 3. I'm worried that memories will keep coming back to me. 4. I feel regretful because I don't have many special memories. -------------------- This person is worried when trying to get rid of things that contain special memories. 1 is the correct answer.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 상자들을 둘 곳이 부족해서 이사를 하게 되었다.
나는 여러 가지 상자를 모으는 습관이 있다.
이제부터 필요 없게 된 물건들은 그냥 버리려고 한다.
상자 속의 물건들은 다 나중에 다시 사용할 것이다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Vì thiếu chỗ để chứa hộp nên tôi đã phải chuyển nhà.
2. Tôi có thói quen sưu tầm nhiều loại hộp.
3. Từ bây giờ tôi sẽ vứt bỏ những đồ vật không cần thiết.
4. Tất cả đồ vật trong hộp sau này sẽ được sử dụng lại.
--------------------
Nhân vật 'tôi' nghĩ rằng mình sẽ nỗ lực để thực sự vứt bỏ những thứ phải bỏ đi. 3 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. We had to move because there was not enough space to put the boxes.
2. I have a habit of collecting various boxes.
3. I just try to throw away things that I no longer need.
4. Everything in the box will be reused later.
--------------------
This person feels like he/she needs to put more effort into throwing out what he/she is going to throw away. 3 is the correct answer.
explain.it:
Scegli quello che corrisponde al contenuto del paragrafo.
1. Ci siamo dovuti spostare perché non c'era abbastanza spazio per riporre gli scatoloni.
2. Ho l'abitudine di collezionare varie scatole.
3. Cerco solo di buttare via le cose che non mi servono più.
4. Tutto nella scatola verrà riutilizzato in seguito.
--------------------
Questa persona sente di dover fare uno sforzo maggiore per buttare via ciò che butterà via. 3 è la risposta corretta.

--------------------

general.g_text: 나의 막내 아들은 작년에 초등학교 1학년이 되었어야 할 나이다. 하지만 아직 학교 근처에도 못 가고 있다. 이 아이가 매우 드문 병에 걸린 것은 벌써 2년 전의 일이다. 그때 수술을 받고 지금까지 치료를 받아 왔다. 그런데 며칠 전부터 아이의 상태가 심상치 않 았다. 아이는 계속 고열에 시달렸다. 우리 부부는 아이의 병이 다시 재발한다면 오늘날의 의학으로는 치료 방법이 없다는 것을 알고 있었다. 아이의 손목을 잡고 병원 문을 들어서는 우리 부부는 <u>무거운 돌이 가슴을 누르는 듯해 숨을 쉬기도 어려웠다</u>. 하지만 예상하지 못했던 검사 결과가 나왔다. 아이는 감기에 걸렸을 뿐, 예전의 병은 거짓말처럼 나은 것이다. 우리 부부는 너무 놀라서 한참 동안 움직일 수 없었다.

g_text_translate.vi: Con trai út của đã đến tuổi lẽ ra phải học lớp một năm ngoái. Nhưng thằng bé vẫn chưa được đi học. Đã hai năm rồi đứa trẻ này mắc một căn bệnh rất hiếm gặp. Khi đó thằng bé đã trải qua một cuộc phẫu thuật và đã được điều trị đến bây giờ. Tuy nhiên, cách đây vài ngày, tình trạng của thằng bé không bình thường. Thằng bé liên tục sốt cao. Chồng tôi và tôi biết rằng nếu bệnh của con chúng tôi tái phát, không có cách nào chữa khỏi bằng y học ngày nay. Khi chúng tôi nắm cổ tay của đứa trẻ và bước vào cửa bệnh viện, <u>cảm giác như có một hòn đá nặng đè lên ngực, khiến chúng tôi thở thôi cũng khó khăn</u>. Tuy nhiên, kết quả xét nghiệm thật ngoài dự đoán. Đứa trẻ chỉ bị cảm, bệnh cũ đã tốt lên tựa như chỉ là một lời nói dối. Tôi và chồng bất ngờ đến mức không thể cử động được một lúc lâu.
g_text_translate.en: My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.it:
Il mio figlio più piccolo avrebbe dovuto frequentare la prima elementare l'anno scorso. Ma non riesce ancora nemmeno ad avvicinarsi alla scuola. Sono già passati due anni da quando questo bambino ha contratto una malattia molto rara. Poi è stato sottoposto ad un intervento chirurgico e da allora continua a ricevere cure. Tuttavia, qualche giorno fa, le condizioni del bambino non erano gravi. Il bambino continuava ad avere la febbre alta. Mio marito ed io sapevamo che se la malattia di nostro figlio avesse avuto una ricaduta, non ci sarebbe stata cura con la medicina odierna. Mentre attraversavamo la porta dell'ospedale tenendo il polso del bambino, <u>avevamo la sensazione che una pietra pesante premesse sul nostro petto, rendendo difficile la respirazione</u>. Tuttavia, sono emersi risultati inaspettati dei test. Il bambino ha preso solo un raffreddore e la malattia precedente è stata curata come una bugia. Mio marito ed io eravamo così scioccati che non potevamo muoverci per un po'.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.fr:
Mon plus jeune fils aurait dû être en première année du primaire l'année dernière. Mais il ne peut même pas encore s'approcher de l'école. Cela fait déjà deux ans que cet enfant a contracté une maladie très rare. Il a ensuite été opéré et depuis, il est soigné. Pourtant, il y a quelques jours, l’état de l’enfant n’était pas grave. L'enfant souffrait toujours d'une forte fièvre. Mon mari et moi savions que si la maladie de notre enfant récidivait, les médicaments d'aujourd'hui ne permettraient pas de la guérir. Alors que nous franchissions la porte de l'hôpital en tenant le poignet de l'enfant, <u>c'était comme si une lourde pierre appuyait sur notre poitrine, rendant la respiration difficile</u>. Cependant, des résultats de tests inattendus sont apparus. L'enfant n'a attrapé qu'un rhume et la maladie précédente a été guérie comme un mensonge. Mon mari et moi avons été tellement choqués que nous n'avons pas pu bouger pendant un moment.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.es:
Mi hijo menor debería haber estado en primer grado de la escuela primaria el año pasado. Pero todavía ni siquiera puede acercarse a la escuela. Ya han pasado dos años desde que este niño contrajo una enfermedad muy rara. Luego fue operado y desde entonces ha estado recibiendo tratamiento. Sin embargo, hace unos días el estado del niño no reviste gravedad. El niño seguía sufriendo fiebre alta. Mi esposo y yo sabíamos que si la enfermedad de nuestro hijo recaía, no habría cura con los medicamentos actuales. Mientras atravesábamos la puerta del hospital sosteniendo la muñeca del niño, <u>sentí como si una piedra pesada estuviera presionando nuestro pecho, dificultando la respiración</u>. Sin embargo, surgieron resultados inesperados de las pruebas. El niño solo se resfrió y la enfermedad anterior se curó como una mentira. Mi esposo y yo quedamos tan impactados que no pudimos movernos por un tiempo.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.id:
Putra bungsu saya seharusnya sudah duduk di bangku kelas satu sekolah dasar tahun lalu. Tapi dia bahkan belum bisa mendekati sekolah. Sudah dua tahun berlalu sejak anak ini terjangkit penyakit yang sangat langka. Kemudian dia menjalani operasi dan menerima perawatan sejak saat itu. Namun, beberapa hari lalu, kondisi anak tersebut tidak parah. Anak itu terus menderita demam tinggi. Saya dan suami tahu, jika penyakit anak kami kambuh, tidak akan ada obat yang bisa disembuhkan dengan obat yang ada saat ini. Saat kami berjalan melewati pintu rumah sakit sambil memegangi pergelangan tangan anak tersebut, <u>rasanya seperti ada batu berat yang menekan dada kami, sehingga sulit bernapas</u>. Namun, hasil tes yang tidak terduga keluar. Anak itu hanya masuk angin, dan penyakit sebelumnya sembuh seperti bohong. Saya dan suami sangat terkejut hingga kami tidak bisa bergerak untuk sementara waktu.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. g_text_translate.ja:
うちの末っ子は去年小学一年生だったはずです。しかし、彼はまだ学校に近づくことさえできません。この子が非常に珍しい病気に罹ってからすでに2年が経ちました。その後手術を受け、現在も治療を受けている。しかし、数日前、子供の状態は深刻ではありませんでした。その子は高熱に悩まされ続けた。夫も私も、子供の病気が再発した場合、今の医学では治療できないことを知っていました。子どもの手首を抱えて病院のドアを通ったとき、 まるで<u>重い石が胸を押さえつけられているような感覚があり、 呼吸が困難</u>になりました。しかし、予想外の検査結果が出た。子供は風邪をひいただけで、前の病気が嘘のように治りました。夫も私もショックのあまり、しばらく動けなくなりました。

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. g_text_translate.de:
Mein jüngster Sohn hätte letztes Jahr in die erste Klasse der Grundschule gehen sollen. Aber er kommt noch nicht einmal in die Nähe der Schule. Es ist bereits zwei Jahre her, dass dieses Kind an einer sehr seltenen Krankheit erkrankte. Dann wurde er operiert und ist seitdem in Behandlung. Vor einigen Tagen war der Zustand des Kindes jedoch nicht ernst. Das Kind litt weiterhin unter hohem Fieber. Mein Mann und ich wussten, dass es mit der heutigen Medizin keine Heilung geben würde, wenn die Krankheit unseres Kindes erneut auftreten würde. Als wir durch die Krankenhaustür gingen und das Handgelenk des Kindes hielten, fühlte es sich an, als würde ein schwerer Stein auf unsere Brust drücken und das Atmen erschweren.</u> Allerdings kamen unerwartete Testergebnisse ans Licht. Das Kind bekam nur eine Erkältung und die vorherige Krankheit wurde wie eine Lüge geheilt. Mein Mann und ich waren so geschockt, dass wir uns eine Zeit lang nicht bewegen konnten.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我最小的儿子去年应该上小学一年级了。但他还不能接近学校。这个孩子患上一种非常罕见的疾病已经两年了。随后他接受了手术，此后一直在接受治疗。不过，前几天，孩子的情况并不严重。孩子继续发高烧。我和丈夫都知道，如果孩子的病复发，用今天的药是治不了的。当我们抱着孩子的手腕走进医院门口时，<u>感觉胸口仿佛压着一块重石，呼吸困难</u>。然而，意想不到的测试结果却出来了。孩子只是感冒了，之前的病就像骗人的一样好了。我和丈夫惊得一时不敢动弹。

g_text_translate.zh-TW:
我最小的兒子去年應該上小學一年級了。但他還不能接近學校。這個孩子患上一種非常罕見的疾病已經兩年了。隨後他接受了手術，此後一直在接受治療。不過，前幾天，孩子的情況並不嚴重。孩子繼續發高燒。我和丈夫都知道，如果孩子的病復發，用今天的藥是治不了的。當我們抱著孩子的手腕走進醫院門口時，<u>感覺胸口彷彿壓著一塊重石，呼吸困難</u>。然而，意想不到的測試結果卻出來了。孩子只是感冒了，之前的病就像騙人的一樣好了。我和丈夫驚得一時不敢動彈。

#######

It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. g_text_translate.ru:
Мой младший сын в прошлом году должен был пойти в первый класс начальной школы. Но он пока не может даже приблизиться к школе. Прошло уже два года с тех пор, как этот ребенок заразился очень редким заболеванием. Затем он перенес операцию и с тех пор получает лечение. Однако несколько дней назад состояние ребенка было несерьезным. Ребенок продолжал страдать от высокой температуры. Мы с мужем знали, что если болезнь нашего ребенка рецидивирует, то с помощью современной медицины ее не вылечить. Когда мы прошли через дверь больницы, держа ребенка за запястье, <u>ощущение было такое, как будто тяжелый камень давил на нашу грудь, мешая дышать</u>. Однако появились неожиданные результаты испытаний. Ребенок только простудился, а предыдущая болезнь вылечилась ложью. Мы с мужем были так потрясены, что некоторое время не могли двигаться.

#######

g_text_translate.th:
ปีที่แล้วลูกชายคนเล็กของฉันน่าจะอยู่ชั้นประถมศึกษาปีที่ 1 แต่เขายังไม่สามารถเข้าใกล้โรงเรียนได้ เป็นเวลาสองปีแล้วที่เด็กคนนี้ติดโรคที่หายากมาก จากนั้นเขาก็เข้ารับการผ่าตัดและได้รับการรักษาตั้งแต่นั้นเป็นต้นมา อย่างไรก็ตาม เมื่อไม่กี่วันก่อน อาการของเด็กไม่ได้ร้ายแรง เด็กยังคงมีไข้สูงอย่างต่อเนื่อง ฉันและสามีรู้ว่าถ้าโรคของลูกเรากำเริบอีก ยาในปัจจุบันคงไม่มีทางรักษาได้ ขณะที่เราเดินผ่านประตูโรงพยาบาลโดยจับข้อมือเด็กไว้ <u>รู้สึกเหมือนมีก้อนหินหนักกดลงบนหน้าอกของเรา ทำให้หายใจลำบาก</u> อย่างไรก็ตาม ผลการทดสอบที่ไม่คาดคิดก็ออกมา เด็กเป็นเพียงไข้หวัดและโรคก่อนหน้านี้ก็หายขาดเหมือนเรื่องโกหก ฉันกับสามีตกใจมากจนขยับตัวไม่ได้สักพักหนึ่ง

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. g_text_translate.pt:
Meu filho mais novo deveria estar na primeira série do ensino fundamental no ano passado. Mas ele ainda não consegue nem chegar perto da escola. Já se passaram dois anos desde que esta criança contraiu uma doença muito rara. Em seguida, ele foi submetido a uma cirurgia e está em tratamento desde então. Porém, há poucos dias, o estado da criança não era grave. A criança continuou a sofrer de febre alta. Meu marido e eu sabíamos que se a doença do nosso filho recidivasse, não haveria cura com os remédios atuais. Ao passarmos pela porta do hospital segurando o pulso da criança, <u>sentimos como se uma pedra pesada estivesse pressionando nosso peito, dificultando a respiração</u>. No entanto, surgiram resultados de testes inesperados. A criança só pegou um resfriado e a doença anterior foi curada como uma mentira. Meu marido e eu ficamos tão chocados que não conseguimos nos mover por um tempo.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.hi:
मेरा सबसे छोटा बेटा पिछले साल प्राथमिक विद्यालय की पहली कक्षा में होना चाहिए था। लेकिन वह अभी तक स्कूल के करीब भी नहीं पहुंच सका है। इस बच्चे को एक बहुत ही दुर्लभ बीमारी से पीड़ित हुए दो साल हो चुके हैं। फिर उनकी सर्जरी हुई और तब से उनका इलाज चल रहा है। हालांकि, कुछ दिन पहले बच्चे की हालत गंभीर नहीं थी. बच्चा लगातार तेज बुखार से पीड़ित रहा। मैं और मेरे पति जानते थे कि अगर हमारे बच्चे की बीमारी दोबारा हो गई, तो आज की दवा से कोई इलाज नहीं होगा। जैसे ही हम बच्चे की कलाई पकड़ कर अस्पताल के दरवाज़े से गुज़रे, <u>ऐसा लगा मानो कोई भारी पत्थर हमारी छाती पर दब रहा हो, जिससे सांस लेना मुश्किल हो रहा हो</u>। हालाँकि, अप्रत्याशित परीक्षण परिणाम सामने आए। बच्चे को केवल सर्दी लग गई, और पिछली बीमारी झूठ की तरह ठीक हो गई। मैं और मेरे पति इतने सदमे में थे कि कुछ देर तक हम हिल भी नहीं सके।

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.
g_text_translate.tr:
En küçük oğlumun geçen sene ilkokul birinci sınıfa gitmesi gerekiyordu. Ama henüz okula yaklaşamıyor bile. Bu çocuğun çok nadir görülen bir hastalığa yakalanmasının üzerinden iki yıl geçti. Daha sonra ameliyata alındı ​​ve o günden beri tedavi görüyor. Ancak birkaç gün önce çocuğun durumunun ciddi olmadığı öğrenildi. Çocuğun yüksek ateşi devam ediyordu. Eşim ve ben, çocuğumuzun hastalığının tekrarlaması durumunda günümüz ilaçları ile çare olmayacağını biliyorduk. Çocuğun bileğini tutarak hastane kapısından içeri girerken <u>sanki göğsümüze ağır bir taş baskı yapıyor ve nefes almamızı zorlaştırıyormuş gibi hissettik</u>. Ancak beklenmeyen test sonuçları ortaya çıktı. Çocuk sadece soğuk algınlığına yakalandı ve önceki hastalık bir yalan gibi iyileşti. Kocam ve ben o kadar şok olduk ki bir süre hareket edemedik.

#######

My youngest son should have been in the first grade of elementary school last year. But he can't even get close to school yet. It has already been two years since this child contracted a very rare disease. Then he underwent surgery and has been receiving treatment ever since. However, a few days ago, the condition of the child was not serious. The child continued to suffer from high fever. My husband and I knew that if our child's disease relapsed, there would be no cure with today's medicine. As we walked through the hospital door holding the child's wrist, <u>it felt as if a heavy stone was pressing down on our chest, making it difficult to breathe</u>. However, unexpected test results came out. The child only caught a cold, and the previous disease was cured like a lie. My husband and I were so shocked that we couldn't move for a while.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.

explain.vi:

1. Đã có kết quả kiểm tra không như dự kiến.
2. Lời nói rằng thằng bé đã khỏi bệnh là lời nói dối.
3. Đứa trẻ đã nhập học tiểu học một năm trước.
4. Bệnh của đứa trẻ lại tái phát.
--------------------
Theo như dự kiến, kết quả xét nghiệm phải là một kết quả xấu. Nhưng kết quả lại vượt ngoài dự đoán, đứa trẻ chỉ bị cảm chứ không phải tái phát bệnh cũ. Do đó 1 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:

1. An unexpected test result came out.
2. It was a lie to say that the child was healed.
3. The child entered elementary school a year ago.
4. The child's disease has relapsed.
--------------------
As expected, the test result should be a bad one. But the child only had a cold, not a relapse of the previous disease. So 1 is the correct answer.

वह चुनें जो अनुच्छेद की सामग्री से मेल खाता हो।
1. एक अप्रत्याशित परीक्षा परिणाम सामने आया।
2. यह कहना झूठ था कि बच्चा ठीक हो गया।
3. बच्चा एक वर्ष पहले प्राथमिक विद्यालय में दाखिल हुआ।
4. बच्चे की बीमारी दोबारा हो गई है.
------------------------
जैसा कि अपेक्षित था, परीक्षा परिणाम ख़राब होना चाहिए। लेकिन बच्चे को केवल सर्दी थी, पिछली बीमारी दोबारा नहीं हुई थी। अतः 1 सही उत्तर है।

#######

Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. An unexpected test result came out.
2. It was a lie to say that the child was healed.
3. The child entered elementary school a year ago.
4. The child's disease has relapsed.
--------------------
As expected, the test result should be a bad one. But the child only had a cold, not a relapse of the previous disease. So 1 is the correct answer.
explain.tr:
Paragrafın içeriğine uygun olanı seçin.
1. Beklenmeyen bir test sonucu çıktı.
2. Çocuğun iyileştiğini söylemek yalandı.
3. Çocuk bir yıl önce ilkokula başladı.
4. Çocuğun hastalığının tekrarlaması.
--------------------
Beklendiği gibi test sonucu kötü olmalı. Ancak çocukta önceki hastalığın nüksetmesi değil, yalnızca soğuk algınlığı vardı. Yani doğru cevap 1'dir.

general.g_text: 나는 무역 회사에 다니고 있는 직장인이다. 나는 많이 긴장을 하거나 스트레스를 받으면 심한 두통이 찾아오곤 하는 습관이 있다. 가끔 회사에서도 그럴 때가 있는데 참을 수 없을 만큼 머리가 아픈데도 병원에 가서 진료를 받기가 쉽지 않다. 너 나 할 것 없이 바쁜 업무 시간에 병원에 간다고 하는 게 눈치가 보이기 때문이다. 며칠 전에도 두통이 너무 심해서 병원에 다녀오겠다는 이야기를 하려다가 부장님의 말을 듣고 그냥 <u>입을 닫아 버렸다</u>. 부장님은 시계를 한 번 보시더니 "아이구, 오늘은 일이 너무 많아서 커피 한 잔 할 시간이 없었네요. 자, 우리 잠깐 커피 한잔씩만 하고 다시 열심히 일합시다. 그래야 퇴근 전에 끝낼 수 있죠." 앞으로도 직장 생활을 잘하려면 아무래도 두통을 참는 연습을 더 많이 해야 할 것 같다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi là nhân viên tại một công ty thương mại. Tôi có thói quen bị đau đầu dữ dội khi lo lắng nhiều hoặc bị căng thẳng. Thỉnh thoảng ở công ty, cũng có những lúc tôi bị như vậy nhưng dù đau đầu đến mức không thể chịu đựng được nhưng việc đến bệnh viện để điều trị cũng không dễ dàng. Đó là bởi tôi cảm thấy ái ngại khi trong thời gian làm việc ai cũng bận rộn mà tôi lại đến bệnh viện. Mấy ngày trước, tôi đau đầu đến mức định nói rằng mình sẽ đi bệnh viện rồi về, nhưng sau khi nghe lời của trưởng phòng, tôi chỉ biết <u>ngậm miệng lại</u>. Trưởng phòng nhìn đồng hồ một cái rồi nói, "Ôi trời, hôm nay nhiều việc quá nên không có thời gian uống cà phê. Nào, chúng ta hãy uống cà phê một chút rồi lại làm việc chăm chỉ nhé. Phải vậy thì mới kết thúc được trước khi tan làm." Trong tương lai nếu muốn làm việc tốt thì dù sao tôi cũng phải luyện tập nhiều hơn để chịu đựng cơn đau đầu.
g_text_translate.en: I am an office worker working for a trading company. I have a habit of getting severe headaches when I am very tense or stressed. Sometimes it happens at work, but it's not easy to go to the hospital and get treatment even though I have an unbearable head ache. This is because everyone is wary of going to the hospital during busy working hours. A few days ago, the headache was so bad that I was going to go to the hospital, but when I heard the manager's words, I just <u>shut my mouth</u>. The manager glanced at his watch and said, "Oh, I didn't have time for a cup of coffee today because I have so much work to do. Now, let's just have a cup of coffee and work hard again. Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.
g_text_translate.it:
Sono un impiegato e lavoro per una società commerciale. Ho l'abitudine di avere forti mal di testa quando sono molto teso o stressato. A volte succede al lavoro, ma non è facile andare in ospedale e farmi curare anche se ho un mal di testa insopportabile. Questo perché tutti sono cauti nell'andare in ospedale durante gli orari di lavoro intensi. Qualche giorno fa il mal di testa era così forte che stavo per andare in ospedale, ma quando ho sentito le parole del direttore, ho <u>chiuso la bocca</u>. Il manager guardò l'orologio e disse: "Oh, non ho avuto tempo per una tazza di caffè oggi perché ho così tanto lavoro da fare. Ora prendiamoci solo una tazza di caffè e lavoriamo sodo di nuovo. Poi possiamo finire prima di lasciare l'ufficio." In futuro, se voglio fare bene al lavoro, penso che dovrò esercitarmi a sopportare di più il mal di testa.

#######

I am an office worker working for a trading company. I have a habit of getting severe headaches when I am very tense or stressed. Sometimes it happens at work, but it's not easy to go to the hospital and get treatment even though I have an unbearable head ache. This is because everyone is wary of going to the hospital during busy working hours. A few days ago, the headache was so bad that I was going to go to the hospital, but when I heard the manager's words, I just <u>shut my mouth</u>. The manager glanced at his watch and said, "Oh, I didn't have time for a cup of coffee today because I have so much work to do. Now, let's just have a cup of coffee and work hard again. Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.
g_text_translate.fr:
Je suis un employé de bureau travaillant pour une société commerciale. J'ai l'habitude d'avoir de graves maux de tête lorsque je suis très tendu ou stressé. Parfois, cela arrive au travail, mais ce n'est pas facile d'aller à l'hôpital et de se faire soigner même si j'ai un mal de tête insupportable. En effet, tout le monde hésite à se rendre à l’hôpital pendant les heures de travail chargées. Il y a quelques jours, le mal de tête était si grave que j'allais aller à l'hôpital, mais quand j'ai entendu les paroles du directeur, j'ai <u>fermé la bouche</u>. Le manager a jeté un coup d'œil à sa montre et a dit : « Oh, je n'ai pas eu le temps de prendre une tasse de café aujourd'hui parce que j'ai tellement de travail à faire. Maintenant, prenons simplement une tasse de café et travaillons dur à nouveau. Ensuite, nous pourrons terminer avant de quitter le bureau. À l’avenir, si je veux réussir au travail, je pense que je devrai m’entraîner davantage à supporter les maux de tête.

g_text_translate.es:
Soy un oficinista que trabaja para una empresa comercial. Tengo la costumbre de sufrir fuertes dolores de cabeza cuando estoy muy tenso o estresado. A veces sucede en el trabajo, pero no es fácil ir al hospital y recibir tratamiento a pesar de que tengo un dolor de cabeza insoportable. Esto se debe a que todo el mundo desconfía de ir al hospital durante las horas de trabajo ocupadas. Hace unos días, el dolor de cabeza era tan fuerte que iba a ir al hospital, pero cuando escuché las palabras del gerente, simplemente <u>cerré la boca</u>. El gerente miró su reloj y dijo: "Oh, hoy no tuve tiempo para tomar una taza de café porque tengo mucho trabajo que hacer. Ahora, tomemos una taza de café y trabajemos duro otra vez. Entonces podremos terminar antes de salir de la oficina." En el futuro, si quiero que me vaya bien en el trabajo, creo que tendré que practicar más para soportar los dolores de cabeza.

g_text_translate.id:
Saya seorang pekerja kantoran yang bekerja di sebuah perusahaan dagang. Saya mempunyai kebiasaan mengalami sakit kepala parah ketika saya sangat tegang atau stres. Kadang terjadi di tempat kerja, namun tidak mudah untuk pergi ke rumah sakit dan berobat meski saya mengalami sakit kepala yang tak tertahankan. Pasalnya, semua orang enggan pergi ke rumah sakit pada jam kerja sibuk. Beberapa hari yang lalu, sakit kepala saya sangat parah sehingga saya hendak pergi ke rumah sakit, tetapi ketika saya mendengar kata-kata manajer, saya hanya <u>menutup mulut</u>. Manajer melirik arlojinya dan berkata, "Oh, saya tidak punya waktu untuk minum kopi hari ini karena ada banyak pekerjaan yang harus saya lakukan. Sekarang, mari kita minum kopi dan bekerja keras lagi. Lalu kita bisa selesaikan sebelum meninggalkan kantor." Di masa depan, jika aku ingin bekerja dengan baik, aku rasa aku perlu lebih banyak berlatih menahan sakit kepala.

g_text_translate.ja:
私は商社に勤める会社員です。私は、非常に緊張したりストレスを感じたりすると、ひどい頭痛が起こる癖があります。仕事でも時々ありますが、耐えられないほどの頭の痛みがあるのに病院に行って治療を受けるのは容易ではありません。勤務時間の忙しい時間帯に病院に行くのは誰もが警戒するからです。数日前、頭痛がひどくて病院に行こうとしたのですが、 店長の言葉を聞いてつい<u>口をつぐんで</u>しまいました。マネージャーは時計をちらっと見て、「ああ、今日は仕事がたくさんあるのでコーヒーを飲む時間がありませんでした。さて、コーヒーを一杯飲んで、また一生懸命仕事しましょう。そうすれば大丈夫です」オフィスを出る前に終わらせてください。」将来、仕事でうまくいきたいなら、頭痛を我慢する練習をもっとする必要があると思います。

g_text_translate.de:
Ich bin Büroangestellter und arbeite für ein Handelsunternehmen. Ich habe die Angewohnheit, starke Kopfschmerzen zu bekommen, wenn ich sehr angespannt oder gestresst bin. Manchmal passiert es bei der Arbeit, aber es ist nicht einfach, ins Krankenhaus zu gehen und sich behandeln zu lassen, obwohl ich unerträgliche Kopfschmerzen habe. Dies liegt daran, dass jeder davor zurückschreckt, während der Arbeitszeit ins Krankenhaus zu gehen. Vor ein paar Tagen waren die Kopfschmerzen so schlimm, dass ich ins Krankenhaus gehen wollte, aber als ich die Worte des Managers hörte, <u>hielt ich einfach den Mund</u>. Der Manager warf einen Blick auf seine Uhr und sagte: „Oh, ich hatte heute keine Zeit für eine Tasse Kaffee, weil ich so viel zu tun habe. Jetzt lasst uns einfach eine Tasse Kaffee trinken und wieder hart arbeiten. Dann können wir.“ beenden, bevor Sie das Büro verlassen. Wenn ich in Zukunft bei der Arbeit gut abschneiden will, muss ich meiner Meinung nach mehr üben, mit Kopfschmerzen umzugehen.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我是一名在贸易公司工作的办公室职员。我有一个习惯，当我非常紧张或有压力时，就会感到严重头痛。有时在工作中也会出现这种情况，但即使头痛难忍，也不容易去医院治疗。这是因为大家对于在繁忙的工作时间去医院都持谨慎态度。前几天头疼得很厉害，本来想去医院，但听到经理的话，我就<u>闭嘴了</u>。经理看了一眼手表，说道：“哦，今天有很多工作要做，所以没时间喝咖啡。现在，我们喝杯咖啡，再努力工作吧，然后我们就可以了。”在离开办公室之前完成。”今后，如果我想做好工作，我想我需要多练习忍受头痛。

我是一名在貿易公司工作的辦公室職員。我有一個習慣，當我非常緊張或有壓力時，就會感到嚴重頭痛。有時在工作中也會出現這種情況，但即使頭痛難忍，也不容易去醫院治療。這是因為大家對於在繁忙的工作時間去醫院都持謹慎態度。前幾天頭疼得很厲害，本來想去醫院，但聽到經理的話，我就<u>閉嘴了</u>。經理看了一眼手錶，說道：“哦，今天有很多工作要做，所以沒時間喝咖啡。現在，我們喝杯咖啡，再努力工作吧，然後我們就可以了。”在離開辦公室之前完成。”今後，如果我想做好工作，我想我需要多練習忍受頭痛。

g_text_translate.ru:
Я офисный работник, работаю в торговой компании. У меня есть привычка испытывать сильные головные боли, когда я очень напряжен или нахожусь в стрессе. Иногда такое случается на работе, но пойти в больницу и подлечиться непросто, хотя у меня невыносимо болит голова. Это потому, что все опасаются идти в больницу в напряженное рабочее время. Несколько дней назад головная боль была настолько сильной, что я собирался ехать в больницу, но когда услышал слова менеджера, просто <u>закрыл рот</u>. Менеджер взглянул на часы и сказал: «О, у меня сегодня не было времени на чашку кофе, потому что у меня так много работы. Теперь давайте просто выпьем чашку кофе и снова поработаем. Тогда мы сможем закончить, прежде чем покинуть офис». В будущем, если я хочу преуспевать на работе, я думаю, мне нужно больше практиковаться в борьбе с головными болями.

g_text_translate.th:
ฉันเป็นพนักงานออฟฟิศที่ทำงานให้กับบริษัทการค้า ฉันมีนิสัยชอบปวดหัวอย่างรุนแรงเวลาเครียดหรือเครียดมาก บางทีก็เกิดขึ้นที่ทำงานแต่การไปโรงพยาบาลเพื่อรับการรักษาไม่ใช่เรื่องง่ายแม้ว่าฉันจะปวดหัวจนทนไม่ไหวก็ตาม เนื่องจากทุกคนระมัดระวังการไปโรงพยาบาลในช่วงเวลาทำงานที่ยุ่งวุ่นวาย เมื่อไม่กี่วันก่อน ปวดหัวหนักมากจนต้องไปโรงพยาบาล แต่พอได้ยินผู้จัดการพูด ฉันก็<u>ปิดปาก</u>เลย ผู้จัดการเหลือบมองนาฬิกาแล้วพูดว่า "โอ้ วันนี้ฉันไม่มีเวลาดื่มกาแฟสักแก้วเพราะฉันมีงานต้องทำมากมาย เอาล่ะ เราขอแค่ดื่มกาแฟสักแก้วแล้วทำงานหนักอีกครั้ง แล้วเราจะได้ ให้เสร็จก่อนออกจากออฟฟิศ” ในอนาคตถ้าอยากทำงานให้ออกมาดี ฉันคิดว่าต้องฝึกฝนเรื่องปวดหัวให้มากขึ้น

Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.
g_text_translate.pt:
Sou um trabalhador de escritório que trabalha para uma empresa comercial. Tenho o hábito de sentir fortes dores de cabeça quando estou muito tenso ou estressado. Às vezes acontece no trabalho, mas não é fácil ir ao hospital e fazer tratamento mesmo tendo uma dor de cabeça insuportável. Isso ocorre porque todos têm receio de ir ao hospital durante horários de trabalho intensos. Há alguns dias, a dor de cabeça era tão forte que eu ia para o hospital, mas quando ouvi as palavras do gerente, simplesmente <u>fechei a boca</u>. O gerente olhou para o relógio e disse: "Ah, não tive tempo para tomar uma xícara de café hoje porque tenho muito trabalho a fazer. Agora, vamos tomar uma xícara de café e trabalhar duro novamente. Então poderemos termine antes de sair do escritório." No futuro, se eu quiser ter um bom desempenho no trabalho, acho que preciso praticar mais para suportar dores de cabeça.

#######

I am an office worker working for a trading company. I have a habit of getting severe headaches when I am very tense or stressed. Sometimes it happens at work, but it's not easy to go to the hospital and get treatment even though I have an unbearable head ache. This is because everyone is wary of going to the hospital during busy working hours. A few days ago, the headache was so bad that I was going to go to the hospital, but when I heard the manager's words, I just <u>shut my mouth</u>. The manager glanced at his watch and said, "Oh, I didn't have time for a cup of coffee today because I have so much work to do. Now, let's just have a cup of coffee and work hard again. Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.
g_text_translate.hi:
मैं एक ट्रेडिंग कंपनी में काम करने वाला एक कार्यालय कर्मचारी हूं। मेरी आदत है कि जब मैं बहुत तनावग्रस्त या परेशान होता हूँ तो मुझे तेज़ सिरदर्द होने लगता है। कभी-कभी ऐसा काम पर होता है, लेकिन सिर में असहनीय दर्द होने पर भी अस्पताल जाना और इलाज कराना आसान नहीं है। ऐसा इसलिए है क्योंकि हर कोई व्यस्त कामकाजी घंटों के दौरान अस्पताल जाने से सावधान रहता है। कुछ दिन पहले, सिरदर्द इतना तेज़ था कि मैं अस्पताल जाने वाला था, लेकिन जब मैंने मैनेजर की बातें सुनीं, तो मैंने अपना मुँह बंद कर लिया। प्रबंधक ने अपनी घड़ी की ओर देखा और कहा, "ओह, मेरे पास आज एक कप कॉफी पीने का समय नहीं था क्योंकि मुझे बहुत काम करना है। अब, चलो बस एक कप कॉफी पीते हैं और फिर से कड़ी मेहनत करते हैं। फिर हम कर सकते हैं कार्यालय छोड़ने से पहले समाप्त करें।" भविष्य में, अगर मैं काम में अच्छा प्रदर्शन करना चाहता हूं, तो मुझे लगता है कि मुझे सिरदर्द सहने का और अधिक अभ्यास करना होगा।

#######

I am an office worker working for a trading company. I have a habit of getting severe headaches when I am very tense or stressed. Sometimes it happens at work, but it's not easy to go to the hospital and get treatment even though I have an unbearable head ache. This is because everyone is wary of going to the hospital during busy working hours. A few days ago, the headache was so bad that I was going to go to the hospital, but when I heard the manager's words, I just <u>shut my mouth</u>. The manager glanced at his watch and said, "Oh, I didn't have time for a cup of coffee today because I have so much work to do. Now, let's just have a cup of coffee and work hard again. Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bir ticaret şirketinde çalışan bir ofis çalışanıyım. Çok gergin veya stresli olduğumda şiddetli baş ağrısı çekme alışkanlığım var. Bazen iş yerinde de oluyor ama dayanılmaz baş ağrım olmasına rağmen hastaneye gidip tedavi olmak hiç de kolay olmuyor. Çünkü herkes yoğun iş saatlerinde hastaneye gitmekten çekiniyor. Birkaç gün önce baş ağrısı o kadar kötüydü ki hastaneye gidecektim ama müdürün sözlerini duyunca <u>çenemi kapattım</u>. Müdür saatine baktı ve şöyle dedi: "Ah, bugün bir fincan kahve içmeye vaktim olmadı çünkü yapacak çok işim var. Şimdi bir fincan kahve içelim ve yeniden sıkı çalışalım. O zaman yapabiliriz ofisten ayrılmadan önce bitirin." Gelecekte işimde başarılı olmak istiyorsam baş ağrısına daha fazla katlanma pratiği yapmam gerektiğini düşünüyorum.

#######

I am an office worker working for a trading company. I have a habit of getting severe headaches when I am very tense or stressed. Sometimes it happens at work, but it's not easy to go to the hospital and get treatment even though I have an unbearable head ache. This is because everyone is wary of going to the hospital during busy working hours. A few days ago, the headache was so bad that I was going to go to the hospital, but when I heard the manager's words, I just <u>shut my mouth</u>. The manager glanced at his watch and said, "Oh, I didn't have time for a cup of coffee today because I have so much work to do. Now, let's just have a cup of coffee and work hard again. Then we can finish before leaving the office." In the future, if I want to do well at work, I think I need to practice putting up with headaches more.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 업무 중에는 머리가 아파도 제때 병원에 갈 수 없다.
나는 항상 회사 일 때문에 스트레스를 받는다.
업무 시간에 병원에 다녀오면 동료들보다 늦게 퇴근해야 한다.
보통 커피를 한 잔 마시고 나면 두통이 사라진다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Trong lúc làm việc, dù tôi có đau đầu cũng không thể đến bệnh viện kịp thời. 
2. Tôi luôn bị căng thẳng vì công việc của công ty.
3. Nếu đi bệnh viện trong giờ làm việc thì phải tan làm muộn hơn đồng nghiệp.
4. Thông thường sau khi tôi uống một cốc cà phê, cơn đau đầu sẽ biến mất.
--------------------
너 나 할 것 없이: Bất kể là ai

Nhân vật 'tôi' ái ngại về việc mình đi bệnh viện trong thời gian làm việc, lúc mọi người đều bận rộn. Do đó 1 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:

2. I am always stressed out because of my work.
3. If I go to the hospital during work hours, I have to leave work later than my colleagues.
4. Usually, after drinking a cup of coffee, the headache goes away.
--------------------
<g>너 나 할 것 없이:</g> Everyone

This person is embarrassed about going to the hospital during work, when everyone is busy. So 1 is the correct answer.

3. Jika saya pergi ke rumah sakit pada jam kerja, saya harus pulang kerja lebih lambat dari rekan kerja saya.
4. Biasanya setelah minum secangkir kopi, sakit kepala hilang.
--------------------
<g>너 나 할 것 없이:</g>Setiap orang

Orang ini malu pergi ke rumah sakit saat bekerja, ketika semua orang sibuk. Jadi 1 adalah jawaban yang benar.

general.g_text: 텔레비전에서 그림자극을 보니 어린 시절 아빠와 함께 그림자놀이를 하던 기억이 떠올랐다. 손 모양을 바꿔 가며 귀여운 토끼도 만들고, 오리도 만들었던 생각이 났다. 또 <u>그림자놀이와 함께 아빠가 들려주시던 옛날이야기도 새록새록 떠올랐다</u>. 침대에 누워서 아빠가 들려주시는 이야기를 듣다 보면 나는 어느새 아름다운 꿈의 세계로 빠져들곤 했다. 하지만 지금은 이런 놀이에 흥미가 전혀 없다. 훨씬 더 재미있는 컴퓨터 게임이 있기 때문이다. 생각해 보면 내가 컴퓨터 게임을 하면서부터 아빠와 점점 멀어지게 된 것 같다. 오늘은 아빠와 함께 그림자놀이를 해 보고 싶다. 그러면 아빠와 다시 가까워질 수 있고, 옛 추억도 되살릴 수 있을 것만 같다.

g_text_translate.vi: Khi tôi xem kịch sử dụng bóng người để diễn trên TV, tôi nhớ mình đã chơi trò chơi tạo hình với bóng với bố khi còn nhỏ. Nó khiến tôi nhớ đến việc làm những chú thỏ và vịt dễ thương bằng cách thay đổi hình dạng của bàn tay. Ngoài ra, <u>câu chuyện cổ tích mà bố tôi kể cho tôi cùng với trò chơi tạo hình với bóng ùa về trong tâm trí tôi</u>. Tôi đã từng hay nằm trên giường và nghe những câu chuyện mà bố tôi kể, và rồi bỗng nhiên rơi vào một thế giới mộng mơ đẹp đẽ. Nhưng hiện tại, tôi không có hứng thú với loại trò chơi này chút nào. Bởi vì có nhiều trò chơi máy tính thú vị hơn nhiều. Nghĩ lại thì có vẻ như tôi ngày càng trở nên xa cách với bố tôi kể từ khi tôi bắt đầu chơi trò chơi trên máy tính. Hôm nay, tôi muốn chơi trò chơi tạo hình với bóng với bố tôi. Nếu làm vậy, tôi nghĩ tôi sẽ lại trở nên gần gũi với bố, và sẽ có thể mang những kỷ niệm xưa về.
g_text_translate.en: Watching shadow plays on television reminded me of my childhood playing shadow with my father. I remembered making cute rabbits and ducks by changing the shape of hands. <u>Along with the shadow play, the old story that my father told me came to mind</u>. When I was lying in bed and listening to my dad's story, I used to fall into the world of beautiful dreams. But now I'm not interested in this kind of play at all. This is because there is a much more interesting games. Come to think of it, I think I've become more distant from my dad since I played computer games. I want to play shadow with my dad today. Then I can get closer to my father again, and I think I can bring back old memories.
g_text_translate.it:
Guardare i giochi d'ombre in televisione mi ha ricordato la mia infanzia quando giocavo con le ombre con mio padre. Mi sono ricordato di aver creato graziosi conigli e anatre cambiando la forma delle mani. <u>Insieme al gioco delle ombre, mi è venuta in mente la vecchia storia che mi raccontava mio padre</u>. Quando ero a letto e ascoltavo la storia di mio padre, cadevo nel mondo dei sogni meravigliosi. Ma ora non sono più interessato a questo tipo di gioco. Questo perché ci sono giochi molto più interessanti. Ora che ci penso, penso di essere diventato più distante da mio padre da quando gioco ai videogiochi. Voglio fare l'ombra con mio padre oggi. Allora potrò avvicinarmi di nuovo a mio padre e penso di poter riportare alla mente vecchi ricordi.

#######

g_text_translate.fr:
Regarder des jeux d'ombres à la télévision m'a rappelé mon enfance en jouant avec mon père. Je me souviens avoir créé de jolis lapins et canards en changeant la forme des mains. <u>En plus du jeu d'ombres, la vieille histoire que mon père m'a racontée m'est venue à l'esprit</u>. Quand j'étais allongé dans mon lit et que j'écoutais l'histoire de mon père, je tombais dans le monde des beaux rêves. Mais maintenant, ce genre de pièce ne m'intéresse plus du tout. C'est parce qu'il existe des jeux beaucoup plus intéressants. À bien y penser, je pense que je me suis éloigné de mon père depuis que je joue à des jeux vidéo. Je veux jouer à l'ombre avec mon père aujourd'hui. Ensuite, je pourrai me rapprocher à nouveau de mon père et je pense que je pourrai ramener de vieux souvenirs.

g_text_translate.es:
Ver juegos de sombras en la televisión me recordó mi infancia jugando a las sombras con mi padre. Recordé haber hecho lindos conejos y patos cambiando la forma de las manos. <u>Junto con el juego de sombras, me vino a la mente la vieja historia que me contó mi padre</u>. Cuando estaba acostada en la cama escuchando la historia de mi papá, solía caer en el mundo de los hermosos sueños. Pero ahora no me interesa en absoluto este tipo de juego. Esto se debe a que hay juegos mucho más interesantes. Ahora que lo pienso, creo que me he distanciado más de mi padre desde que juego juegos de computadora. Hoy quiero jugar a la sombra con mi papá. Entonces podré volver a acercarme a mi padre y creo que podré recuperar viejos recuerdos.

g_text_translate.id:
Menonton wayang kulit di televisi mengingatkan saya pada masa kecil saya bermain wayang kulit bersama ayah saya. Saya ingat membuat kelinci dan bebek lucu dengan mengubah bentuk tangan. <u>Bersamaan dengan wayang kulit, kisah lama yang diceritakan ayahku muncul di benakku</u>. Ketika saya sedang berbaring di tempat tidur dan mendengarkan cerita ayah saya, saya sering jatuh ke dalam dunia mimpi indah. Tapi sekarang saya sama sekali tidak tertarik dengan permainan seperti ini. Ini karena ada permainan yang jauh lebih menarik. Kalau dipikir-pikir, sepertinya aku menjadi semakin jauh dari ayahku sejak aku bermain game komputer. Saya ingin bermain bayangan dengan ayah saya hari ini. Lalu aku bisa lebih dekat dengan ayahku lagi, dan kupikir aku bisa membawa kembali kenangan lama.

g_text_translate.ja:
テレビで影絵劇を観ていると、幼少期に父と一緒に影絵遊びをしたことを思い出しました。手の形を変えて可愛いウサギやアヒルを作ったのを思い出しました。その<u>影絵劇とともに、 父が話してくれた昔話が思い出されました</u>。ベッドに横になって父の話を聞いていると、よく美しい夢の世界に迷い込んでいました。でも今はそういう遊びには全く興味がありません。もっと面白いゲームがあるからです。そういえば、パソコンゲームをしてから父との距離が遠くなったような気がします。今日はお父さんとシャドーをしたいです。そうすればまた父に近づけるし、昔の思い出も甦ると思います。

g_text_translate.de:
Das Anschauen von Schattenspielen im Fernsehen erinnerte mich an meine Kindheit, als ich mit meinem Vater Schatten spielte. Ich erinnere mich, dass ich niedliche Kaninchen und Enten gemacht habe, indem ich die Form der Hände verändert habe. <u>Zusammen mit dem Schattenspiel kam mir die alte Geschichte in den Sinn, die mir mein Vater erzählt hatte</u>. Wenn ich im Bett lag und die Geschichte meines Vaters hörte, tauchte ich in die Welt der schönen Träume ein. Aber jetzt interessiere ich mich überhaupt nicht mehr für diese Art von Theaterstücken. Dies liegt daran, dass es viel interessantere Spiele gibt. Wenn ich darüber nachdenke, glaube ich, dass ich mich von meinem Vater distanziert habe, seit ich Computerspiele gespielt habe. Ich möchte heute mit meinem Vater Schatten spielen. Dann kann ich meinem Vater wieder näher kommen und denke, dass ich alte Erinnerungen zurückbringen kann.

#######

Watching shadow plays on television reminded me of my childhood playing shadow with my father. I remembered making cute rabbits and ducks by changing the shape of hands. <u>Along with the shadow play, the old story that my father told me came to mind</u>. When I was lying in bed and listening to my dad's story, I used to fall into the world of beautiful dreams. But now I'm not interested in this kind of play at all. This is because there is a much more interesting games. Come to think of it, I think I've become more distant from my dad since I played computer games. I want to play shadow with my dad today. Then I can get closer to my father again, and I think I can bring back old memories.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在电视上看皮影戏让我想起了小时候和父亲一起玩皮影的情景。我记得通过改变手的形状来制作可爱的兔子和鸭子。 <u>伴随着皮影戏，我的脑海里浮现出父亲给我讲的那个老故事</u>。当我躺在床上听着爸爸的故事时，我常常陷入美丽的梦的世界。但现在我对这种玩法完全不感兴趣了。这是因为还有更有趣的游戏。想想看，自从玩电脑游戏以来，我和爸爸的距离越来越远了。今天我想和爸爸一起玩影子游戏。这样我就可以再次和父亲更加亲近，我想我可以找回以前的记忆。

Когда я лежал в постели и слушал рассказ отца, я погружался в мир прекрасных снов. Но сейчас мне вообще не интересна такая игра. Это потому, что есть гораздо более интересные игры. Если подумать, мне кажется, что я стал еще дальше отдаляться от отца с тех пор, как начал играть в компьютерные игры. Сегодня я хочу поиграть в тень со своим отцом. Тогда я снова смогу стать ближе к отцу и, думаю, смогу вернуть старые воспоминания.

g_text_translate.th:
การได้ดูละครเงาในโทรทัศน์ทำให้ฉันนึกถึงสมัยเด็กๆ ที่ได้เล่นเงากับพ่อ ฉันจำได้ว่าทำกระต่ายและเป็ดน่ารักโดยการเปลี่ยนรูปร่างของมือ <u>นอกจากการเล่นเงาแล้ว เรื่องเก่าๆ ที่พ่อเล่าให้ฟังก็เข้ามาในความคิด</u> ตอนที่ฉันนอนฟังเรื่องราวของพ่อ ฉันเคยตกอยู่ในโลกแห่งความฝันที่สวยงาม แต่ตอนนี้ฉันไม่สนใจการเล่นแบบนี้เลย เนื่องจากมีเกมที่น่าสนใจอีกมากมาย ลองคิดดูสิ ฉันคิดว่าฉันห่างเหินจากพ่อมากขึ้นตั้งแต่ฉันเล่นเกมคอมพิวเตอร์ วันนี้ฉันอยากเล่นเงากับพ่อ จากนั้นฉันก็จะได้ใกล้ชิดกับพ่อมากขึ้นอีกครั้ง และฉันคิดว่าฉันสามารถรื้อฟื้นความทรงจำเก่าๆ ได้

g_text_translate.pt:
Assistir jogos de sombras na televisão me lembrou da minha infância brincando de sombras com meu pai. Lembrei-me de fazer coelhos e patos fofos mudando o formato das mãos. <u>Junto com o jogo de sombras, veio à mente a velha história que meu pai me contou</u>. Quando eu estava deitado na cama ouvindo a história do meu pai, costumava cair no mundo dos lindos sonhos. Mas agora não estou nem um pouco interessado nesse tipo de peça. Isso ocorre porque há jogos muito mais interessantes. Pensando bem, acho que fiquei mais distante do meu pai desde que joguei no computador. Quero brincar de sombra com meu pai hoje. Então poderei me aproximar de meu pai novamente e acho que poderei trazer de volta velhas lembranças.

g_text_translate.hi:
टेलीविजन पर छाया नाटक देखकर मुझे बचपन में अपने पिता के साथ छाया खेल की याद आ गई। मुझे हाथों का आकार बदल कर प्यारे खरगोश और बत्तखें बनाने की याद आई। <u>छाया नाटक के साथ-साथ, वह पुरानी कहानी याद आ गई जो मेरे पिता ने मुझे सुनाई थी</u>। जब मैं बिस्तर पर लेटा हुआ अपने पिता की कहानी सुन रहा होता था, तो मैं खूबसूरत सपनों की दुनिया में खो जाता था। लेकिन अब मुझे इस तरह के खेल में बिल्कुल भी दिलचस्पी नहीं है. ऐसा इसलिए है क्योंकि यहां बहुत अधिक दिलचस्प गेम हैं। इसके बारे में सोचें, मुझे लगता है कि जब से मैंने कंप्यूटर गेम खेला है तब से मैं अपने पिता से और अधिक दूर हो गया हूं। मैं आज अपने पिता के साथ छाया खेलना चाहता हूं। तब मैं फिर से अपने पिता के करीब आ सकता हूं और मुझे लगता है कि मैं पुरानी यादें वापस ला सकता हूं।

g_text_translate.tr:
Televizyonda gölge oyunları izlemek bana çocukluğumda babamla gölge oynadığım günleri hatırlattı. Ellerin şeklini değiştirerek sevimli tavşanlar ve ördekler yaptığımı hatırladım. <u>Gölge oyunuyla birlikte aklıma babamın anlattığı eski hikaye geldi</u>. Yatakta uzanıp babamın hikâyesini dinlerken güzel rüyalar dünyasına dalardım. Ama artık bu tür oyunlarla hiç ilgilenmiyorum. Çünkü çok daha ilginç oyunlar var. Düşününce bilgisayar oyunları oynadığımdan beri babamdan daha da uzaklaştığımı düşünüyorum. Bugün babamla gölge oynamak istiyorum. O zaman babama tekrar yakınlaşabilirim ve eski anıları canlandırabileceğimi düşünüyorum.

#######

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 지금은 그림자놀이보다 컴퓨터 게임에 빠져 있다.
어렸을 때 그림자놀이를 하다가 잠이 들곤 했다.
컴퓨터 게임을 통해 아빠와 내가 가까워질 수 있었다.
지금도 토끼와 오리를 만드는 그림자놀이를 한다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Bây giờ tôi thích chơi game máy tính hơn là chơi tạo hình với bóng.
2. Khi còn nhỏ, tôi thường ngủ trong khi đang chơi trò chơi tạo hình với bóng.
3. Thông qua trò chơi máy tính, bố và tôi đã có thể gần gũi nhau hơn.
4. Ngay cả bây giờ tôi vẫn chơi trò chơi tạo hình với bóng làm thỏ và vịt.
--------------------
Hiện tại, nhân vật 'tôi' đã không còn hứng thú với trò chơi tạo hình với bóng, lý do là vì có nhiều trò chơi máy tính thú vị hơn. 1 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. I am more into computer games than playing with shadows.
2. When I was young, I used to fall asleep while playing with shadows.
3. Computer games brought my father and I closer.
4. I still do shadow play making rabbits and ducks.
--------------------
Currently, this person is no longer interested in playing with shadows, the reason is that there are more interesting computer games. 1 is the correct answer.
