g_text_translate.ja:
地元の先輩の勧めで「銭湯の垢すり」の仕事を始めることになった。最初はすぐに食べていくのが不安だったので、数年後に辞めるつもりでした。しかし、実際にやってみると、なかなか魅力的な仕事でした。何より、汗をかきながら働くことが私の適性に合っていました。ところがある日、息子に学校から家庭環境調査が届きました。私はその子の顔を覚えていたので<u>親の職業欄に「銭湯のアカスリ」 と一瞬躊躇して書けなかった</u>のです。その日から私は10年間貯めたお金でレストランを開きました。しかし、未経験で開業した飲食店がうまくいくはずはありません。結局、レストランは多額の借金のため1年で閉店しました。一番得意な仕事を見つけた私は銭湯に戻った。私は人々の体を掃除するアーティストとして戻ってきました。

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. Above all, working with sweat suited my aptitude well. But one day, my son received a home environment survey from school. I remembered the child's face, <u>so I hesitated for a moment and could not write 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse' in the parent's occupation column</u>. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. In the end, the restaurant was closed after a year due to heavy debt. I, who had found the job that I do best, returned to the bathhouse. I'm back as an artist cleaning people's bodies.
g_text_translate.de:
Auf Empfehlung eines Seniors in meiner Heimatstadt begann ich als „Körperwäscher in einem öffentlichen Badehaus“ zu arbeiten. Zuerst machte ich mir Sorgen, dass ich sofort meinen Lebensunterhalt verdienen könnte, also wollte ich nach ein paar Jahren aufhören. Als ich es jedoch tatsächlich tat, war es ein ziemlich attraktiver Job. Vor allem die Arbeit mit Schweiß passte gut zu meinen Fähigkeiten. Aber eines Tages erhielt mein Sohn von der Schule eine Umfrage zum häuslichen Umfeld. Ich erinnerte mich an das Gesicht des Kindes, <u>also zögerte ich einen Moment und konnte nicht „Körperwäscher in einem öffentlichen Badehaus“ in die Berufsspalte der Eltern schreiben</u>. Nach diesem Tag eröffnete ich mit dem Geld, das ich 10 Jahre lang gespart hatte, ein Restaurant. Allerdings hätte die Eröffnung eines Restaurants ohne Erfahrung nicht gut funktionieren können. Letztendlich wurde das Restaurant wegen hoher Schulden nach einem Jahr geschlossen. Ich, der den Job gefunden hatte, den ich am besten kann, kehrte ins Badehaus zurück. Ich bin als Künstler zurück und reinige die Körper von Menschen.

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在家乡一位前辈的推荐下，我开始了“公共澡堂擦身工”的工作。起初，我担心立即谋生，所以打算几年后退出。然而，当我真正去做的时候，这是一份非常有吸引力的工作。最重要的是，流汗工作很适合我的才能。但有一天，儿子收到了学校发来的一份家庭环境调查报告。我记住了孩子的脸，<u>所以犹豫了一下，没能在家长的职业栏里写上“公共澡堂擦身”</u>。那天之后，我用10年的积蓄开了一家餐馆。然而，没有经验的餐厅是不可能开得好的。最终，餐厅在一年后因负债累累而关门。找到了自己最擅长的工作的我又回到了澡堂。我作为一名清洁人们身体的艺术家回来了。

g_text_translate.ru:
По рекомендации старшего человека из моего родного города я начал работать «мойщиком в общественной бане». Сначала я беспокоился о том, как сразу зарабатывать на жизнь, поэтому планировал уйти через несколько лет. Однако, когда я действительно этим занимался, это была довольно привлекательная работа. Прежде всего, работа с потом хорошо соответствовала моим способностям. Но однажды мой сын получил из школы опрос об обстановке в доме. Я запомнила лицо ребенка, <u>поэтому на мгновение заколебалась и не смогла написать «мойка для тела в общественной бане» в графе родов деятельности родителя</u>. После того дня я открыл ресторан на деньги, которые копил за 10 лет. Однако ресторан, открытый без опыта, не мог бы работать хорошо. В итоге через год ресторан закрыли из-за больших долгов. Я, нашедший работу, которая у меня получается лучше всего, вернулся в баню. Я вернулся как художник, очищающий тела людей.

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. Above all, working with sweat suited my aptitude well. But one day, my son received a home environment survey from school. I remembered the child's face, <u>so I hesitated for a moment and could not write 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse' in the parent's occupation column</u>. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. In the end, the restaurant was closed after a year due to heavy debt. I, who had found the job that I do best, returned to the bathhouse. I'm back as an artist cleaning people's bodies.
g_text_translate.th:
ตามคำแนะนำของผู้อาวุโสในบ้านเกิดของฉัน ฉันเริ่มทำงานเป็น 'พนักงานขัดผิวในโรงอาบน้ำสาธารณะ' ตอนแรกฉันกังวลเรื่องการหาเลี้ยงชีพทันที เลยวางแผนลาออกหลังจากผ่านไปไม่กี่ปี อย่างไรก็ตาม เมื่อฉันทำจริง มันเป็นงานที่น่าสนใจทีเดียว เหนือสิ่งอื่นใด การทำงานโดยต้องเสียเหงื่อเหมาะกับความถนัดของฉันเป็นอย่างดี แต่วันหนึ่ง ลูกชายของฉันได้รับแบบสำรวจสภาพแวดล้อมภายในบ้านจากโรงเรียน ฉันจำใบหน้าของเด็กได้ <u>ฉันจึงลังเลอยู่ครู่หนึ่งและไม่สามารถเขียน 'คนขัดผิวในโรงอาบน้ำสาธารณะ' ลงในคอลัมน์อาชีพของผู้ปกครองได้</u> หลังจากวันนั้น ผมก็เปิดร้านอาหารด้วยเงินที่ผมเก็บมาได้ 10 ปี อย่างไรก็ตาม ร้านอาหารที่เปิดโดยไม่มีประสบการณ์ไม่สามารถทำงานได้ดี ในที่สุดร้านอาหารก็ปิดตัวลงหลังจากผ่านไปหนึ่งปีเนื่องจากมีหนี้จำนวนมาก ฉันที่ได้งานที่ฉันถนัดที่สุดก็กลับมาที่โรงอาบน้ำ ฉันกลับมาในฐานะศิลปินทำความสะอาดร่างกายผู้คน

#######

g_text_translate.pt:
Por recomendação de um veterano da minha cidade natal, comecei a trabalhar como “lavador corporal em uma casa de banho pública”. No início, eu estava preocupado em ganhar a vida imediatamente, então planejei desistir depois de alguns anos. No entanto, quando realmente fiz isso, foi um trabalho bastante atraente. Acima de tudo, trabalhar com suor combinava bem com minha aptidão. Mas um dia, meu filho recebeu da escola uma pesquisa sobre o ambiente doméstico. Lembrei-me do rosto da criança, <u>então hesitei por um momento e não consegui escrever 'lavador corporal em balneário público' na coluna de ocupação dos pais</u>. Depois daquele dia, abri um restaurante com o dinheiro que economizei durante 10 anos. No entanto, um restaurante aberto sem experiência não poderia ter funcionado bem. No final, o restaurante foi fechado depois de um ano devido a pesadas dívidas. Eu, que encontrei o trabalho que faço melhor, voltei para o balneário. Estou de volta como artista limpando o corpo das pessoas.

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. Above all, working with sweat suited my aptitude well. But one day, my son received a home environment survey from school. I remembered the child's face, <u>so I hesitated for a moment and could not write 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse' in the parent's occupation column</u>. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. In the end, the restaurant was closed after a year due to heavy debt. I, who had found the job that I do best, returned to the bathhouse. I'm back as an artist cleaning people's bodies.
g_text_translate.hi:
अपने गृहनगर में एक वरिष्ठ की सिफारिश पर, मैंने 'सार्वजनिक स्नानघर में बॉडी स्क्रबर' के रूप में काम करना शुरू कर दिया। सबसे पहले, मैं तुरंत आजीविका कमाने के बारे में चिंतित था, इसलिए मैंने कुछ वर्षों के बाद नौकरी छोड़ने की योजना बनाई। हालाँकि, जब मैंने वास्तव में इसे किया, तो यह काफी आकर्षक काम था। सबसे बढ़कर, पसीने के साथ काम करना मेरी योग्यता के अनुकूल था। लेकिन एक दिन, मेरे बेटे को स्कूल से घरेलू पर्यावरण सर्वेक्षण प्राप्त हुआ। मुझे बच्चे का चेहरा याद आ गया, <u>इसलिए मैं एक पल के लिए झिझका और माता-पिता के व्यवसाय वाले कॉलम में 'सार्वजनिक स्नानघर में बॉडी स्क्रबर' नहीं लिख सका</u>। उस दिन के बाद मैंने 10 साल तक बचाए पैसों से एक रेस्टोरेंट खोला। हालाँकि, बिना अनुभव के खोला गया रेस्तरां अच्छा काम नहीं कर सकता था। अंत में, भारी कर्ज के कारण एक साल बाद रेस्तरां बंद कर दिया गया। मैं, जिसे वह काम मिल गया था जो मैं सबसे अच्छा करता हूँ, स्नानागार में लौट आया। मैं लोगों के शरीर की सफाई करने वाले कलाकार के रूप में वापस आ गया हूं।

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. Above all, working with sweat suited my aptitude well. But one day, my son received a home environment survey from school. I remembered the child's face, <u>so I hesitated for a moment and could not write 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse' in the parent's occupation column</u>. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. In the end, the restaurant was closed after a year due to heavy debt. I, who had found the job that I do best, returned to the bathhouse. I'm back as an artist cleaning people's bodies.
g_text_translate.tr:
Memleketimdeki bir son sınıf arkadaşımın tavsiyesi üzerine 'halk hamamında vücut temizleyici' olarak çalışmaya başladım. İlk başta geçimimi hemen sağlama konusunda endişeliydim, bu yüzden birkaç yıl sonra bırakmayı planladım. Ancak bunu gerçekten yaptığımda oldukça çekici bir işti. Her şeyden önce terle çalışmak yeteneğime çok uygundu. Ama bir gün oğlum okuldan bir ev ortamı anketi aldı. Çocuğun yüzünü hatırladım, <u>bir an tereddüt ettim ve ebeveyn mesleği sütununa 'halka açık hamamda vücut temizleyicisi' yazamadım</u>. O günden sonra 10 yıl boyunca biriktirdiğim parayla restoran açtım. Ancak tecrübesiz açılan bir restoranın iyi sonuç vermesi mümkün değildi. Sonunda restoran ağır borç nedeniyle bir yıl sonra kapatıldı. En iyi yaptığım işi bulmuş olan ben, hamama döndüm. İnsanların vücutlarını temizleyen bir sanatçı olarak geri döndüm.

#######

At the recommendation of a senior in my hometown, I started working as a 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse'. At first, I was worried about making a living right away, so I planned to quit after a few years. However, when I actually did it, it was a pretty attractive job. Above all, working with sweat suited my aptitude well. But one day, my son received a home environment survey from school. I remembered the child's face, <u>so I hesitated for a moment and could not write 'body scrubber at a public bathhouse' in the parent's occupation column</u>. After that day, I opened a restaurant with the money I saved for 10 years. However, a restaurant opened without experience could not have worked well. In the end, the restaurant was closed after a year due to heavy debt. I, who had found the job that I do best, returned to the bathhouse. I'm back as an artist cleaning people's bodies.

general.g_text: 나는 거위 두 마리를 데려다 키웠다. 한 마리가 울면 다른 한 마리가 따라서 우는 것이 마치 무슨 이야기를 나누는 듯하고 물도 함께 마시고 모이도 함께 먹었다. 날마다 거위가 노는 것을 보는 것이 하나의 재미였는데 어느 날 밤에 그중 한 마리가 죽어 버렸다. 아침에 일어나 보니 살아 있는 거위가 죽은 거위를 품고서 날개를 치며 울고 있었다. 죽은 거위를 치우자 산 거위는 이리저리 왔다 갔다 움직이기 시작했다. 울음소리는 더욱 간절해지고 너무 울어 나중에는 소리도 제대로 내지 못하게 되었다. <u>나는 이 모습을 보면서 할 말을 잃었다</u>. 세상 사람들도 모두 너희와 같은 마음을 가지면 얼마나 좋을까?

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi đã đưa hai con ngỗng về nuôi. Một con kêu thì con kia kêu theo, giống như đang chia sẻ câu chuyện gì đó, chúng cùng uống nước và cùng ăn mồi. Mỗi ngày việc xem ngỗng chơi là một thú vui của tôi, nhưng một đêm nọ, một trong hai con đã chết. Buổi sáng tôi thức dậy thì thấy con ngỗng còn sống ôm con ngỗng đã chết, vỗ cánh và kêu. Khi tôi xử lý con ngỗng đã chết, con ngỗng còn sống bắt đầu di chuyển qua lại chỗ này chỗ kia. Tiếng kêu của nó càng trở nên thiết tha hơn và nó kêu quá nhiều nên sau này không thể kêu bình thường được nữa. <u>Nhìn bộ dạng đó, tôi không nói nên lời</u>. Nếu tất cả mọi người trên thế giới đều có trái tim giống với chúng thì tốt biết mấy?
g_text_translate.en: I took in two geese and raised them. When one cries, the other cries as if they are talking, drinking water and eating food together. It was a pleasure to watch the geese play every day, but one night one of them died. When I woke up in the morning, I saw the live goose holding the dead goose, flapping its wings and crying. When the dead goose was removed, the live goose started moving back and forth. The sound of crying became more desperate, and the live goose cried so much that it couldn't even make a sound properly afterwards. <u>I was at a loss for words when I saw this</u>. How nice it would be if all the people in the world had the same mind as them.
g_text_translate.it:
Ho preso due oche e le ho allevate. Quando uno piange, l'altro piange come se stessero parlando, bevendo acqua e mangiando cibo insieme. Era un piacere guardare le oche giocare ogni giorno, ma una notte una di loro morì. Quando mi sono svegliato la mattina, ho visto l'oca viva che teneva in braccio l'oca morta, sbattendo le ali e piangendo. Quando l'oca morta fu rimossa, l'oca viva cominciò a muoversi avanti e indietro. Il suono del pianto divenne più disperato e l'oca viva pianse così tanto che dopo non riuscì nemmeno a emettere un suono corretto. <u>Sono rimasto senza parole quando ho visto questo</u>. Quanto sarebbe bello se tutte le persone nel mondo avessero la loro stessa mente.

#######

I took in two geese and raised them. When one cries, the other cries as if they are talking, drinking water and eating food together. It was a pleasure to watch the geese play every day, but one night one of them died. When I woke up in the morning, I saw the live goose holding the dead goose, flapping its wings and crying. When the dead goose was removed, the live goose started moving back and forth. The sound of crying became more desperate, and the live goose cried so much that it couldn't even make a sound properly afterwards. <u>I was at a loss for words when I saw this</u>. How nice it would be if all the people in the world had the same mind as them.
g_text_translate.fr:
J'ai recueilli deux oies et je les ai élevées. Quand l’un pleure, l’autre pleure comme s’ils parlaient, buvaient de l’eau et mangeaient ensemble. C'était un plaisir de regarder les oies jouer tous les jours, mais une nuit, l'une d'elles est morte. Quand je me suis réveillé le matin, j'ai vu l'oie vivante tenant l'oie morte, battant des ailes et pleurant. Lorsque l’oie morte a été retirée, l’oie vivante a commencé à se déplacer d’avant en arrière. Le son des pleurs est devenu plus désespéré, et l'oie vivante a tellement pleuré qu'elle ne pouvait même plus émettre un son correctement par la suite. <u>J'étais à court de mots quand j'ai vu ça</u>. Comme ce serait bien si tous les gens du monde avaient le même esprit qu’eux.

#######

I took in two geese and raised them. When one cries, the other cries as if they are talking, drinking water and eating food together. It was a pleasure to watch the geese play every day, but one night one of them died. When I woke up in the morning, I saw the live goose holding the dead goose, flapping its wings and crying. When the dead goose was removed, the live goose started moving back and forth. The sound of crying became more desperate, and the live goose cried so much that it couldn't even make a sound properly afterwards. <u>I was at a loss for words when I saw this</u>. How nice it would be if all the people in the world had the same mind as them.
g_text_translate.es:
Cogí dos gansos y los crié. Cuando uno llora, el otro llora como si estuvieran hablando, bebiendo agua y comiendo juntos. Era un placer ver jugar a los gansos todos los días, pero una noche uno de ellos murió. Cuando me desperté por la mañana, vi al ganso vivo sosteniendo al ganso muerto, batiendo sus alas y llorando. Cuando sacaron el ganso muerto, el ganso vivo comenzó a moverse de un lado a otro. El sonido del llanto se volvió más desesperado y el ganso vivo lloró tanto que ni siquiera pudo emitir ningún sonido después. <u>Me quedé sin palabras cuando vi esto</u>. Qué bonito sería si todas las personas del mundo tuvieran la misma opinión que ellos.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Saya mengambil dua ekor angsa dan membesarkan mereka. Ketika yang satu menangis, yang lain menangis seolah-olah sedang berbicara, minum air, dan makan bersama. Sungguh menyenangkan menyaksikan angsa bermain setiap hari, namun suatu malam salah satu dari mereka mati. Ketika saya bangun di pagi hari, saya melihat angsa hidup memegangi angsa mati sambil mengepakkan sayapnya dan menangis. Ketika angsa yang mati dikeluarkan, angsa yang hidup mulai bergerak maju mundur. Suara tangisan menjadi semakin putus asa, dan angsa hidup menangis begitu keras hingga tidak dapat mengeluarkan suara dengan baik setelahnya. <u>Saya kehilangan kata-kata ketika melihat ini</u>. Alangkah baiknya jika semua orang di dunia mempunyai pikiran yang sama dengan mereka.

g_text_translate.ja:
ガチョウを２羽引き取って育てました。一人が泣くと、もう一人は話をしているかのように泣き、一緒に水を飲んだり、食べ物を食べたりします。毎日ガチョウが遊ぶのを見るのが楽しみだったのですが、ある夜、一羽が死んでしまいました。朝起きると、生きているガチョウが死んだガチョウを抱えて羽ばたいて鳴いているのが見えました。死んだガチョウが取り除かれると、生きたガチョウが前後に動き始めました。泣き声はますます絶望的になり、その後、生きているガチョウは声さえまともに出せないほど泣きました。これを見たとき<u>私は言葉を失いました</u>。世界中のすべての人が彼らと同じ心を持てたらどんなに素晴らしいでしょう。

g_text_translate.de:
Ich habe zwei Gänse aufgenommen und aufgezogen. Wenn einer weint, weint der andere, als ob sie miteinander reden, Wasser trinken und essen würden. Es war jeden Tag eine Freude, den Gänsen beim Spielen zuzusehen, doch eines Nachts starb eine von ihnen. Als ich morgens aufwachte, sah ich die lebende Gans, die die tote Gans hielt, mit den Flügeln schlug und weinte. Als die tote Gans entfernt wurde, begann sich die lebende Gans hin und her zu bewegen. Das Weinen wurde immer verzweifelter und die lebende Gans weinte so sehr, dass sie danach nicht einmal mehr einen richtigen Laut von sich geben konnte. <u>Mir fehlten die Worte, als ich das sah</u>. Wie schön wäre es, wenn alle Menschen auf der Welt den gleichen Geist hätten wie sie.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我收了两只鹅并把它们养大。一个人哭了，另一个人也跟着一起哭，就好像他们在一起喝水、吃饭一样。每天看鹅们玩耍是一件很高兴的事情，但是有一天晚上，其中一只死了。早上醒来，我看见活鹅抱着死鹅，拍打着翅膀，哭泣着。当死鹅被移走后，活鹅开始来回移动。哭声变得更加绝望，活鹅哭得连声音都发不出来了。 <u>当我看到这个时，我不知所措</u>。如果全世界的人都有和他们一样的想法该多好。

g_text_translate.zh-TW:
我收了兩隻鵝並把它們養大。一個人哭了，另一個人也跟著一起哭，就好像他們在一起喝水、吃飯一樣。每天看鵝們玩耍是一件很高興的事情，但是有一天晚上，其中一隻死了。早上醒來，我看見活鵝抱著死鵝，拍打著翅膀，哭泣著。當死鵝被移走後，活鵝開始來回移動。哭聲變得更加絕望，活鵝哭得連聲音都發不出來了。 <u>當我看到這個時，我不知所措</u>。如果全世界的人都有和他們一樣的想法該多好。

g_text_translate.ru:
Я взял двух гусей и вырастил их. Когда один плачет, другой плачет так, как будто они вместе разговаривают, пьют воду и едят. Было приятно каждый день наблюдать за игрой гусей, но однажды ночью один из них умер. Проснувшись утром, я увидел живого гуся, держащего мертвого гуся, хлопающего крыльями и плачущего. Когда мертвого гуся убрали, живой гусь начал двигаться взад и вперед. Плач стал еще отчаяннее, а живой гусь плакал так сильно, что после этого не мог даже издать ни звука. <u>Я потерял дар речи, когда увидел это</u>. Как было бы хорошо, если бы все люди в мире имели тот же ум, что и они.

g_text_translate.th:
ฉันเอาห่านสองตัวมาเลี้ยง เมื่อคนหนึ่งร้องไห้ อีกคนหนึ่งก็ร้องไห้ราวกับกำลังพูดคุย ดื่มน้ำ และกินอาหารด้วยกัน ดีใจที่ได้ดูห่านเล่นทุกวัน แต่คืนหนึ่งมีห่านตัวหนึ่งตาย ตื่นเช้ามาก็เห็นห่านเป็นๆ จับห่านที่ตายแล้ว กระพือปีกแล้วร้องไห้ เมื่อห่านที่ตายแล้วถูกเอาออกไป ห่านที่มีชีวิตก็เริ่มเคลื่อนไหวไปมา เสียงร้องไห้ยิ่งสิ้นหวังมากขึ้น และห่านที่มีชีวิตก็ร้องไห้มากจนไม่สามารถส่งเสียงได้อย่างเหมาะสมในภายหลัง <u>ฉันหมดคำพูดเมื่อเห็นสิ่งนี้</u> จะดีแค่ไหนถ้าทุกคนในโลกมีความคิดเหมือนกันกับพวกเขา

g_text_translate.pt:
Peguei dois gansos e os criei. Quando um chora, o outro chora como se estivessem conversando, bebendo água e comendo juntos. Foi um prazer ver os gansos brincarem todos os dias, mas uma noite um deles morreu. Quando acordei de manhã, vi o ganso vivo segurando o ganso morto, batendo as asas e chorando. Quando o ganso morto foi removido, o ganso vivo começou a se mover para frente e para trás. O som do choro ficou mais desesperador, e o ganso vivo chorou tanto que depois não conseguiu emitir nenhum som direito. <u>Fiquei sem palavras quando vi isso</u>. Como seria bom se todas as pessoas do mundo tivessem a mesma opinião que elas.

g_text_translate.hi:
मैंने दो हंस लिये और उनका पालन-पोषण किया। जब एक रोता है तो दूसरा ऐसे रोता है जैसे वे बात कर रहे हों, पानी पी रहे हों और साथ में खाना खा रहे हों। हर दिन हंसों को खेलते देखना आनंददायक था, लेकिन एक रात उनमें से एक की मृत्यु हो गई। सुबह जब मैं उठा तो मैंने देखा कि जिंदा हंस मरे हुए हंस को पकड़कर पंख फड़फड़ा रहा है और रो रहा है। जब मरे हुए हंस को हटाया गया तो जीवित हंस आगे-पीछे घूमने लगा। रोने की आवाज और अधिक तीव्र हो गई और जीवित हंस इतना रोया कि बाद में ठीक से आवाज भी नहीं निकाल सका। <u>जब मैंने इसे देखा तो मेरे पास शब्द नहीं थे</u>। कितना अच्छा होता अगर दुनिया के सभी लोगों की सोच उनके जैसी ही होती।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
İki kaz aldım ve büyüttüm. Biri ağladığında diğeri sanki konuşuyormuş gibi, birlikte su içiyor, birlikte yemek yiyormuş gibi ağlıyor. Her gün kazların oynamasını izlemek büyük bir zevkti ama bir gece kazlardan biri öldü. Sabah uyandığımda canlı kazın ölü kazı tuttuğunu, kanatlarını çırptığını ve ağladığını gördüm. Ölü kaz kaldırıldığında canlı kaz ileri geri hareket etmeye başladı. Ağlama sesi daha da umutsuzlaşmış ve canlı kaz o kadar çok ağlamış ki daha sonra doğru düzgün ses bile çıkaramamış. <u>Bunu gördüğümde söyleyecek söz bulamıyorum</u>. Dünyadaki bütün insanlar onlarla aynı düşünceye sahip olsaydı ne güzel olurdu.

content.q_text: 밑줄 친 부분에 나타난 나의 심정으로 알맞은 것을 고르십시오.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với tâm trạng của 'tôi' trong phần gạch chân.
1. Yên tâm
2. Thần kỳ
3. Giật mình
4. Tội nghiệp
--------------------
Có thể đoán rằng việc nhân vật 'tôi' không nói nên lời khi nhìn thấy bộ dạng của con ngỗng còn sống là do người này thấy tội nghiệp. 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the right one based on the feeling of 'I' shown in the underlined part.
1. To feel relieved
2. To be amazing 
3. To be surprised
4. To feel sorry and sad
--------------------
It can be guessed that this person was speechless when he saw the appearance of the live goose because this person felt sorry and sad. 4 is the correct answer.
explain.it:
Scegli quello giusto in base al sentimento di 'io' mostrato nella parte sottolineata.
1. Sentirsi sollevato
2. Per essere sorprendente
3. Essere sorpresi
4. Sentirsi dispiaciuti e tristi
--------------------
Si può immaginare che questa persona sia rimasta senza parole quando ha visto l'apparizione dell'oca viva perché si sentiva dispiaciuta e triste. 4 è la risposta corretta.

#######

--------------------
Kaz, bir zamanlar birlikte yaşadığı kaz öldüğünde üzüntüsünü ifade eder. Yani hayvanların da tıpkı insanlar gibi duygulara sahip olabileceği söylenebilir. 3 doğru cevaptır.

#######

Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. Geese are animals that get along well with humans.
2. A goose dies when one of the geese it lives with dies.
3. Animals can have emotions just like humans.
4. Humans are superior to animals in every way.
--------------------
The goose expresses grief when the goose that once lived with it dies. So it can be said that animals can have emotions just like humans. 3 is the correct answer.

general.g_text:
방학이 되자 학교 도서실에서 독서 캠프를 열었다.
“여러분, 소원을 들어주는 마법 램프 이야기 들어 봤나요?"
잘 아는 동화 이야기에 아이들은 어느새 수업에 흠뻑 빠져들었다. 간절한 꿈을 매일 스무 번씩 말하고 노력하면 꼭 이루어진다고 하자 아이들은 저마다 소원을 이야기했다. 부자가 되고 싶다는 아이도 있었고, 가수가 되겠다는 아이도 있었다. 그러나 재우는 아무 말이 없었다.
수업이 끝나고 집으로 돌아가는 길에 재우를 보았다. 재우는 혼자 고개를 푹 숙인 채 터덜터덜 걷고 있었다. 가까이 가자 재우의 중얼거리는 소리가 들렸다.
“엄마, 아빠랑 같이 산다. 엄마, 아빠랑 같이 산다.”
재우의 목소리가 얼마나 간절하던지 나는 <u>코끝이 찡했다</u>.

g_text_translate.vi:
Đến kỳ nghỉ, tôi tổ chức hội trại đọc sách trong thư viện của trường.
"Mấy đứa à, các con có bao giờ nghe nói về cây đèn thần ban điều ước chưa?"
Trước câu chuyện cổ tích quen thuộc, các bé đắm chìm hoàn toàn vào trong lớp học từ lúc nào. Khi tôi nói rằng nếu các bé nói ra ước mơ tha thiết của mình 20 lần một ngày và nỗ lực thì điều đó sẽ thành hiện thực, các bé đều chia sẻ ước muốn của mình. Có những đứa trẻ muốn trở nên giàu có, và có những đứa trẻ muốn trở thành ca sĩ. Nhưng Jaewoo không nói gì cả.
Trên đường về nhà sau giờ học, tôi nhìn thấy Jaewoo. Jaewoo đang lê bước đi một mình trong tư thế cúi đầu. Khi tôi đến gần hơn, tôi nghe thấy tiếng lầm bầm của Jaewoo.
"Sống với mẹ và bố. Sống với mẹ và bố."
Giọng của Jaewoo tha thiết đến nỗi khiến tôi<u>nghẹn ngào nơi đầu mũi</u>.
g_text_translate.en:
When vacation came, I held a reading camp in the school library.
“Everyone, have you heard of the magic lamp that grants wishes?”
With the fairy tale story they know well, the children are immersed in the class without knowing it. When I said that if they said their earnest dream 20 times a day and worked hard, it would come true, the children each talked about their wishes. There were children who wanted to be rich, and there were children who wanted to be singers. But Jaewoo didn't say anything.
I saw Jaewoo on the way home after class. Jaewoo was walking alone with his head down. As I got closer, I heard Jaewoo's muttering.
“Living with my mom and dad. Living with my mom and dad.”
Jaewoo's voice made me <u>choked up</u> at how desperate he was.
g_text_translate.it:
Quando arrivarono le vacanze, organizzai un campo di lettura nella biblioteca della scuola.
"Tutti, avete sentito parlare della lampada magica che esaudisce i desideri?"
Con la fiaba che conoscono bene, i bambini si immergono nella classe senza saperlo. Quando ho detto che se avessero raccontato il loro sogno più sincero 20 volte al giorno e avessero lavorato sodo, si sarebbe avverato, i bambini hanno parlato ciascuno dei loro desideri. C'erano bambini che volevano diventare ricchi e c'erano bambini che volevano diventare cantanti. Ma Jaewoo non ha detto nulla.
Ho visto Jaewoo mentre tornavo a casa dopo la lezione. Jaewoo camminava da solo a testa bassa. Mentre mi avvicinavo, ho sentito il mormorio di Jaewoo.
“Vivo con mia mamma e mio papà. Vivere con mia mamma e mio papà."
La voce di Jaewoo mi ha fatto <u>soffocare</u> per quanto fosse disperato.

Quand les vacances sont arrivées, j'ai organisé un camp de lecture à la bibliothèque de l'école.
« Tout le monde, avez-vous entendu parler de la lampe magique qui exauce les vœux ?
Avec le conte de fée qu’ils connaissent bien, les enfants sont immergés dans la classe sans le savoir. Quand j'ai dit que s'ils répétaient leur rêve sérieux 20 fois par jour et travaillaient dur, il se réaliserait, les enfants ont chacun parlé de leurs souhaits. Il y avait des enfants qui voulaient être riches, et il y avait des enfants qui voulaient devenir chanteurs. Mais Jaewoo n'a rien dit.
J'ai vu Jaewoo sur le chemin du retour après les cours. Jaewoo marchait seul, la tête baissée. En me rapprochant, j’entendis les marmonnements de Jaewoo.
«Je vis avec ma mère et mon père. Je vis avec ma mère et mon père.
La voix de Jaewoo m'a fait <u>étouffer</u> de voir à quel point il était désespéré.

g_text_translate.es:
Cuando llegaron las vacaciones, realicé un campamento de lectura en la biblioteca de la escuela.
"Todos, ¿han oído hablar de la lámpara mágica que concede deseos?"
Con el cuento de hadas que conocen bien, los niños se sumergen en la clase sin saberlo. Cuando dije que si decían su sueño más sincero 20 veces al día y trabajaban duro, se haría realidad, cada uno de los niños habló de sus deseos. Había niños que querían ser ricos y había niños que querían ser cantantes. Pero Jaewoo no dijo nada.
Vi a Jaewoo camino a casa después de clase. Jaewoo caminaba solo con la cabeza gacha. Mientras me acercaba, escuché el murmullo de Jaewoo.
“Viviendo con mi mamá y mi papá. Viviendo con mi mamá y mi papá”.
La voz de Jaewoo me hizo <u>ahogarse</u> por lo desesperado que estaba.

“Semuanya, pernahkah kalian mendengar tentang lampu ajaib yang mengabulkan permintaan?”
Dengan cerita dongeng yang mereka kenal dengan baik, anak-anak tenggelam dalam kelas tanpa menyadarinya. Ketika saya mengatakan bahwa jika mereka mengucapkan impian sungguh-sungguh mereka 20 kali sehari dan bekerja keras, itu akan menjadi kenyataan, anak-anak masing-masing membicarakan keinginan mereka. Ada anak yang ingin kaya, dan ada anak yang ingin jadi penyanyi. Tapi Jaewoo tidak mengatakan apa-apa.
Saya melihat Jaewoo dalam perjalanan pulang setelah kelas. Jaewoo sedang berjalan sendirian dengan kepala tertunduk. Saat aku semakin dekat, aku mendengar gumaman Jaewoo.
“Tinggal bersama ibu dan ayahku. Tinggal bersama ibu dan ayahku.”
Suara Jaewoo membuatku <u>tersedak</u> melihat betapa putus asanya dia.

g_text_translate.ja:
休暇になると、私は学校の図書館で読書キャンプを開催しました。
「みなさん、願いを叶える魔法のランプをご存知ですか？」
よく知っている童話の物語で、子どもたちは知らず知らずのうちに授業に没頭しています。 「切実な夢を1日20回言って頑張れば叶うよ」と言うと、子どもたちはそれぞれ自分の願いを語っていました。お金持ちになりたい子供もいれば、歌手になりたい子供もいた。しかしジェウは何も言わなかった。
授業が終わって家に帰る途中、ジェウに会った。ジェウはうつむきながら一人で歩いていた。近づくと、ジェウのつぶやきが聞こえました。
「お父さんとお母さんと暮らしています。お父さんとお母さんと一緒に暮らしています。」
ジェウの声を聞くと、 あまりの必死さに<u>息が詰まり</u>ました。

„Habt ihr alle von der Wunderlampe gehört, die Wünsche erfüllt?“
Mit der Märchengeschichte, die sie gut kennen, tauchen die Kinder in den Unterricht ein, ohne es zu wissen. Als ich sagte, dass, wenn sie ihren ernsthaften Traum 20 Mal am Tag aussprechen und hart arbeiten würden, er wahr werden würde, sprachen die Kinder jeweils über ihre Wünsche. Es gab Kinder, die reich sein wollten, und es gab Kinder, die Sänger werden wollten. Aber Jaewoo sagte nichts.
Ich habe Jaewoo auf dem Heimweg nach dem Unterricht gesehen. Jaewoo ging allein mit gesenktem Kopf. Als ich näher kam, hörte ich Jaewoos Gemurmel.

Jaewoos Stimme ließ mich ersticken, als ich sah, wie verzweifelt er war.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
放假的时候，我在学校图书馆举办了读书营。
“诸位，你们听说过可以实现愿望的神灯吗？”
伴随着耳熟能详的童话故事，孩子们不知不觉地沉浸在课堂中。当我说如果每天说20遍自己的梦想并努力奋斗就会实现时，孩子们各自讲述了自己的愿望。有些孩子想成为有钱人，有些孩子想成为歌手。但贾宇什么也没说。
下课回家路上我看到了在宇。贾宇一个人低着头走着。当我走近时，我听到贾宇的嘀咕声。
“和我爸爸妈妈住在一起。和爸爸妈妈住在一起。”
Jaewoo 的声音让我因他的绝望而<u>哽咽</u>。

Когда пришли каникулы, я провел читальный лагерь в школьной библиотеке.
«Все, вы слышали о волшебной лампе, исполняющей желания?»
Благодаря хорошо знакомому им сказочному сюжету дети погружаются в занятие, сами того не подозревая. Когда я сказала, что если они произнесут свою заветную мечту 20 раз в день и будут усердно работать, то она сбудется, каждый из детей рассказал о своих желаниях. «Живу с мамой и папой. Живу с мамой и папой».
Голос Джэу заставил меня <u>задохнуться</u> от того, в каком отчаянии он был.

g_text_translate.hi:
जब छुट्टियां आईं तो मैंने स्कूल की लाइब्रेरी में रीडिंग कैंप लगाया।
"सभी, क्या आपने उस जादुई दीपक के बारे में सुना है जो इच्छाएँ पूरी करता है?"
जिस परी कथा की कहानी वे अच्छी तरह से जानते हैं, बच्चे उसे जाने बिना ही कक्षा में डूबे रहते हैं। जब मैंने कहा कि यदि वे दिन में 20 बार अपने गंभीर सपने के बारे में कहें और कड़ी मेहनत करें, तो यह सच हो जाएगा, तो प्रत्येक बच्चे ने अपनी इच्छाओं के बारे में बात की। ऐसे बच्चे थे जो अमीर बनना चाहते थे, और ऐसे बच्चे भी थे जो गायक बनना चाहते थे। लेकिन जेवू ने कुछ नहीं कहा.
क्लास के बाद घर जाते समय मैंने जेवू को देखा। जावू सिर झुकाए अकेला चल रहा था। जैसे ही मैं करीब आया, मैंने जेवू की बड़बड़ाहट सुनी।
“अपनी माँ और पिताजी के साथ रह रहा हूँ। अपनी माँ और पिताजी के साथ रह रहा हूँ।”
जेवू की आवाज़ ने मुझे यह देखकर चौंका दिया कि वह कितना हताश था।

#######

When vacation came, I held a reading camp in the school library.
“Everyone, have you heard of the magic lamp that grants wishes?”
With the fairy tale story they know well, the children are immersed in the class without knowing it. When I said that if they said their earnest dream 20 times a day and worked hard, it would come true, the children each talked about their wishes. There were children who wanted to be rich, and there were children who wanted to be singers. But Jaewoo didn't say anything.
I saw Jaewoo on the way home after class. Jaewoo was walking alone with his head down. As I got closer, I heard Jaewoo's muttering.
“Living with my mom and dad. Living with my mom and dad.”
Jaewoo's voice made me <u>choked up</u> at how desperate he was.
g_text_translate.tr:
Tatil geldiğinde okul kütüphanesinde okuma kampı düzenledim.
"Millet, dilekleri yerine getiren sihirli lambayı duydunuz mu?"
Çocuklar çok iyi bildikleri masallarla, farkında olmadan derse dalmış oluyorlar. Günde 20 kez en içten hayallerini söyleseler ve çok çalışırlarsa bu hayalin gerçekleşeceğini söylediğimde çocuklar her biri dileklerini anlattı. Zengin olmak isteyen çocuklar vardı, şarkıcı olmak isteyen çocuklar da vardı. Ama Jaewoo hiçbir şey söylemedi.
Jaewoo'yu dersten sonra eve giderken gördüm. Jaewoo başı aşağıda tek başına yürüyordu. Yaklaştıkça Jaewoo'nun mırıldandığını duydum.
"Annem ve babamla yaşıyorum. Annem ve babamla yaşıyorum."
Jaewoo'nun sesi onun ne kadar çaresiz olduğunu görünce beni <u>boğulmama</u> neden oldu.

#######

When vacation came, I held a reading camp in the school library.
“Everyone, have you heard of the magic lamp that grants wishes?”
With the fairy tale story they know well, the children are immersed in the class without knowing it. When I said that if they said their earnest dream 20 times a day and worked hard, it would come true, the children each talked about their wishes. There were children who wanted to be rich, and there were children who wanted to be singers. But Jaewoo didn't say anything.
I saw Jaewoo on the way home after class. Jaewoo was walking alone with his head down. As I got closer, I heard Jaewoo's muttering.
“Living with my mom and dad. Living with my mom and dad.”
Jaewoo's voice made me <u>choked up</u> at how desperate he was.

2. Không hài lòng, tiếc nuối, hối tiếc
3. Tội nghiệp
4. Ngột ngạt, bức bối

explain.en:
Choose the right one based on the feeling of 'I' shown in the underlined part.

2. To feel disappointed
3. To feel sorry
4. To be stuffy

Upon hearing Jaewoo's wish, the teacher understood that the child did not live with his father and mother. Because of that, the teacher was choked up because he/she felt sorry for Jaewoo. 3 is the correct answer.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 재우는 지금 엄마, 아빠와 함께 산다.
재우는 선생님이 한 말을 믿고 있다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Jaewoo không thể tham gia hội trại đọc sách.
2. Jaewoo nói rằng cậu bé muốn trở thành người giàu có.
3. Bây giờ Jaewoo đang sống với bố mẹ.
4. Jaewoo tin lời giáo viên nói.
--------------------
Jaewoo lặp lại điều ước của mình trên đường về. Điều đó cho thấy đứa trẻ tin lời giáo viên rằng nếu nói ra điều ước 20 lần một ngày thì điều ước sẽ trở thành sự thật. 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. Jaewoo could not participate in the reading camp.
2. Jaewoo said he wants to be rich.
3. Jaewoo now lives with his mom and dad.
4. Jaewoo believes what the teacher said.
--------------------
Jaewoo repeated his wish on the way home. It shows that the child believes the teacher that if he says his wish 20 times a day, the wish will come true. 4 is the correct answer.
explain.it:

2. Jaewoo ha detto che vuole essere ricco.
3. Jaewoo ora vive con sua madre e suo padre.
4. Jaewoo crede a ciò che ha detto l'insegnante.
--------------------
Jaewoo ripeté il suo desiderio mentre tornava a casa. Ciò dimostra che il bambino crede all'insegnante che se esprime il suo desiderio 20 volte al giorno, il desiderio si avvererà. 4 è la risposta corretta.

Jaewoo wiederholte seinen Wunsch auf dem Heimweg. Es zeigt, dass das Kind dem Lehrer glaubt, dass dieser Wunsch in Erfüllung geht, wenn er seinen Wunsch 20 Mal am Tag äußert. 4 ist die richtige Antwort.

general.g_text: 초등학교 5학년 때의 일이다. 나는 초등학교 합창 대회에 참가하기 위해 학교 합창 단원으로 뽑혔다. 합창단원이 되었다는 사실이 무척 자랑스럽고 기뻤다. 연습을 시작한 지 이틀째가 되는 날 선생님께서 학생 한 명 한 명의 노랫소리를 자세히 들으시더니 나에게 입만 벌리고 소리는 내지 말라고 하셨다. 나는 선생님 말씀대로 하다가 '이 많은 학생이 노래하는데 내 목소리가 들리겠어?' 생각하고 다시 노래를 부르기 시작했다. 잠시 후 선생님께서 오시더니 나를 합창단에서 빼셨다. 나는 <u>얼굴이 벌게지고 고개를 숙인 채</u> 합창단에서 나왔다. 지금도 그때를 생각하면 얼굴이 뜨거워지고 창피한 생각이 다시 떠오른다.

g_text_translate.vi: Chuyện xảy ra khi tôi học lớp 5. Tôi được chọn là thành viên của dàn hợp xướng của trường để tham gia cuộc thi hợp xướng cấp tiểu học. Tôi rất tự hào và hạnh phúc vì tôi đã trở thành một thành viên của dàn hợp xướng. Trong ngày luyện tập thứ hai, giáo viên lắng nghe kỹ từng tiếng hát của học sinh rồi dặn tôi phải mở miệng nhưng đừng phát ra âm thanh. Tôi làm theo những gì giáo viên nói, sau đó tôi nghĩ 'Nhiều học sinh hát thế này thì giáo viên có nghe được giọng của mình không?' và bắt đầu hát trở lại. Một lúc sau, giáo viên đến và loại tôi ra khỏi dàn hợp xướng. Tôi bước ra khỏi dàn hợp xướng <u>với khuôn mặt đỏ bừng và cúi đầu</u>. Ngay cả bây giờ, khi tôi nghĩ đến thời điểm đó, mặt tôi vẫn nóng lên và những suy nghĩ xấu hổ quay trở lại trong tôi.
g_text_translate.en: This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.it:
Questo è successo quando ero in quinta elementare. Sono stato selezionato come membro del coro della scuola per partecipare al concorso corale della scuola elementare. Ero molto orgoglioso e felice di far parte del coro. Il secondo giorno di pratica, l'insegnante ha ascoltato attentamente ogni studente cantare e mi ha detto di aprire la bocca e di non fare rumore. Ho fatto come aveva detto l'insegnante, ma ho pensato: 'L'insegnante può sentire la mia voce quando così tanti studenti cantano?' e ricominciò a cantare. Dopo un po' venne l'insegnante e mi portò fuori dal coro. Sono uscito dal coro con <u>il viso arrossato e la testa chinata</u>. Anche adesso, quando ripenso a quel periodo, il mio viso si infiamma e mi ritornano pensieri imbarazzanti.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.fr:
Cela s'est produit quand j'étais en 5e année du primaire. J'ai été sélectionnée comme membre de la chorale de l'école pour participer au concours de chorale d'école primaire. J'étais très fier et heureux d'être membre de la chorale. Le deuxième jour de pratique, le professeur a écouté attentivement chaque élève chanter et m'a dit d'ouvrir la bouche et de ne faire aucun bruit. J'ai fait ce que le professeur m'a dit, mais j'ai pensé : « Le professeur peut-il entendre ma voix alors que tant d'élèves chantent ? et j'ai recommencé à chanter. Au bout d'un moment, le professeur est venu et m'a fait sortir de la chorale. Je suis sorti de la chorale avec <u>le visage rougi et la tête baissée</u>. Même maintenant, quand je pense à cette époque, mon visage s’échauffe et des pensées embarrassantes me reviennent.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.es:
Esto sucedió cuando estaba en quinto grado de la escuela primaria. Fui seleccionado como miembro del coro de la escuela para participar en el concurso de coros de la escuela primaria. Estaba muy orgulloso y feliz de ser miembro del coro. El segundo día de práctica, la maestra escuchó atentamente a cada alumno cantar y me dijo que abriera la boca y no hiciera ningún ruido. Hice lo que me dijo el maestro, pero pensé: '¿Puede el maestro escuchar mi voz cuando tantos estudiantes cantan?' y empezó a cantar de nuevo. Al rato vino la maestra y me sacó del coro. Salí del coro con <u>la cara enrojecida y la cabeza gacha</u>. Incluso ahora, cuando pienso en ese momento, mi cara se calienta y pensamientos embarazosos regresan a mí.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.id:
Hal ini terjadi ketika saya duduk di kelas 5 sekolah dasar. Saya terpilih menjadi anggota paduan suara sekolah untuk mengikuti kompetisi paduan suara sekolah dasar. Saya sangat bangga dan senang menjadi anggota paduan suara. Pada latihan hari kedua, guru dengan cermat mendengarkan setiap siswa bernyanyi dan menyuruh saya membuka mulut dan tidak membuat suara apa pun. Aku melakukan apa yang guru katakan, tapi aku berpikir, 'Dapatkah guru mendengar suaraku ketika banyak siswa yang bernyanyi?' dan mulai bernyanyi lagi. Tak lama kemudian guru datang dan membawaku keluar dari paduan suara. Aku keluar dari paduan suara dengan <u>wajahku memerah dan kepalaku tertunduk</u>. Bahkan sekarang, ketika aku memikirkan saat itu, wajahku memanas dan pikiran memalukan kembali muncul di benakku.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.ja:
私が小学5年生の時の出来事です。私は小学校の合唱コンクールに出場するため、学校の合唱団のメンバーに選ばれました。合唱団の一員になれたことがとても誇りに思い、幸せでした。練習２日目、先生は生徒一人一人の歌を注意深く聞いて、「口を開けて音を立てないように」と言われました。先生の言うとおりにしたのですが、「こんなにたくさんの生徒が歌っているのに、先生に私の声が聞こえるだろうか？」と思いました。そしてまた歌い始めました。しばらくすると先生が来て私を合唱団から外しました。私は<u>顔を真っ赤にして頭を下げ</u>て合唱団から出てきました。今でも当時のことを思い出すと顔が熱くなり、恥ずかしい思いが甦ります。

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.de:
Das passierte, als ich in der 5. Klasse der Grundschule war. Ich wurde als Mitglied des Schulchors ausgewählt, um am Grundschulchorwettbewerb teilzunehmen. Ich war sehr stolz und glücklich, Mitglied des Chores zu sein. Am zweiten Übungstag hörte der Lehrer jedem Schüler beim Singen aufmerksam zu und forderte mich auf, den Mund zu öffnen und keinen Lärm zu machen. Ich tat, was der Lehrer sagte, aber ich dachte: „Kann der Lehrer meine Stimme hören, wenn so viele Schüler singen?“ und fing wieder an zu singen. Nach einer Weile kam der Lehrer und holte mich aus dem Chor. Ich kam mit <u>gerötetem Gesicht und gesenktem Kopf</u> aus dem Chor. Selbst jetzt, wenn ich an diese Zeit zurückdenke, wird mein Gesicht heiß und peinliche Gedanken kommen mir wieder in den Sinn.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
这件事发生在我小学五年级的时候。我被选为学校合唱团成员，参加小学合唱团比赛。作为合唱团的一员，我感到非常自豪和高兴。第二天练习，老师仔细听每个学生唱歌，并叮嘱我张开嘴，不要出声。我按照老师说的做了，但我想：这么多学生在唱歌，老师能听到我的声音吗？并再次开始唱歌。过了一会儿，老师来了，把我带出了合唱团。我从唱诗班出来，<u>脸红了，低着头</u>。直到现在，一想起当时的情景，我的脸就会发热，一些尴尬的想法又浮现在我的脑海里。

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
這件事發生在我小學五年級的時候。我被選為學校合唱團成員，參加小學合唱團比賽。作為合唱團的一員，我感到非常自豪和高興。第二天練習，老師仔細聽每個學生唱歌，並叮囑我張開嘴，不要出聲。我按照老師說的做了，但我想：這麼多學生在唱歌，老師能聽到我的聲音嗎？並再次開始唱歌。過了一會兒，老師來了，把我帶出了合唱團。我從唱詩班出來，<u>臉紅了，低著頭</u>。直到現在，一想起當時的情景，我的臉就會發熱，一些尷尬的想法又浮現在我的腦海裡。

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.ru:
Это произошло, когда я учился в 5 классе начальной школы. Меня выбрали в состав школьного хора для участия в конкурсе хоров начальной школы. Я был очень горд и счастлив быть членом хора. На второй день практики учитель внимательно слушал пение каждого ученика и велел мне открыть рот и не шуметь. Я сделал, как сказал учитель, но подумал: «Может ли учитель услышать мой голос, когда поет так много учеников?» и снова начал петь. Через некоторое время пришла учительница и забрала меня из хора. Я вышел из хора с <u>покрасневшим лицом и опущенной головой</u>. Даже сейчас, когда я думаю о том времени, мое лицо горит, и ко мне возвращаются неловкие мысли.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.th:
เรื่องนี้เกิดขึ้นเมื่อฉันอยู่ชั้นประถมศึกษาปีที่ 5 ฉันได้รับเลือกให้เป็นคณะนักร้องประสานเสียงของโรงเรียนเพื่อเข้าร่วมการแข่งขันขับร้องประสานเสียงของโรงเรียนประถมศึกษา ฉันภูมิใจและมีความสุขมากที่ได้เป็นสมาชิกคณะนักร้องประสานเสียง ในวันที่สองของการฝึก ครูตั้งใจฟังนักเรียนแต่ละคนร้องเพลงและบอกให้ฉันอ้าปากห้ามส่งเสียงดัง ฉันทำตามที่ครูพูด แต่ฉันคิดว่า 'ครูจะได้ยินเสียงของฉันเวลานักเรียนร้องเพลงกันเยอะๆ ได้ไหม' และเริ่มร้องเพลงอีกครั้ง สักพักอาจารย์ก็มาพาผมออกจากคณะนักร้องประสานเสียง ฉันออกจากคณะนักร้องประสานเสียงโดย<u>หน้าแดงและก้มศีรษะ</u> แม้ตอนนี้เมื่อฉันนึกถึงเวลานั้น ใบหน้าของฉันก็ร้อนขึ้นและความคิดที่น่าอายก็กลับมาหาฉัน

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.pt:
Isso aconteceu quando eu estava na 5ª série do ensino fundamental. Fui selecionado como membro do coral da escola para participar da competição de corais do ensino fundamental. Fiquei muito orgulhoso e feliz por fazer parte do coral. No segundo dia de prática, a professora ouviu atentamente cada aluno cantando e me disse para abrir a boca e não fazer barulho. Fiz o que a professora disse, mas pensei: 'Será que a professora consegue ouvir minha voz quando tantos alunos estão cantando?' e comecei a cantar novamente. Depois de um tempo a professora veio e me tirou do coral. Saí do coral com <u>o rosto vermelho e a cabeça baixa</u>. Mesmo agora, quando penso naquela época, meu rosto esquenta e pensamentos embaraçosos voltam à minha mente.

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.hi:
यह तब हुआ जब मैं प्राथमिक विद्यालय की 5वीं कक्षा में था। प्राथमिक विद्यालय गायन प्रतियोगिता में भाग लेने के लिए मुझे स्कूल गायक दल के सदस्य के रूप में चुना गया था। गायक मंडल का सदस्य बनकर मुझे बहुत गर्व और खुशी हुई। अभ्यास के दूसरे दिन, शिक्षक ने प्रत्येक छात्र के गायन को ध्यान से सुना और मुझसे कहा कि मैं अपना मुँह खोलूँ और कोई शोर न मचाऊँ। मैंने वैसा ही किया जैसा शिक्षक ने कहा था, लेकिन मैंने सोचा, 'जब इतने सारे छात्र गा रहे हों तो क्या शिक्षक मेरी आवाज़ सुन सकते हैं?' और फिर से गाना शुरू कर दिया. थोड़ी देर बाद टीचर आये और मुझे गायन मंडली से बाहर ले गये। मैं गाना बजानेवालों से बाहर आया <u>मेरा चेहरा लाल था और मेरा सिर झुका हुआ था</u>। अब भी, जब मैं उस समय के बारे में सोचता हूं, तो मेरा चेहरा गर्म हो जाता है और शर्मनाक विचार मेरे सामने आते हैं।

#######

This happened when I was in the 5th grade of elementary school. I was selected as a member of the school choir to participate in the elementary school choir competition. I was very proud and happy to be a member of the choir. On the second day of practice, the teacher carefully listened to each student singing and told me to open my mouth and not make any noise. I did as the teacher said, but I thought, 'Can the teacher hear my voice when so many students are singing?' and started singing again. After a while the teacher came and took me out of the choir. I came out of the choir with <u>my face reddened and my head bowed</u>. Even now, when I think of that time, my face heats up and embarrassing thoughts come back to me.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bu, ilkokul 5. sınıftayken oldu. İlkokul korosu yarışmasına katılmak üzere okul korosuna seçildim. Koronun bir üyesi olmaktan büyük gurur ve mutluluk duydum. Uygulamanın ikinci gününde öğretmen her öğrencinin şarkı söylemesini dikkatle dinledi ve bana ağzımı açmamı ve ses çıkarmamamı söyledi. Öğretmenin dediğini yaptım ama 'Bu kadar öğrenci şarkı söylerken öğretmen benim sesimi duyabilir mi?' diye düşündüm. ve tekrar şarkı söylemeye başladım. Bir süre sonra öğretmen gelip beni korodan çıkardı. Korodan <u>yüzüm kızarmış ve başım öne eğilmiş</u> bir halde çıktım. Şimdi bile o zamanı düşündüğümde yüzüm kızarıyor ve utanç verici düşünceler aklıma geliyor.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 나는 합창 대회에 나가서 노래를 불렀다.
선생님이 나에게 노래 부르지 말고 흉내만 내라고 하셨다.
합창 연습을 하면서 '조화'의 의미를 알게 되었다.
나는 노래를 부르지 말라고 해서 합창단을 그만두었다.

explain.vi:

1. Tôi đã tham gia cuộc thi hợp xướng và hát.
2. Giáo viên bảo tôi đừng hát mà chỉ bắt chước thôi.
3. Khi luyện tập hợp xướng tôi đã hiểu được ý nghĩa của 'sự hòa hợp'.
4. Vì được bảo là đừng hát nên tôi đã từ bỏ dàn hợp xướng.
--------------------
Nhân vật 'tôi' được giáo viên yêu cầu phải mở miệng nhưng đừng phát ra âm thanh. 2 là đáp án đúng.

--------------------

3. J'ai appris le sens de « harmonie » en pratiquant le chœur.
4. J'ai quitté la chorale parce qu'on m'a dit de ne pas chanter.
--------------------
Le professeur a dit à cette personne d'ouvrir la bouche mais de ne pas émettre de son. 2 est la bonne réponse.

#######

Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. I sang in a choir competition.
2. The teacher told me not to sing, just imitate.
3. I learned the meaning of 'harmony' while practicing chorus.
4. I quit the choir because I was told not to sing.
--------------------
This person was told by the teacher to open his mouth but not to make a sound. 2 is the correct answer.

general.g_text:
오늘도 내 휴대 전화에선 휘파람 소리가 들린다. 벨 소리로 정할 만큼 난 휘파람 소리가 좋다. <u>휘파람 소리를 들으면 나도 모르게 얼굴에 미소가 떠오르고 가슴이 콩닥거린다</u>. 거기엔 내 첫사랑이 녹아 있기 때문이다. 25년 전, 나를 따라다니던 수줍음 많던 소년이 있었다. 어느 날, 소년이 물었다.
“혹시 휘파람 소리 못 들었니?"
내가 못 들었다고 하자 그 소년은 “매일 저녁 일곱 시에 네 창문 밖에서 불었는데.” 하고 말했다.
그 뒤 신기한 일이 일어났다. 한 번도 들린 적 없던 휘파람 소리가 귓가에 선명하게 울렸다.
"휘익~ 휘익~.”
어김없이 저녁 7시면 그 소리가 들렸다. 시간이 지나면서 나는 점점 그 소리가 들리기를 기다리게 되었고, 지금 나는 휘파람을 불던 그 소년과 결혼해 알콩달콩 살고 있다.

g_text_translate.vi:
Hôm nay, tôi cũng nghe thấy tiếng huýt sáo từ điện thoại của mình. Tôi thích âm thanh huýt sáo đến mức đặt nó làm tiếng chuông. <u>Nếu nghe thấy tiếng huýt sáo, tôi sẽ vô thức nở một nụ cười trên gương mặt và tim thì đập thình thịch</u>. Bởi vì mối tình đầu của tôi tan chảy trước nó. 25 năm trước, có một cậu bé rất nhút nhát đi theo tôi. Một ngày nọ, cậu bé hỏi.
"Cậu không nghe thấy tiếng huýt sáo à?"
Khi tôi nói rằng tôi không nghe thấy, cậu bé nói, "Tớ huýt ở ngoài cửa sổ của cậu vào lúc bảy giờ tối hàng ngày."
Sau đó, một điều kỳ lạ đã xảy ra. Một tiếng huýt sáo chưa bao giờ tôi được nghe vang lên rõ ràng bên tai tôi.
"Huýt~ Huýt~."
Mỗi khi đến đúng bảy giờ tối, tôi lại nghe thấy âm thanh đó. Thời gian trôi qua, tôi ngày càng mong đợi được nghe âm thanh đó, giờ tôi đã kết hôn với chàng trai huýt sáo đó và sống hạnh phúc.
g_text_translate.en:
Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.
"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.it:
Ancora oggi sento un fischio sul cellulare. Mi piace abbastanza il suono del fischio da decidere che sia un campanello. <u>Quando sento il fischio, mi viene un sorriso in faccia e il mio cuore batte forte</u>. È perché il mio primo amore si è sciolto lì. 25 anni fa c'era un ragazzo timido che mi seguiva ovunque. Un giorno, il ragazzo chiese
"Non hai sentito il fischio?"
Quando ho detto che non avevo sentito, il ragazzo ha detto: "Ogni notte fischio fuori dalla tua finestra alle sette".
Dopodiché accadde una cosa strana. Un fischio che non avevo mai sentito prima risuonò chiaramente nelle mie orecchie.
"Fischio~ Fischio~."
Era sempre alle 7 di sera che la sentivo. Col passare del tempo, ho iniziato ad aspettare di sentire quel suono, e ora sono sposata con il ragazzo che fischietta e vivo felice e contenta.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.
"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.fr:
Aujourd'hui encore, j'entends un sifflement sur mon téléphone portable. J'aime suffisamment le son du sifflet pour le décider comme une cloche. <u>Quand j'entends le sifflet, un sourire apparaît sur mon visage et mon cœur s'emballe</u>. C'est parce que mon premier amour y a fondu. Il y a 25 ans, il y avait un garçon timide qui me suivait partout. Un jour, le garçon a demandé
« Vous n'avez pas entendu le sifflet ?
Quand j'ai dit que je n'avais pas entendu, le garçon a répondu : « Chaque nuit, je siffle devant ta fenêtre à sept heures.
Après cela, une chose étrange s’est produite. Un sifflement que je n’avais jamais entendu auparavant résonna clairement à mes oreilles.
"Sifflet~ Sifflet~."
C'était toujours à 19 heures que je l'entendais. Au fil du temps, j'ai commencé à attendre d'entendre ce son, et maintenant je suis mariée au garçon qui siffle et je vis heureuse pour toujours.

#######

g_text_translate.es:
Aún hoy escucho un silbido en mi celular. Me gusta el sonido del silbato lo suficiente como para decidirlo como una campana. <u>Cuando escucho el silbido, una sonrisa aparece en mi cara y mi corazón se acelera</u>. Es porque mi primer amor se derritió allí. Hace 25 años, había un chico tímido que me seguía a todas partes. Un día, el niño preguntó
“¿No oíste el silbido?”
Cuando dije que no lo había oído, el niño dijo: "Todas las noches silbo fuera de tu ventana a las siete en punto".
Después de eso, sucedió algo extraño. Un silbido que nunca antes había escuchado sonó claramente en mis oídos.
"Silbato ~ Silbato ~."
Siempre era a las siete de la tarde cuando lo oía. Conforme pasó el tiempo, comencé a esperar a escuchar ese sonido, y ahora estoy casada con el niño que silba y vivo feliz para siempre.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."

"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.id:
Bahkan hari ini, aku mendengar peluit di ponselku. Saya cukup menyukai suara peluit untuk menjadikannya sebagai lonceng. <u>Saat aku mendengar peluit, senyuman muncul di wajahku dan jantungku berdebar kencang</u>. Itu karena cinta pertamaku melebur di sana. 25 tahun yang lalu, ada seorang anak laki-laki pemalu yang mengikutiku kemana-mana. Suatu hari, anak laki-laki itu bertanya
“Apakah kamu tidak mendengar peluitnya?”
Saat aku bilang aku tidak mendengar, anak itu berkata, "Setiap malam aku bersiul di luar jendelamu pada jam tujuh."
Setelah itu, terjadi hal aneh. Peluit yang belum pernah kudengar sebelumnya terdengar jelas di telingaku.
"Peluit~ Peluit~."
Saya selalu mendengarnya pada jam 7 malam. Seiring berjalannya waktu, saya mulai menunggu untuk mendengar suara itu, dan sekarang saya menikah dengan anak laki-laki yang bersiul dan hidup bahagia selamanya.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
今でも携帯電話から笛の音が聞こえます。笛の音は鐘と決めるくらい好きです。 <u>汽笛を聞くと顔に笑みが浮かび、 心臓も高鳴ります</u>。私の初恋がそこで溶けてしまったからです。 25年前、私の後をつけてくる内気な少年がいました。ある日、少年はこう尋ねた。
「汽笛が聞こえませんでしたか？」
私が聞いていないと言うと、少年は「毎晩7時にあなたの窓の外で口笛を吹いています」と言いました。
その後、奇妙なことが起こりました。今まで聞いたことのない笛の音が耳にはっきりと響きました。
「ホイッスル～ホイッスル～」。
それを聞いたのはいつも夜の7時でした。時が経ち、私はその音を待ち続けるようになり、今では口笛を吹く少年と結婚して幸せに暮らしています。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Noch heute höre ich ein Pfeifen auf meinem Handy. Ich mag den Klang der Pfeife so sehr, dass ich sie als Glocke verwende. <u>Wenn ich den Pfiff höre, erscheint ein Lächeln auf meinem Gesicht und mein Herz rast</u>. Das liegt daran, dass meine erste Liebe dort geschmolzen ist. Vor 25 Jahren folgte mir ein schüchterner Junge. Eines Tages fragte der Junge
„Hast du den Pfiff nicht gehört?“
Als ich sagte, ich hätte es nicht gehört, sagte der Junge: „Jeden Abend pfeife ich um sieben Uhr vor deinem Fenster.“
Danach geschah etwas Seltsames. Ein Pfeifen, das ich noch nie zuvor gehört hatte, ertönte deutlich in meinen Ohren.
„Pfeife~ Pfeife~.“
Es war immer um 19 Uhr abends, als ich es hörte. Mit der Zeit begann ich darauf zu warten, dieses Geräusch zu hören, und jetzt bin ich mit dem pfeifenden Jungen verheiratet und lebe glücklich bis ans Ende meiner Tage.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.
"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
直到今天，我的手机里仍能听到口哨声。我很喜欢哨子的声音，足以决定它是铃声。 <u>当我听到哨声时，我的脸上露出笑容，我的心跳加速</u>。因为我的初恋在那里融化了。 25年前，有一个害羞的男孩一直跟着我。有一天，男孩问
“你没听到哨声吗？”
当我说我没有听到时，男孩说：“每天晚上七点钟我都会在你窗外吹口哨。”
之后，奇怪的事情发生了。一声从未听过的哨声清晰地在耳边响起。
“哨～哨～。”
我总是在晚上7:00听到它。随着时间的流逝，我开始等待听到那个声音，现在我嫁给了那个吹口哨的男孩，从此过上了幸福的生活。

直到今天，我的手機裡仍能聽到口哨聲。我很喜歡哨子的聲音，足以決定它是鈴聲。 <u>當我聽到哨聲時，我的臉上露出笑容，我的心跳加速</u>。因為我的初戀在那裡融化了。 25年前，有一個害羞的男孩一直跟著我。有一天，男孩問
“你沒聽到哨聲嗎？”
當我說我沒有聽到時，男孩說：“每天晚上七點鐘我都會在你窗外吹口哨。”

g_text_translate.ru:
Даже сегодня я слышу свист на своем мобильном телефоне. Мне настолько нравится звук свистка, что я могу принять его за звонок. <u>Когда я слышу свисток, на моем лице появляется улыбка и сердце колотится</u>. Это потому, что там растаяла моя первая любовь. 25 лет назад за мной следовал застенчивый мальчик. Однажды мальчик спросил
— Ты не слышал свистка?
Когда я сказал, что не слышал, мальчик ответил: «Каждую ночь я свистю за твоим окном в семь часов».
После этого произошла странная вещь. В моих ушах ясно прозвучал свист, которого я никогда раньше не слышал.
«Свист~ Свист~».
Я всегда слышал это в семь часов вечера. Со временем я стала ждать возможности услышать этот звук, и теперь я замужем за свистящим мальчиком и живу долго и счастливо.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”

g_text_translate.th:
แม้กระทั่งวันนี้ ฉันได้ยินเสียงนกหวีดทางโทรศัพท์มือถือของฉัน ผมชอบเสียงนกหวีดมากพอที่จะตัดสินว่าเป็นเสียงระฆัง <u>เมื่อฉันได้ยินเสียงนกหวีด ใบหน้าของฉันมีรอยยิ้มและหัวใจของฉันก็เต้นรัว</u> เพราะรักแรกของฉันหลอมละลายที่นั่น เมื่อ 25 ปีที่แล้ว มีเด็กขี้อายคนหนึ่งเดินตามฉันไปทั่ว วันหนึ่งเด็กชายถาม
“คุณไม่ได้ยินเสียงนกหวีดเหรอ?”
เมื่อฉันบอกว่าไม่ได้ยิน เด็กชายก็พูดว่า "ทุกคืนฉันจะผิวปากออกไปนอกหน้าต่างบ้านคุณตอนเจ็ดโมงเช้า"
หลังจากนั้นก็มีเรื่องแปลกๆเกิดขึ้น นกหวีดที่ฉันไม่เคยได้ยินมาก่อนดังก้องอยู่ในหูของฉันอย่างชัดเจน
"นกหวีด~ นกหวีด~"
ฉันได้ยินมาตลอดเวลา 7.00 น. เมื่อเวลาผ่านไป ฉันเริ่มรอที่จะได้ยินเสียงนั้น และตอนนี้ ฉันแต่งงานกับเด็กผิวปากและใช้ชีวิตอย่างมีความสุขตลอดไป

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.

It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.pt:
Ainda hoje ouço um apito no meu celular. Gosto do som do apito o suficiente para considerá-lo um sino. <u>Quando ouço o apito, um sorriso surge em meu rosto e meu coração dispara</u>. É porque meu primeiro amor derreteu ali. Há 25 anos, havia um garoto tímido que me seguia. Um dia, o menino perguntou
“Você não ouviu o apito?”
Quando eu disse que não tinha ouvido falar, o menino disse: “Todas as noites eu assobio do lado de fora da sua janela às sete horas”.
Depois disso, uma coisa estranha aconteceu. Um assobio que eu nunca tinha ouvido antes soou claramente em meus ouvidos.
“Apito ~ Assobio ~.”
Era sempre às 19h que eu ouvia isso. Com o passar do tempo, comecei a esperar para ouvir aquele som, e agora estou casada com o menino que assobia e vivo feliz para sempre.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.
"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
g_text_translate.hi:
आज भी मुझे अपने सेल फोन पर सीटी की आवाज सुनाई देती है। मुझे सीटी की आवाज़ इतनी पसंद है कि मैं इसे घंटी के रूप में तय कर सकता हूँ। <u>जब मैं सीटी सुनता हूं, तो मेरे चेहरे पर मुस्कान आ जाती है और मेरा दिल धड़कने लगता है</u>। ऐसा इसलिए है क्योंकि मेरा पहला प्यार वहीं पिघल गया। 25 साल पहले, एक शर्मीला लड़का मेरे पीछे-पीछे घूमता रहता था। एक दिन लड़के ने पूछा
“क्या तुमने सीटी नहीं सुनी?”
जब मैंने कहा कि मैंने नहीं सुना, तो लड़के ने कहा, "मैं हर रात सात बजे आपकी खिड़की के बाहर सीटी बजाता हूँ।"
उसके बाद एक अजीब घटना घटी. एक सीटी जो मैंने पहले कभी नहीं सुनी थी, मेरे कानों में स्पष्ट रूप से बजी।
"सीटी~सीटी~।"
मैं इसे हमेशा शाम के 7:00 बजे सुनता था। जैसे-जैसे समय बीतता गया, मैं उस आवाज को सुनने का इंतजार करने लगी और अब मैंने सीटी बजाने वाले लड़के से शादी कर ली है और हमेशा खुशी से रहती हूं।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
Bugün bile cep telefonumda bir ıslık sesi duyuyorum. Düdüğün sesini, zil olduğuna karar verecek kadar seviyorum. <u>Düdüğü duyduğumda yüzümde bir gülümseme beliriyor ve kalbim hızla çarpıyor</u>. Çünkü ilk aşkım orada eridi. 25 yıl önce beni takip eden utangaç bir çocuk vardı. Bir gün oğlan sordu:
"Düdüğü duymadın mı?"
Ben duymadığımı söylediğimde çocuk, "Her gece saat yedide pencerenin önünde ıslık çalıyorum" dedi.
Bundan sonra tuhaf bir şey oldu. Daha önce hiç duymadığım bir ıslık sesi kulaklarımda çınladı.
"Islık~ Islık~."
Bunu her zaman akşam saat 7'de duyuyordum. Zaman geçtikçe o sesi duymayı beklemeye başladım ve artık ıslık çalan çocukla evliyim ve sonsuza kadar mutlu yaşıyorum.

#######

Even today, I hear a whistle on my cell phone. I like the sound of the whistle enough to decide it as a bell. <u>When I hear the whistle, a smile comes to my face and my heart races</u>. It's because my first love melted there. 25 years ago, there was a shy boy who followed me around. One day, the boy asked
“Did you not hear the whistle?”
When I said I hadn't heard, the boy said, "Every night I whistle outside your window at seven o'clock."
After that, a strange thing happened. A whistle that I had never heard before rang clearly in my ears.
"Whistle~ Whistle~."
It was always at 7:00 in the evening that I heard it. As time passed, I began to wait to hear that sound, and now I am married to the whistling boy and live happily ever after.
