I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.
g_text_translate.ru:
Это было тогда, когда я получил свою первую работу на неполный рабочий день. Однажды ребенок внезапно встал из-за стола покупателя, заказавшего еду, и ребенок пролил еду в штаны посетителю за соседним столиком. Я не мог ничего сказать, <u>мое лицо было красным, поэтому я просто стоял там</u>. Увидев это, начальник тут же побежал к заказчику и извинился, что из-за того, что я давно не работал, я плохо справляюсь с работой. И вместе со счетами за стирку он нашел где-то дополнительные штаны, которые можно обменять. Мне было так жаль, что я сказал своему начальнику, что допустил ошибку и что я исправлю ее и уволюсь с подработки. Начальник утешил меня, посоветовав в следующий раз быть осторожнее, сказав: «Ты сделал это не нарочно, возможно, это произошло потому, что это произошло внезапно». В то время теплые слова начальника придали мне большую силу и смелость.

#######

It was when I got my first part-time job. One day, the child suddenly got up from the table of a customer who ordered food, and the child spilled the food down the pants of a customer at the next table. I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.
g_text_translate.th:
มันเป็นตอนที่ฉันได้งานพาร์ทไทม์ครั้งแรก วันหนึ่ง จู่ๆ เด็กน้อยก็ลุกขึ้นจากโต๊ะของลูกค้าที่สั่งอาหาร และเด็กก็ทำอาหารหกใส่กางเกงของลูกค้าโต๊ะถัดไป ฉันไม่สามารถพูดอะไรได้ และ <u>หน้าของฉันแดง ดังนั้นฉันจึงยืนอยู่ที่นั่น</u> เห็นแบบนี้เจ้านายก็รีบวิ่งไปหาลูกค้าทันทีและขอโทษว่าเพราะไม่ได้ทำงานมานานจึงทำงานไม่เก่ง นอกจากค่าซักรีดแล้ว เขายังพบกางเกงสำรองจากที่ไหนสักแห่งที่สามารถแลกเปลี่ยนได้ ฉันเสียใจมากที่ฉันบอกเจ้านายว่าฉันได้ทำผิดพลาด และฉันจะชดเชยและลาออกจากงานพาร์ทไทม์ เจ้านายปลอบฉันโดยบอกคราวหน้าให้ฉันระวัง โดยบอกว่า “คุณไม่ได้ตั้งใจ อาจเป็นเพราะมันเกิดขึ้นกะทันหัน” ในเวลานั้นคำพูดอันอบอุ่นของเจ้านายทำให้ฉันมีความแข็งแกร่งและความกล้าหาญอย่างมาก

#######

It was when I got my first part-time job. One day, the child suddenly got up from the table of a customer who ordered food, and the child spilled the food down the pants of a customer at the next table. I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.
g_text_translate.pt:
Foi quando consegui meu primeiro emprego de meio período. Um dia, a criança levantou-se repentinamente da mesa de um cliente que pedia comida e derramou a comida nas calças de um cliente da mesa ao lado. Eu não consegui dizer nada e <u>meu rosto estava vermelho, então fiquei ali parado</u>. Vendo isso, o patrão correu imediatamente até o cliente e pediu desculpas porque, por eu não trabalhar há muito tempo, não era bom no trabalho. E junto com as contas da lavanderia, ele encontrou calças extras em algum lugar que podem ser trocadas. Lamentei muito ter dito ao meu chefe que cometi um erro e que iria compensar e largar meu emprego de meio período. O chefe me consolou dizendo-me para ter cuidado na próxima vez, dizendo: “Você não fez isso de propósito e pode ser porque aconteceu de repente”. Naquela época, as palavras calorosas do patrão me deram muita força e coragem.

#######

It was when I got my first part-time job. One day, the child suddenly got up from the table of a customer who ordered food, and the child spilled the food down the pants of a customer at the next table. I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.
g_text_translate.hi:
यह तब था जब मुझे अपनी पहली अंशकालिक नौकरी मिली थी। एक दिन खाना ऑर्डर करने वाले एक ग्राहक की टेबल से बच्चा अचानक उठ गया और बच्चे ने खाना अगली टेबल पर बैठे एक ग्राहक की पैंट के नीचे गिरा दिया। मैं कुछ नहीं कह सका और <u>मेरा चेहरा लाल हो गया था, इसलिए मैं वहीं खड़ा रहा</u>। यह देखकर बॉस तुरंत दौड़कर ग्राहक के पास गया और माफी मांगी कि क्योंकि मैंने काफी समय से काम नहीं किया था, इसलिए मैं काम में अच्छा नहीं था। और कपड़े धोने के बिल के साथ, उसे कहीं से अतिरिक्त पैंट भी मिली जिसे बदला जा सकता था। मुझे इतना अफ़सोस हुआ कि मैंने अपने बॉस से कहा कि मुझसे गलती हुई है और मैं इसकी भरपाई करूँगा और अपनी अंशकालिक नौकरी छोड़ दूँगा। बॉस ने मुझे सांत्वना देते हुए अगली बार सावधान रहने को कहा और कहा, "तुमने यह जानबूझकर नहीं किया और हो सकता है कि यह अचानक हो गया हो।" उस समय बॉस के स्नेहपूर्ण शब्दों ने मुझे बहुत शक्ति और साहस दिया।

#######

It was when I got my first part-time job. One day, the child suddenly got up from the table of a customer who ordered food, and the child spilled the food down the pants of a customer at the next table. I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.
g_text_translate.tr:
İlk yarı zamanlı işime başladığım zamandı. Bir gün yemek siparişi veren müşterinin masasından aniden çocuk kalkmış ve çocuk, yemeği yan masadaki müşterinin pantolonuna dökmüş. Hiçbir şey söyleyemedim ve <u>yüzüm kırmızıydı, bu yüzden orada öylece durdum</u>. Bunu gören patron hemen müşterinin yanına koştu ve uzun süredir çalışmadığım için işimde iyi olmadığım için özür diledi. Çamaşır faturalarının yanı sıra bir yerden değiştirilebilecek fazladan pantolon da buldu. Patronuma bir hata yaptığımı, bunu telafi edip yarı zamanlı işimden ayrılacağımı söylediğim için çok pişman oldum. Patron bir dahaki sefere dikkatli olmamı söyleyerek, "Bilerek yapmadın, aniden olduğu için olabilir" diyerek beni teselli etti. O dönemde patronun sıcak sözleri bana büyük güç ve cesaret vermişti.

#######

It was when I got my first part-time job. One day, the child suddenly got up from the table of a customer who ordered food, and the child spilled the food down the pants of a customer at the next table. I couldn't say anything and <u>my face was red, so I just stood there</u>. Seeing this, the boss immediately ran to the customer and apologized that because I had not worked for a long time, I was not good at work. And along with laundry bills, he found extra pants from somewhere that can be exchanged. I was so sorry that I told my boss that I had made a mistake and that I would make up for it and quit my part-time job. The boss consoled me by telling me to be careful next time, saying, "You didn't do it on purpose and it might be because it happened suddenly." At that time, the warm words of the boss gave me great strength and courage.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 손님은 나의 실수에 화가 났지만 계속 참았다.
나는 아이의 부모님께 사과를 받고 싶었다.
나는 사장님의 배려와 인품에 감동을 받았다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Khách hàng nổi giận với lỗi của tôi, nhưng tôi vẫn tiếp tục chịu đựng.
2. Tôi muốn một lời xin lỗi từ cha mẹ của đứa trẻ.
3. Tôi nghỉ việc vì mắc lỗi với khách hàng.
4. Tôi cảm động trước sự quan tâm và tính cách của ông chủ.
--------------------
Những lời nói ấm áp của chủ quán đã tiếp thêm sức mạnh và dũng khí cho nhân vật 'tôi'. 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:

2. I wanted an apology from the child's parents.

4. I was moved by the consideration and character of the boss.
--------------------
The boss's warm words gave strength and courage to this person. 4 is the correct answer.

4. Sono rimasto commosso dalla considerazione e dal carattere del capo.
--------------------

#######

general.g_text: 유치원에서 교사로 일한 지 4년이 넘었다. 우리 유치원은 건물 3층에 있어서 수업이 끝나면 계단을 이용해 아이들을 내보냈다. 행여 계단에서 아이들이 다칠까봐 수업이 끝날 때면 나는 물론이고 모든 동료 교사들이 신경을 쓰는 시간이다. 아이들을 좀 더 안전하고 질서 있게 보내고자 생각해 낸 것이 여자 아이들을 먼저 나가게 하는 것이었다. 평소처럼 수업이 끝나고 나는 "공주님들, 가방 챙겼지요? 자, 그럼 공주님들 먼저 밖으로 나가세요 ”라고 말했다. 그런데 한 남자 아이가 입술을 뻐쭉 내밀고 나를 쳐다보았다. 그러고는 손을 들고 "왜 맨날 맨날 공주님들만 먼저 나가요. 왕자님들도 순서를 바꿔 가면서 먼저 가게 해 주세요.”라고 말하는 것이었다. <u>순간 나는 할 말을 잃고 말았다</u>. 남자가 여자에게 양보하는 것이 당연하다는 나의 평소 생각을 되돌아보게 되었고 집에 빨리 가고 싶은 아이의 마음을 헤아리지 못한 것 같았기 때문이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Tôi đã làm giáo viên ở trường mẫu giáo được hơn 4 năm. Trường mẫu giáo của chúng tôi ở tầng 3 của tòa nhà nên sau khi kết thúc giờ học, chúng tôi đã dùng cầu thang để đưa các em đi. Biết đâu những đứa trẻ sẽ bị thương ở cầu thang nên khi kết thúc giờ học, tất nhiên đây là khoảng thời gian mà tôi và tất cả các giáo viên đồng nghiệp phải chú ý. Điều tôi nghĩ ra để gửi các em đi một cách an toàn và có trật tự hơn là đưa các bé gái ra ngoài trước. Sau khi giờ học kết thúc như thường lệ, tôi nói: "Các công chúa đã lấy ba lô cả chưa? Chà, vậy thì các công chúa hãy ra ngoài trước đi". Nhưng một cậu bé chìa môi ra và nhìn tôi chằm chằm. Sau đó cậu bé giơ tay lên và nói: "Tại sao ngày nào cũng chỉ có các công chúa ra ngoài trước. Hãy để các hoàng tử thay đổi thứ tự và đi trước đi ạ." <u>Trong khoảnh khắc tôi quên mất phải nói gì</u>. Bởi vì tôi nhìn lại suy nghĩ thường ngày của mình rằng đàn ông nhường nhịn phụ nữ là điều đương nhiên và dường như tôi đã không hiểu được tâm trạng của đứa trẻ muốn về nhà sớm.
g_text_translate.en: I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.it:
Lavoro come maestra d'asilo da oltre 4 anni. La nostra scuola materna si trova al 3° piano dell'edificio, quindi quando le lezioni finiscono usiamo le scale per mandare fuori i bambini. È un momento in cui tutti i miei colleghi insegnanti, compreso me, prestano attenzione quando la lezione finisce, temendo che i bambini si facciano male sulle scale. L’idea per mandare i bambini un po’ più sicuri e ordinati era di lasciare andare prima le ragazze. Dopo la lezione, come al solito, ho detto: “Principesse, avete fatto le valigie? Ora, principesse, uscite prima”. Ma un ragazzo ha imbronciato le labbra e mi ha guardato. Poi alzò la mano e disse: "Perché le principesse vanno sempre per prime? Per favore, cambia l'ordine e lascia andare i principi per primi". <u>In quel momento ero senza parole.</u>. Era perché ripensavo ai miei soliti pensieri che era naturale per un uomo cedere a una donna, e non mi sembrava di capire il desiderio del bambino di tornare a casa velocemente.

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.fr:
Je travaille comme enseignante en maternelle depuis plus de 4 ans. Notre jardin d'enfants est au 3ème étage du bâtiment, donc une fois les cours terminés, nous utilisons les escaliers pour faire sortir les enfants. C'est un moment où tous mes collègues enseignants, ainsi que moi, sont attentifs à la fin des cours, craignant que les enfants ne se blessent dans les escaliers. L’idée pour rendre les enfants un peu plus sûrs et plus ordonnés était de laisser les filles partir en premier. Après le cours, comme d'habitude, j'ai dit : « Princesses, avez-vous fait vos valises ? Maintenant, princesses, sortez d’abord. Mais un garçon a fait la moue et m'a regardé. Puis il leva la main et dit : « Pourquoi les princesses commencent-elles toujours en premier ? S'il vous plaît, changez l'ordre et laissez les princes partir en premier. <u>À ce moment-là, j'étais sans voix.</u>. C'est parce que je repensais à mes pensées habituelles qu'il était naturel qu'un homme cède devant une femme, et je ne semblais pas comprendre le désir de l'enfant de rentrer rapidement à la maison.

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.es:
Trabajo como maestra de jardín de infantes desde hace más de 4 años. Nuestro jardín de infantes está en el tercer piso del edificio, por lo que cuando terminan las clases, usamos las escaleras para enviar a los niños. Es un momento en el que todos mis compañeros profesores, así como yo, prestamos atención cuando termina la clase, por temor a que los niños se lastimen en las escaleras. La idea de enviar a los niños un poco más seguros y ordenados era dejar ir primero a las niñas. Después de clase, como de costumbre, dije: “Princesas, ¿han hecho las maletas? Ahora, princesas, salgan primero”. Pero un niño hizo un puchero y me miró. Luego levantó la mano y dijo: "¿Por qué las princesas siempre van primero? Por favor, cambia el orden y deja que los príncipes vayan primero". <u>En ese momento, me quedé sin palabras.</u>. Fue porque recordé mis pensamientos habituales que era natural que un hombre cediera ante una mujer, y no parecía entender el deseo del niño de irse a casa rápidamente.

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.id:
Saya telah bekerja sebagai guru taman kanak-kanak selama lebih dari 4 tahun. Taman kanak-kanak kami berada di lantai 3 gedung, jadi setelah kelas selesai, kami menggunakan tangga untuk mengantar anak-anak keluar. Ini adalah saat dimana semua rekan guru, termasuk saya, memperhatikan saat kelas selesai, karena takut anak-anak akan terluka di tangga. Gagasan untuk menyekolahkan anak-anak sedikit lebih aman dan tertib adalah dengan membiarkan gadis-gadis itu pergi terlebih dahulu. Setelah kelas selesai seperti biasa, saya berkata, “Putri, apakah Anda sudah mengemas tas Anda? Sekarang, tuan putri, keluarlah dulu.” Tapi seorang anak laki-laki mencibir bibirnya dan menatapku. Lalu dia mengangkat tangannya dan berkata, "Mengapa para putri selalu pergi duluan? Tolong ubah urutannya dan biarkan para pangeran pergi dulu." <u>Pada saat itu, saya terdiam.</u>. Itu karena aku mengingat kembali pemikiranku yang biasa bahwa wajar bagi seorang pria untuk mengalah pada seorang wanita, dan sepertinya aku tidak memahami keinginan anak itu untuk segera pulang.

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.ja:
私は幼稚園の先生として4年以上働いています。私たちの幼稚園はビルの3階にあるので、授業が終わると階段を使って送り出します。私だけでなく同僚の先生たちも授業が終わると、子供たちが階段で怪我をするのではないかと気を配る時期です。子どもたちをもう少し安全かつ秩序正しく送るという考えは、女の子たちを最初に行かせることでした。いつものように授業が終わった後、私はこう言いました。さあ、お姫様たち、まずは外へ出てください。」しかし、男の子は唇を尖らせて私を見つめました。それから彼は手を挙げて言いました、「なぜいつもお姫様が先に行くのですか？順番を変えて王子様を先に行かせてください。」その瞬間<u>私は言葉を失いました</u>。それは、男が女に屈するのが当然だという普段の考えを振り返っていたからで、早く帰りたいという子供の気持ちが理解できなかったようだ。

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.de:
Ich arbeite seit über 4 Jahren als Kindergärtnerin. Unser Kindergarten befindet sich im 3. Stock des Gebäudes. Wenn der Unterricht vorbei ist, schicken wir die Kinder über die Treppe hinaus. Es ist eine Zeit, in der alle meine Lehrerkollegen und auch ich aufmerksam sind, wenn der Unterricht zu Ende ist, aus Angst, dass die Kinder auf der Treppe verletzt werden könnten. Die Idee, die Kinder etwas sicherer und geordneter zu schicken, bestand darin, den Mädchen den Vortritt zu lassen. Nach dem Unterricht sagte ich wie immer: „Prinzessinnen, habt ihr eure Koffer gepackt?“ Nun, Prinzessinnen, geht zuerst nach draußen.“ Aber ein Junge schmollte und sah mich an. Dann hob er seine Hand und sagte: „Warum gehen die Prinzessinnen immer zuerst? Bitte ändern Sie die Reihenfolge und lassen Sie die Prinzen zuerst gehen.“ <u>In diesem Moment war ich sprachlos.</u>. Das lag daran, dass ich auf meine üblichen Gedanken zurückblickte, dass es für einen Mann ganz natürlich sei, einer Frau nachzugeben, und ich schien den Wunsch des Kindes, schnell nach Hause zu gehen, nicht zu verstehen.

#######

I have been working as a kindergarten teacher for over 4 years. Our kindergarten is on the 3rd floor of the building, so when class is over, we use the stairs to send the children out. It is a time when all my fellow teachers, as well as me, pay attention when class is over, fearing that the children will get hurt on the stairs. The idea to send the children a little safer and orderly was to let the girls go first. After class as usual, I said, “Princesses, have you packed your bags? Now, princesses, go outside first.” But a boy pouted his lips and looked at me. Then he raised his hand and said, "Why do the princesses always go first? Please change the order and let the princes go first." <u>At that moment, I was speechless.</u>. It was because I looked back on my usual thoughts that it was natural for a man to yield to a woman, and I didn't seem to understand the child's desire to go home quickly.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
我担任幼儿园老师已有四年多了。我们的幼儿园在大楼的三楼，所以放学后我们都是用楼梯送孩子们出去。下课的时候，我和我所有的老师同事都会集中注意力，生怕孩子们在楼梯上受伤。为了让孩子们更安全、更有秩序，我们的想法是让女孩们先走。下课后，我像往常一样说：“公主们，你们收拾行李了吗？现在，公主们，先出去吧。”但是一个男孩撅起嘴唇看着我。然后他举手说道：“为什么总是公主们先走？请改变一下顺序，让王子们先走。” <u>那一刻，我无语了。</u>。就是因为回想自己平时的想法，男人向女人屈服是理所当然的事情，而我似乎不太理解孩子想要快点回家的心情。

g_text_translate.ru:
Работаю воспитателем в детском саду более 4 лет. Наш детский сад находится на 3 этаже здания, поэтому, когда занятия заканчиваются, мы выводим детей по лестнице. Это время, когда все мои коллеги-учителя, как и я, обращают внимание на окончание урока, опасаясь, что дети поранятся на лестнице. Идея сделать детей более безопасными и организованными заключалась в том, чтобы отпустить девочек первыми. После занятий я, как обычно, сказала: «Принцессы, вы уже собрали чемоданы? А теперь, принцессы, сначала выйдите наружу. Но мальчик надул губы и посмотрел на меня. Затем он поднял руку и сказал: «Почему принцессы всегда идут первыми? Пожалуйста, измените порядок и позвольте принцам идти первыми». <u>В этот момент я потерял дар речи.</u>. Именно потому, что я оглянулся на свои обычные мысли о том, что для мужчины естественно уступить женщине, и я, казалось, не понимал желания ребенка поскорее пойти домой.

g_text_translate.th:
ฉันทำงานเป็นครูอนุบาลมา4ปีกว่าแล้ว โรงเรียนอนุบาลของเราอยู่บนชั้น 3 ของอาคาร ดังนั้นเมื่อเลิกเรียนเราก็ใช้บันไดเพื่อส่งเด็กออกไป เป็นช่วงเวลาที่เพื่อนครูทุกคนรวมทั้งฉันให้ความสนใจเมื่อเลิกเรียนด้วยกลัวว่าเด็กๆ จะได้รับบาดเจ็บบนบันได ความคิดที่จะส่งเด็กๆ ให้ปลอดภัยและเป็นระเบียบมากขึ้นอีกหน่อยคือปล่อยให้เด็กผู้หญิงไปก่อน หลังเลิกเรียนตามปกติ ฉันก็พูดว่า “เจ้าหญิง คุณเก็บกระเป๋าแล้วหรือยัง? ตอนนี้เจ้าหญิงออกไปข้างนอกก่อน” แต่มีเด็กชายคนหนึ่งเม้มริมฝีปากแล้วมองมาที่ฉัน จากนั้นเขาก็ยกมือขึ้นแล้วพูดว่า "ทำไมเจ้าหญิงถึงไปก่อนเสมอ โปรดเปลี่ยนลำดับและให้เจ้าชายไปก่อน" <u>ในขณะนั้น ฉันก็พูดไม่ออก</u> เป็นเพราะฉันมองย้อนกลับไปในความคิดปกติของฉันว่ามันเป็นเรื่องธรรมดาที่ผู้ชายจะยอมจำนนต่อผู้หญิง และดูเหมือนฉันจะไม่เข้าใจความปรารถนาของเด็กที่จะกลับบ้านอย่างรวดเร็ว

g_text_translate.pt:
Trabalho como professora de jardim de infância há mais de 4 anos. Nosso jardim de infância fica no 3º andar do prédio, então quando a aula acaba usamos a escada para mandar as crianças sair. É um momento em que todos os meus colegas professores, assim como eu, prestamos atenção quando a aula acaba, temendo que as crianças se machuquem na escada. A ideia de deixar as crianças um pouco mais seguras e organizadas era deixar as meninas irem primeiro. Depois da aula, como sempre, eu disse: “Princesas, vocês já fizeram as malas? Agora, princesas, saiam primeiro.” Mas um menino fez beicinho e olhou para mim. Então ele ergueu a mão e disse: “Por que as princesas sempre vão primeiro? Por favor, mude a ordem e deixe os príncipes irem primeiro”. <u>Naquele momento, fiquei sem palavras.</u>. Foi porque relembrei meus pensamentos habituais que era natural que um homem se rendesse a uma mulher, e eu não parecia entender o desejo da criança de voltar para casa rapidamente.

#######

g_text_translate.hi:
मैं 4 वर्षों से अधिक समय से किंडरगार्टन शिक्षक के रूप में काम कर रहा हूँ। हमारा किंडरगार्टन इमारत की तीसरी मंजिल पर है, इसलिए जब कक्षा खत्म हो जाती है, तो हम बच्चों को बाहर भेजने के लिए सीढ़ियों का उपयोग करते हैं। यह एक ऐसा समय है जब मेरे साथ-साथ मेरे सभी साथी शिक्षक कक्षा समाप्त होने पर ध्यान देते हैं, इस डर से कि सीढ़ियों पर बच्चों को चोट लग जाएगी। बच्चों को थोड़ा सुरक्षित और व्यवस्थित भेजने का विचार यह था कि लड़कियों को पहले जाने दिया जाए। हमेशा की तरह कक्षा के बाद, मैंने कहा, “राजकुमारियाँ, क्या आपने अपना बैग पैक कर लिया है? अब, राजकुमारियों, पहले बाहर जाओ। लेकिन एक लड़के ने अपने होंठ फैलाए और मेरी तरफ देखा। फिर उसने अपना हाथ उठाया और कहा, "राजकुमारियाँ हमेशा पहले क्यों जाती हैं? कृपया क्रम बदलें और राजकुमारों को पहले जाने दें।" <u>उस पल, मैं अवाक रह गया।</u>। ऐसा इसलिए था क्योंकि मैंने अपने सामान्य विचारों पर नज़र डाली कि एक पुरुष के लिए एक महिला के सामने झुकना स्वाभाविक है, और मुझे बच्चे की जल्दी घर जाने की इच्छा समझ में नहीं आई।

g_text_translate.tr:
4 yılı aşkın süredir anaokulu öğretmeni olarak çalışıyorum. Anaokulumuz binanın 3. katında olduğundan ders bittiğinde çocukları dışarı göndermek için merdivenleri kullanıyoruz. Benim gibi tüm öğretmen arkadaşlarımın da çocukların merdivenlerde yaralanmasından korkarak ders bitimine dikkat ettikleri bir dönemdir. Çocukları biraz daha güvenli ve düzenli göndermenin amacı kızların önce gitmesine izin vermekti. Dersten sonra her zamanki gibi "Prensesler çantalarınızı topladınız mı?" dedim. Şimdi prensesler, önce dışarı çıkın.” Ama bir çocuk dudaklarını büküp bana baktı. Sonra elini kaldırdı ve şöyle dedi: "Neden her zaman prensesler ilk önce gider? Lütfen sırayı değiştirin ve prenslerin ilk önce gitmesine izin verin." <u>O anda suskundum.</u>. Bunun nedeni, bir erkeğin bir kadına teslim olmasının doğal olduğu yönündeki her zamanki düşüncelerime baktığımda ve çocuğun bir an önce eve gitme arzusunu anlamıyormuş gibi görünmemdi.

#######

general.g_text:
오늘은 6월 6일 현충일이다. 그래서 우리 고모 이야기를 좀 하려고 한다.
우리 고모는 스무 살에 결혼을 했는데 결혼을 하자마자 곧 전쟁이 터졌다. 그리고 고모부가 군인으로 전쟁터에 나간 후 다시는 얼굴을 볼 수 없었다. 죽었는지 살았는지 알 수 없었기 때문에 고모는 매일 고모부가 돌아오기를 기도했지만 소용이 없었다. 전쟁이 끝난 후까지도 연락이 없었을 때는 <u>거의 미칠 지경이었다</u>. 할머니는 고모가 고모부를 계속해서 기다리게 할 수 없다면서 이제는 죽은 셈치고 그만 기다리라고 위로했다. 그때부터 지금까지 고모는 재혼하지 않고 혼자 살고 있다. 그리고 전쟁 때 다친 사람들을 위해 병원에서 일하면서 보람을 느끼고 있다.

g_text_translate.vi:
Hôm nay là ngày liệt sĩ 6 tháng 6. Vì vậy tôi muốn kể một chút về cô tôi.
Cô tôi kết hôn năm 20 tuổi và ngay sau khi cô kết hôn, chiến tranh nổ ra. Và sau khi dượng tôi ra chiến trường với tư cách là một người lính, cô đã không thể gặp lại dượng nữa. Vì không biết dượng đã chết hay còn sống nên ngày nào cô cũng cầu nguyện cho dượng quay về nhưng không có tác dụng gì. Khi chiến tranh kết thúc nhưng vẫn không có liên lạc gì, <u>cô gần như phát điên</u>. Bà an ủi cô rằng bây giờ hãy xem như dượng đã chết và cô đừng chờ nữa. Từ đó đến nay, cô tôi không tái hôn mà sống một mình. Và tôi cảm thấy có ý nghĩa khi làm việc tại bệnh viện vì những người bị thương trong chiến tranh.
g_text_translate.en:
Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. And I feel rewarded working in a hospital for people who were injured during the war.
g_text_translate.it:
Oggi, 6 giugno, è la Giornata della Memoria. Allora ti racconterò un po' di mia zia.
Mia zia si sposò quando aveva 20 anni e subito dopo il matrimonio scoppiò la guerra. E dopo che mio zio andò sul campo di battaglia come soldato, non vide mai più la sua faccia. Non si sapeva se fosse vivo o morto, quindi mia zia pregava ogni giorno affinché mio zio tornasse, ma senza successo. Anche dopo la guerra, quando non ci furono contatti, <u>quasi impazzì</u>. Mia nonna consolò mia zia a non aspettare, dicendole di non aspettare ma di fingere che fosse morto. Da allora fino ad oggi mia zia non si è più risposata e vive da sola. E mi sento gratificato lavorando in un ospedale per persone ferite durante la guerra.

#######

Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. And I feel rewarded working in a hospital for people who were injured during the war.
g_text_translate.fr:
Aujourd'hui, le 6 juin, c'est le Memorial Day. Alors je vais vous parler un peu de ma tante.
Ma tante s'est mariée quand elle avait 20 ans et peu après le mariage, la guerre a éclaté. Et après que mon oncle soit allé sur le champ de bataille en tant que soldat, elle n'a plus jamais revu son visage. On ne savait pas s'il était mort ou vivant, alors ma tante priait chaque jour pour que mon oncle revienne, mais en vain. Même après la guerre, lorsqu'il n'y avait aucun contact, <u>elle devenait presque folle</u>. Ma grand-mère a réconforté ma tante pour qu'elle ne attende pas, lui disant de ne pas attendre mais de faire comme s'il était mort. Depuis, ma tante ne s'est pas remariée et vit seule. Et je me sens récompensé de travailler dans un hôpital pour les personnes blessées pendant la guerre.

#######

Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. And I feel rewarded working in a hospital for people who were injured during the war.
g_text_translate.es:
Hoy, 6 de junio, es el Día de los Caídos. Entonces les voy a contar un poco sobre mi tía.
Mi tía se casó cuando tenía 20 años y poco después de la boda estalló la guerra. Y después de que mi tío fue al campo de batalla como soldado, nunca volvió a ver su rostro. No se sabía si estaba vivo o muerto, por lo que mi tía oraba todos los días para que mi tío regresara, pero fue en vano. Incluso después de la guerra, cuando no hubo contacto, <u>casi se volvió loca</u>. Mi abuela consoló a mi tía para que no esperara, diciéndole que no esperara sino que fingiera que él estaba muerto. Desde entonces hasta ahora mi tía no se ha vuelto a casar y vive sola. Y me siento recompensado trabajando en un hospital para personas que resultaron heridas durante la guerra.

#######

Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. And I feel rewarded working in a hospital for people who were injured during the war.
g_text_translate.id:
Hari ini, 6 Juni adalah Hari Peringatan. Jadi aku akan bercerita sedikit tentang bibiku.
Bibi saya menikah ketika dia berusia 20 tahun, dan segera setelah pernikahan tersebut, perang pun pecah. Dan setelah paman saya pergi ke medan perang sebagai tentara, dia tidak pernah melihat wajahnya lagi. Tidak diketahui apakah dia hidup atau mati, jadi bibi saya berdoa setiap hari agar paman saya kembali, tetapi tidak berhasil. Bahkan setelah perang, ketika tidak ada kontak, <u>dia hampir menjadi gila</u>. Nenek menghibur bibiku untuk tidak menunggu, menyuruhnya untuk tidak menunggu tetapi berpura-pura dia sudah mati. Sejak saat itu hingga saat ini, tante saya belum menikah lagi dan hidup sendiri. Dan saya merasa dihargai bekerja di rumah sakit untuk orang-orang yang terluka selama perang.

#######

Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. And I feel rewarded working in a hospital for people who were injured during the war.
g_text_translate.ja:
本日6月6日は戦没将兵追悼記念日です。そこで、私の叔母について少しお話します。
私の叔母は20歳のときに結婚しましたが、結婚後すぐに戦争が始まりました。そして叔父が兵士として戦場に行った後、彼女は二度と叔父の顔を見ることはなかった。生死も不明だったので、叔母は毎日叔父が戻ってくるように祈りましたが、無駄でした。戦後も連絡が取れなかったときは<u>気が狂いそうになった</u>そうです。祖母は叔母に、待たずに死んだふりをしなさいと言って慰めました。それ以来現在に至るまで叔母は再婚せず一人暮らしをしています。そして、戦争中に負傷した人々のための病院で働くことにやりがいを感じています。

#######

Today, June 6th, is Memorial Day. So I'm going to tell you a little bit about my aunt.
My aunt got married when she was 20, and soon after the wedding, war broke out. And after my uncle went to the battlefield as a soldier, she never saw his face again. It was unknown whether he was dead or alive, so my aunt prayed every day for my uncle to return, but to no avail. Even after the war, when there was no contact, <u>she almost went mad</u>. My grandmother comforted my aunt not to wait, telling her not to wait but to pretend he was dead. Since then until now, my aunt has not remarried and is living alone. g_text_translate.de:
Heute, am 6. Juni, ist Memorial Day. Deshalb werde ich Ihnen ein wenig über meine Tante erzählen.
Meine Tante heiratete, als sie 20 war, und kurz nach der Hochzeit brach der Krieg aus. Und nachdem mein Onkel als Soldat auf das Schlachtfeld gezogen war, sah sie sein Gesicht nie wieder. Es war nicht bekannt, ob er tot oder lebendig war, also betete meine Tante jeden Tag für die Rückkehr meines Onkels, aber ohne Erfolg. Auch nach dem Krieg, als es keinen Kontakt mehr gab, <u>verfiel sie fast in den Wahnsinn</u>. Meine Großmutter tröstete meine Tante, nicht zu warten, und sagte ihr, sie solle nicht warten, sondern so tun, als wäre er tot. Seitdem hat meine Tante bis heute nicht wieder geheiratet und lebt allein. Und ich fühle mich belohnt, in einem Krankenhaus für Menschen zu arbeiten, die während des Krieges verletzt wurden.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
今天，6月6日，是阵亡将士纪念日。所以我要告诉你一些关于我姨妈的事情。
我姨妈20岁的时候结婚了，结婚后不久，战争就爆发了。而舅舅当兵上战场后，她就再也没有见过他的脸。不知他是死是活，姨妈每天都祈祷舅舅能回来，但都无济于事。即使在战争结束后，当没有联系时，<u>她几乎要疯了</u>。奶奶安慰姑姑别等了，让她别等了，就装死吧。从那时起一直到现在，姑姑都没有再婚，一个人生活。我觉得在医院为战争期间受伤的人工作是值得的。

g_text_translate.ru:
Сегодня, 6 июня, День памяти. Итак, я собираюсь рассказать вам немного о моей тете.
Моя тетя вышла замуж, когда ей было 20, и вскоре после свадьбы началась война. А после того, как мой дядя ушел на поле боя солдатом, она больше никогда не видела его лица. Неизвестно, жив он или мертв, поэтому тетя каждый день молилась о возвращении дяди, но безуспешно. Даже после войны, когда контакта не было, <u>она чуть не сошла с ума</u>. Моя бабушка утешала мою тетю, чтобы она не ждала, сказав ей не ждать, а притвориться, что он мертв. С тех пор и по сей день моя тетя повторно не выходила замуж и живет одна. И я чувствую себя вознагражденным, работая в больнице для людей, получивших ранения во время войны.

ป้าของฉันแต่งงานตอนที่เธออายุ 20 ปี และไม่นานหลังจากงานแต่งงาน สงครามก็ปะทุขึ้น และหลังจากที่ลุงของฉันไปสนามรบในฐานะทหาร เธอก็ไม่เคยเห็นหน้าเขาอีกเลย ไม่มีใครรู้ว่าเขาตายหรือมีชีวิตอยู่ ป้าของฉันจึงสวดภาวนาทุกวันเพื่อให้ลุงกลับมา แต่ก็ไม่มีประโยชน์ แม้หลังสงคราม เมื่อไม่มีการติดต่อใดๆ <u>เธอก็แทบจะเป็นบ้า</u> คุณยายปลอบป้าว่าไม่ต้องรอ โดยบอกเธอว่าอย่ารอแต่ให้ทำเป็นว่าเขาตายแล้ว ตั้งแต่นั้นมาจนถึงตอนนี้ป้าของฉันยังไม่ได้แต่งงานใหม่และอยู่คนเดียว และฉันรู้สึกได้รับรางวัลที่ได้ทำงานในโรงพยาบาลสำหรับผู้ที่ได้รับบาดเจ็บระหว่างสงคราม

g_text_translate.pt:
Hoje, 6 de junho, é o Memorial Day. Então vou contar um pouco sobre minha tia.
Minha tia se casou quando tinha 20 anos e logo após o casamento estourou a guerra. E depois que meu tio foi para o campo de batalha como soldado, ela nunca mais viu o rosto dele. Não se sabia se ele estava vivo ou morto, então minha tia orava todos os dias para que meu tio voltasse, mas sem sucesso. Mesmo depois da guerra, quando não houve contato, <u>ela quase enlouqueceu</u>. Minha avó confortou minha tia para não esperar, dizendo-lhe para não esperar, mas fingir que ele estava morto. Desde então até agora, minha tia não se casou novamente e mora sozinha. E sinto-me recompensado por trabalhar num hospital para pessoas que ficaram feridas durante a guerra.

g_text_translate.hi:
आज, 6 जून, स्मृति दिवस है। तो मैं आपको अपनी चाची के बारे में थोड़ा बताने जा रहा हूँ।
मेरी चाची की शादी तब हुई जब वह 20 साल की थीं और शादी के तुरंत बाद युद्ध छिड़ गया। और मेरे चाचा के एक सैनिक के रूप में युद्ध के मैदान में जाने के बाद, उसने फिर कभी उनका चेहरा नहीं देखा। यह अज्ञात था कि वह मर गया था या जीवित था, इसलिए मेरी चाची हर दिन मेरे चाचा के वापस आने के लिए प्रार्थना करती थी, लेकिन कोई फायदा नहीं हुआ। युद्ध के बाद भी, जब कोई संपर्क नहीं हुआ, <u>वह लगभग पागल हो गई थी</u>। मेरी दादी ने मेरी चाची को इंतज़ार न करने के लिए सांत्वना दी और कहा कि इंतज़ार न करें बल्कि यह दिखावा करें कि वह मर गया है। तब से लेकर अब तक मेरी चाची ने दोबारा शादी नहीं की है और अकेली ही रह रही हैं. और मैं युद्ध के दौरान घायल हुए लोगों के लिए अस्पताल में काम करके पुरस्कृत महसूस करता हूं।

g_text_translate.tr:
Bugün 6 Haziran Anma Günü. Bu yüzden size biraz teyzemden bahsedeceğim.
Teyzem 20 yaşındayken evlendi ve düğünün hemen ardından savaş çıktı. Amcam asker olarak savaş alanına gittikten sonra onun yüzünü bir daha görmedi. Onun ölü mü diri mi olduğu bilinmiyordu, bu yüzden teyzem amcamın geri dönmesi için her gün dua etti ama işe yaramadı. Savaştan sonra bile hiçbir temas olmayınca <u>neredeyse deliriyordu</u>. Büyükannem teyzemi beklememesi konusunda teselli etti, ona beklememesini, ölmüş gibi davranmasını söyledi. O günden bugüne teyzem yeniden evlenmedi ve yalnız yaşıyor. Ve savaş sırasında yaralanan insanların tedavi edildiği bir hastanede çalışmanın ödüllendirildiğini hissediyorum.

#######

general.g_text: 우리 아이가 여섯 살 때의 일이다. 남편이 기자라서 집에 영화표가 많이 들어왔다. 그 중 '슈퍼맨' 영화표가 눈에 띄어 아이와 함께 영화를 보러 갔다. 영화를 보고 와서 아이는 자기가 슈퍼맨이 되기라도 한 듯이 빨간색 보자기를 등 뒤에 두르고 온 집안과 동네를 헤집고 다녔다. 처음에는 ‘며칠 저러다가 말겠지’라고 생각을 했다. 그러던 어느 날 마당에서 빨래를 널고 있는데 2층 아이 방 창문에 아이가 빨간 보자기를 두르고 서 있었다. 그러더니 아이는 "엄마, 나 날 수 있어. 자, 간다” 하고 2층에서 뛰어내리는 것이 아닌가. 순간 나는 <u>아무 말도 할 수 없었고 몸도 움직일 수가 없었다</u>. 천만다행으로 아이가 빨랫줄에 걸려 마당에 사뿐히 서게 되었다. 아이는 자기가 얼마나 위험했는지 알지 못하고 날았다며 기뻐하고 있었다. 나는 아이에게 달려가 아무 말 없이 엉덩이를 때릴 뿐 아무 말도 할 수가없었다.

g_text_translate.vi: Chuyện xảy ra khi con tôi sáu tuổi. Vì chồng tôi là nhà báo nên nhận được rất nhiều vé xem phim ở nhà. Trong số đó, nổi bật là vé xem phim 'Siêu nhân' nên tôi cùng con đi xem phim. Sau khi xem phim về, đứa trẻ đeo một tấm vải đỏ trên lưng như thể nó đã trở thành siêu nhân và chạy quanh nhà và quanh xóm. Lúc đầu, tôi nghĩ, 'Chắc nó sẽ không như thế nữa trong vài ngày'. Rồi một ngày nọ khi tôi đang phơi đồ giặt ngoài sân, con tôi đang đứng ở cửa sổ phòng con trên tầng 2, choàng một tấm vải đỏ. Sau đó, đứa trẻ nói: "Mẹ ơi, con có thể bay. Nào, đi thôi" và nhảy từ tầng 2. Trong giây lát, tôi <u>không nói được gì và cũng không thể cử động</u>. May mà con bị vướng trên dây phơi nên đứng một cách nhẹ nhàng xuống sân. Con vui mừng nói rằng mình đã bay mà không biết nó nguy hiểm thế nào. Tôi chạy đến chỉ tát vào mông nó mà không nói gì vì không thể nói được bất cứ điều gì.
g_text_translate.en: This happened when my son was six years old. My husband is a journalist, so there are a lot of movie tickets in the house. Among them, the 'Superman' movie ticket caught my eye, so I went to see the movie with my child. After watching the movie, the child put a red cloth behind his back as if he had become Superman and wandered around the whole house and neighborhood. At first, I thought, ‘He'll do that for a few days and then stop’. Then one day, while I was hanging out the laundry in the yard, the child was standing in the window of the second-floor child's room, wearing a red cloth. Then the child said, "Mom, I can fly. Come on, let's go" and jumped off the second floor. At that moment, I <u>couldn't say anything or even move my body</u>. Thankfully, the child got caught on the clothesline and stood softly in the yard. The child was delighted to say that he had flown without knowing how dangerous he was. I ran up to him and hit him on the butt without saying anything.
g_text_translate.it:
Questo è successo quando mio figlio aveva sei anni. Mio marito è un giornalista, quindi ci sono molti biglietti per il cinema in casa. Tra questi, il biglietto del cinema "Superman" ha attirato la mia attenzione, quindi sono andato a vedere il film con mio figlio. Dopo aver visto il film, il bambino si mise un panno rosso dietro la schiena come se fosse diventato Superman e vagò per tutta la casa e il quartiere. All’inizio ho pensato: “Lo farà per qualche giorno e poi smetterà”. Poi un giorno, mentre stavo stendendo la biancheria in cortile, il bambino stava davanti alla finestra della cameretta del secondo piano, con indosso un panno rosso. Allora il bambino ha detto: "Mamma, so volare. Vieni, andiamo" e si è buttato dal secondo piano. In quel momento, <u>non potevo dire nulla e nemmeno muovere il corpo</u>. Per fortuna, il bambino è rimasto impigliato nella corda da bucato ed è rimasto in silenzio nel cortile. Il bambino fu felicissimo di dire che aveva volato senza sapere quanto fosse pericoloso. Gli sono corsa incontro e l'ho colpito sul sedere senza dire nulla.

#######

This happened when my son was six years old. My husband is a journalist, so there are a lot of movie tickets in the house. Among them, the 'Superman' movie ticket caught my eye, so I went to see the movie with my child. After watching the movie, the child put a red cloth behind his back as if he had become Superman and wandered around the whole house and neighborhood. At first, I thought, ‘He'll do that for a few days and then stop’. Come on, let's go" and jumped off the second floor. At that moment, I <u>couldn't say anything or even move my body</u>. g_text_translate.fr:
Cela s'est produit lorsque mon fils avait six ans. Mon mari est journaliste, donc il y a beaucoup de billets de cinéma à la maison. Parmi eux, le billet de cinéma « Superman » m'a attiré l'attention, alors je suis allé voir le film avec mon enfant. Après avoir regardé le film, l'enfant a mis un tissu rouge derrière son dos comme s'il était devenu Superman et a erré dans toute la maison et le quartier. Au début, je pensais : « Il va faire ça pendant quelques jours puis arrêter ». Puis un jour, alors que j'étendais le linge dans la cour, l'enfant se tenait à la fenêtre de la chambre d'enfant du deuxième étage, vêtu d'un drap rouge. Puis l'enfant a dit : « Maman, je peux voler. Allez, allons-y » et a sauté du deuxième étage. À ce moment-là, je <u>ne pouvais rien dire ni même bouger mon corps</u>. Heureusement, l'enfant s'est accroché à la corde à linge et s'est tenu tranquillement dans la cour. L'enfant était ravi de dire qu'il avait volé sans savoir à quel point il était dangereux. J'ai couru vers lui et je l'ai frappé sur les fesses sans rien dire.

g_text_translate.es:
Esto sucedió cuando mi hijo tenía seis años. Mi marido es periodista, así que hay muchas entradas de cine en casa. Entre ellas, me llamó la atención la entrada al cine 'Superman', así que fui a ver la película con mi hijo. Después de ver la película, el niño se puso un paño rojo a la espalda como si se hubiera convertido en Superman y deambuló por toda la casa y el barrio. Al principio pensé: "Hará eso durante unos días y luego dejará de hacerlo". Entonces, un día, mientras estaba tendiendo la ropa en el patio, el niño estaba parado en la ventana de la habitación infantil del segundo piso, vestido con un paño rojo. Entonces el niño dijo: "Mamá, puedo volar. Vamos, vámonos" y saltó del segundo piso. En ese momento, <u>no podía decir nada ni siquiera mover mi cuerpo</u>. Afortunadamente, el niño quedó atrapado en el tendedero y se quedó quieto en el patio. El niño dijo encantado que había volado sin saber lo peligroso que era. Corrí hacia él y le di un golpe en el trasero sin decir nada.

g_text_translate.id:
Ini terjadi ketika anak saya berusia enam tahun. Suami saya seorang jurnalis, jadi banyak tiket bioskop di rumah. Diantaranya, tiket film 'Superman' menarik perhatian saya, jadi saya pergi menonton film tersebut bersama anak saya. Usai menonton film, anak tersebut memasang kain merah di belakang punggungnya seolah-olah ia telah menjadi Superman dan berkeliaran di seluruh rumah dan lingkungan. Pada awalnya, saya berpikir, ‘Dia akan melakukan itu selama beberapa hari dan kemudian berhenti’. Lalu suatu hari, ketika saya sedang menjemur pakaian di halaman, anak itu sedang berdiri di jendela kamar anak di lantai dua sambil mengenakan kain merah. Kemudian anak itu berkata, “Bu, saya bisa terbang. Ayo berangkat” dan melompat dari lantai dua. Pada saat itu, saya <u>tidak bisa mengatakan apa pun atau bahkan menggerakkan tubuh saya</u>. Syukurlah, anak itu tersangkut di tali jemuran dan berdiri dengan lembut di halaman. Anak itu dengan senang hati mengatakan bahwa dia terbang tanpa mengetahui betapa berbahayanya dia. Aku berlari ke arahnya dan memukul pantatnya tanpa berkata apa-apa.

g_text_translate.ja:
息子が6歳の時の出来事です。私の夫はジャーナリストなので、家には映画のチケットがたくさんあります。その中で『スーパーマン』の映画チケットが目に留まったので、子供と映画を観に行ってきました。映画を見た後、子供はスーパーマンになったかのように背中に赤い布をかぶせ、家中や近所を歩き回りました。最初は、「彼は数日間それをやって、その後やめるだろう」と思いました。ある日、私が庭に洗濯物を干していると、２階の子供部屋の窓のところに赤い布をかぶった子供が立っていたのです。すると子供は、「お母さん、飛べるよ。さあ、行こう」と言って、二階から飛び降りました。その瞬間、 私は<u>何も言えず、 体も動かすことができなかった</u>のです。幸いなことに、子供は物干し竿に引っかかり、そっと庭に立っていました。その子は、自分がどれほど危険であるかを知らずに飛んでしまったと喜んでいた。私は何も言わずに彼に駆け寄り、彼のお尻を叩きました。

g_text_translate.de:
Das passierte, als mein Sohn sechs Jahre alt war. Mein Mann ist Journalist, daher gibt es im Haus viele Kinokarten. Unter anderem fiel mir die Kinokarte „Superman“ ins Auge, sodass ich mir den Film mit meinem Kind ansah. Nachdem er den Film gesehen hatte, legte das Kind ein rotes Tuch hinter seinen Rücken, als wäre es Superman geworden, und wanderte durch das ganze Haus und die Nachbarschaft. Zuerst dachte ich: „Er macht das ein paar Tage lang und hört dann auf.“ Dann, eines Tages, als ich die Wäsche im Hof ​​aufhängte, stand das Kind im Fenster des Kinderzimmers im zweiten Stock und trug ein rotes Tuch. Dann sagte das Kind: „Mama, ich kann fliegen. Komm, lass uns gehen“ und sprang aus dem zweiten Stock. In diesem Moment <u>konnte ich nichts sagen oder meinen Körper bewegen</u>. Zum Glück blieb das Kind an der Wäscheleine hängen und blieb sanft im Hof ​​stehen. Das Kind freute sich, sagen zu können, dass es geflogen sei, ohne zu wissen, wie gefährlich es sei. Ich rannte auf ihn zu und schlug ihm wortlos auf den Hintern.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
这件事发生在我儿子六岁的时候。我丈夫是一名记者，所以家里有很多电影票。其中，《超人》的电影票引起了我的注意，于是我就带着孩子去看了电影。看完电影后，孩子在背后背上一块红布，仿佛自己变成了超人，在整个房子和街区里闲逛。起初，我想，‘他会这样做几天，然后就停下来’。然后有一天，当我在院子里晾衣服的时候，孩子站在二楼儿童房的窗户里，身上穿着一件红布。然后孩子说：“妈妈，我会飞了，来吧，我们走吧”，然后从二楼跳了下来。那一刻，我<u>无法说话，甚至无法动弹身体</u>。庆幸的是，孩子被晾衣绳夹住了，轻轻地站在院子里。孩子很高兴地说，他飞走了，却不知道自己有多危险。我跑到他身边，二话不说就打了他的屁股。

g_text_translate.ru:
Это произошло, когда моему сыну было шесть лет. Мой муж журналист, поэтому в доме много билетов в кино. Среди них мне на глаза попался билет в кино «Супермен», и я пошла на фильм вместе с ребенком. После просмотра фильма ребенок закинул себе за спину красную ткань, как будто стал Суперменом, и бродил по всему дому и окрестностям. Сначала я подумал: «Он будет делать это несколько дней, а потом перестанет». Однажды, когда я развешивала белье во дворе, у окна детской комнаты на втором этаже стоял ребенок, одетый в красную ткань. Тогда ребенок сказал: «Мама, я умею летать. Давай, пойдем» и спрыгнул со второго этажа. В тот момент я <u>не мог ничего сказать и даже пошевелиться</u>. К счастью, ребенок зацепился за бельевую веревку и тихо стоял во дворе. Ребенок с радостью рассказал, что летел, не зная, насколько он опасен. Я подбежал к нему и ударил его по заднице, ничего не сказав.

g_text_translate.th:
เรื่องนี้เกิดขึ้นเมื่อลูกชายของฉันอายุได้หกขวบ สามีของฉันเป็นนักข่าว ในบ้านจึงมีตั๋วหนังเยอะมาก ในหมู่พวกเขาตั๋วหนัง 'Superman' ดึงดูดสายตาฉันดังนั้นฉันจึงไปดูหนังกับลูก หลังจากชมภาพยนตร์ เด็กน้อยก็เอาผ้าสีแดงไปไว้ด้านหลังราวกับว่าเขากลายเป็นซูเปอร์แมน และเดินไปรอบๆ บ้านและละแวกใกล้เคียง ตอนแรกฉันคิดว่า 'เขาจะทำอย่างนั้นสักสองสามวันแล้วหยุด' วันหนึ่ง ขณะที่ฉันกำลังซักผ้าอยู่ในสนามหญ้า เด็กคนนั้นยืนอยู่ที่หน้าต่างห้องเด็กชั้นสอง นุ่งผ้าสีแดง เด็กจึงพูดว่า “แม่ครับ ผมบินได้ มาเลย ไปกันเถอะ” แล้วกระโดดลงจากชั้นสอง ในขณะนั้น ฉัน<u>ไม่สามารถพูดอะไรหรือแม้แต่ขยับร่างกายได้</u> โชคดีที่เด็กถูกจับได้บนราวตากผ้าและยืนอยู่อย่างแผ่วเบาในสวน เด็กดีใจที่บอกว่าเขาบินโดยไม่รู้ว่าตนเองอันตรายแค่ไหน ฉันวิ่งเข้าไปหาเขาแล้วตีก้นเขาโดยไม่พูดอะไรเลย

g_text_translate.pt:
Isso aconteceu quando meu filho tinha seis anos. Meu marido é jornalista, então tem muitos ingressos de cinema em casa. Entre eles, o ingresso do cinema ‘Superman’ me chamou a atenção, então fui ver o filme com meu filho. Após assistir ao filme, a criança colocou um pano vermelho nas costas como se tivesse se tornado o Super-Homem e perambulou por toda a casa e bairro. No começo pensei: ‘Ele vai fazer isso por alguns dias e depois parar’. Então, um dia, enquanto eu pendurava a roupa no quintal, a criança estava parada na janela do quarto do segundo andar, vestindo um pano vermelho. Aí a criança disse: “Mãe, eu sei voar. Vamos, vamos” e pulou do segundo andar. Naquele momento, eu <u>não conseguia dizer nada nem mover meu corpo</u>. Felizmente, a criança ficou presa no varal e ficou parada no quintal. A criança ficou encantada ao dizer que havia voado sem saber o quão perigoso era. Corri até ele e bati na bunda dele sem dizer nada.

g_text_translate.hi:
यह तब हुआ जब मेरा बेटा छह साल का था। मेरे पति एक पत्रकार हैं, इसलिए घर में बहुत सारे मूवी टिकट हैं। उनमें से 'सुपरमैन' फिल्म के टिकट पर मेरी नजर गई, इसलिए मैं अपने बच्चे के साथ फिल्म देखने चला गया। फिल्म देखने के बाद बच्चे ने अपनी पीठ के पीछे लाल कपड़ा डाल लिया, जैसे वह सुपरमैन बन गया हो और पूरे घर और मोहल्ले में घूमता रहा. पहले तो मैंने सोचा, 'वह कुछ दिनों तक ऐसा करेगा और फिर बंद कर देगा।' फिर एक दिन, जब मैं आँगन में कपड़े धो रही थी, तो दूसरी मंजिल पर बच्चों के कमरे की खिड़की पर बच्चा लाल कपड़ा पहने खड़ा था। तभी बच्चे ने कहा, "माँ, मैं उड़ सकता हूँ। चलो, चलते हैं" और दूसरी मंजिल से कूद गया। उस पल, मैं <u>कुछ भी नहीं कह सका या अपना शरीर हिला भी नहीं सका</u>। शुक्र है, बच्चा कपड़े की डोरी में फंस गया और धीरे से आँगन में खड़ा हो गया। बच्चे को यह कहते हुए खुशी हुई कि वह बिना यह जाने उड़ गया कि वह कितना खतरनाक है। मैं उसके पास दौड़ा और बिना कुछ कहे उसके बट पर मारा।

Among them, the 'Superman' movie ticket caught my eye, so I went to see the movie with my child. After watching the movie, the child put a red cloth behind his back as if he had become Superman and wandered around the whole house and neighborhood. At first, I thought, ‘He'll do that for a few days and then stop’. Then one day, while I was hanging out the laundry in the yard, the child was standing in the window of the second-floor child's room, wearing a red cloth. Then the child said, "Mom, I can fly. Come on, let's go" and jumped off the second floor. At that moment, I <u>couldn't say anything or even move my body</u>. Thankfully, the child got caught on the clothesline and stood softly in the yard. The child was delighted to say that he had flown without knowing how dangerous he was. I ran up to him and hit him on the butt without saying anything.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bu, oğlum altı yaşındayken oldu. Kocam gazeteci olduğundan evde bir sürü sinema bileti var. Bunların arasında 'Süpermen' sinema bileti gözüme çarptı ve çocuğumla birlikte filme gittik. Çocuk filmi izledikten sonra sanki Süpermen olmuş gibi arkasına kırmızı bir bez koyarak tüm evi ve mahalleyi dolaştı. İlk başta 'Bunu birkaç gün yapar, sonra bırakır' diye düşündüm. Sonra bir gün, ben bahçede çamaşırları asarken, çocuk ikinci kattaki çocuk odasının penceresinde kırmızı bir bezle duruyordu. Daha sonra çocuk "Anne ben uçabiliyorum. Hadi gidelim" deyip ikinci kattan atladı. O anda <u>hiçbir şey söyleyemedim, hatta bedenimi hareket ettiremedim</u>. Neyse ki çocuk çamaşır ipine takıldı ve yavaşça bahçede durdu. Çocuk ne kadar tehlikeli olduğunu bilmeden uçtuğunu söylemekten büyük mutluluk duydu. Hiçbir şey söylemeden yanına koştum ve kıçına vurdum.

#######

This happened when my son was six years old. My husband is a journalist, so there are a lot of movie tickets in the house. Among them, the 'Superman' movie ticket caught my eye, so I went to see the movie with my child. After watching the movie, the child put a red cloth behind his back as if he had become Superman and wandered around the whole house and neighborhood. At first, I thought, ‘He'll do that for a few days and then stop’. Then one day, while I was hanging out the laundry in the yard, the child was standing in the window of the second-floor child's room, wearing a red cloth. Then the child said, "Mom, I can fly. Come on, let's go" and jumped off the second floor. At that moment, I <u>couldn't say anything or even move my body</u>. Thankfully, the child got caught on the clothesline and stood softly in the yard. The child was delighted to say that he had flown without knowing how dangerous he was. I ran up to him and hit him on the butt without saying anything.

general.g_text: 지난 주말, 출산 후 육아에 전념하느라 온종일 집에만 있던 나에게 남편이 봄도 되었으니 바람이라도 쐬러 가자고 하였다. 오래간만에 남편과 함께 하는 외출이었다. 결혼 전에 입던 옷 중에 가장 편한 옷으로 대충 챙겨 입고 집을 나섰다. 따뜻한 날씨에 외식도 하고 커피도 마실 수 있어서 기분이 좋았다. 계절도 바뀌었으니까 옷 구경이라도 할까 싶어서 백화점에 들렀다. 남편도 나온 김에 옷을 한 벌 사 입으라고 해서 기분 좋게 옷을 고르기 시작했다. 원피스, 치마, 바지 등 한 시간 넘게 고르고 입어도 봤지만 뭐 하나 제대로 맞는 옷이 없었다. 아기는 울어 대고 마음에 드는 옷은 못 고르는 것이 나의 자존감을 바닥으로 떨어뜨렸다. 예전의 모습은 사라지고 거울에 비친 낯선 내 모습에 속이 상해서 고개를 숙인 채 우두커니 서 있었다. 그때 남편이 내 손을 꼭 잡으며 '내 눈엔 당신이 여전히 예뻐....'라고 말했다. 그 말을 듣는 순간 내 눈에는 <u>눈물이 핑 돌았다</u>.

g_text_translate.vi: Cuối tuần trước, tôi, người vốn chỉ ở nhà cả ngày để tập trung chăm sóc con sau khi sinh, được chồng rủ đi hóng gió một chút vì đã đến mùa xuân rồi. Lâu lắm rồi tôi mới ra ngoài với chồng. Tôi đã mặc đại khái quần áo thoải mái nhất trong số quần áo tôi từng mặc trước khi kết hôn và ra khỏi nhà. Tâm trạng tôi rất tốt vì có thể đi ăn ngoài và uống cà phê trong thời tiết ấm áp. Vì mùa đã thay đổi nên tôi đã ghé qua trung tâm thương mại để xem quần áo. Chồng tôi cũng bảo tôi mua một bộ quần áo nhân dịp ra ngoài nên tôi bắt đầu chọn quần áo một cách vui vẻ. Tôi đã chọn và mặc thử đầm, quần, váy, v.v. hơn một giờ đồng hồ nhưng không có bộ nào vừa cả. Việc đứa bé quấy khóc và tôi không thể chọn được bộ đồ ưng ý khiến lòng tự trọng của tôi rơi xuống sàn. Hình dáng trước đây của tôi biến mất và tôi cảm thấy đau lòng khi nhìn thấy hình ảnh xa lạ của mình trong gương, vì vậy tôi đứng đó và cúi đầu. Lúc đó chồng tôi nắm chặt tay tôi và nói: 'Trong mắt anh, em vẫn đẹp…' Khoảnh khắc nghe câu đó, trong mắt tôi <u>nước mắt trực trào</u>.
g_text_translate.en: Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. It's been a long time since I've been out with my husband. I left home wearing the most comfortable clothes I had worn before marriage. I was happy to eat out and drink coffee in warm weather. Since the season has changed, I stopped by the department store to see some clothes. My husband also told me to buy clothes while I was out, so I started to choose clothes happily. I chose and wore dresses, skirts, and pants for more than an hour, but none of them fit properly. The crying baby and not being able to pick out the clothes I liked ruined my self-esteem. My old self disappeared and I stood there with my head bowed, upset at the strange reflection of myself in the mirror. Then my husband held my hand tightly and said, 'You are still pretty in my eyes...'. <u>Tears welled up</u> in my eyes the moment I heard it.
g_text_translate.it:
Lo scorso fine settimana mio marito ha chiesto a me, che rimanevo a casa tutto il giorno concentrandomi sulla cura dei bambini dopo il parto, di andare a prendere una boccata d'aria perché era primavera. È da molto tempo che non esco con mio marito. Sono uscito di casa indossando gli abiti più comodi che avevo indossato prima del matrimonio. Ero felice di mangiare fuori e bere un caffè quando faceva caldo. Dato che la stagione è cambiata, mi sono fermata al grande magazzino per vedere dei vestiti. Mio marito mi ha anche detto di comprare dei vestiti mentre ero fuori, così ho iniziato a scegliere i vestiti con gioia. Ho scelto e indossato abiti, gonne e pantaloni per più di un'ora, ma nessuno di loro mi andava bene. Il bambino che piangeva e non riuscire a scegliere i vestiti che mi piacevano hanno rovinato la mia autostima. Il mio vecchio io è scomparso e sono rimasto lì con la testa chinata, sconvolto dallo strano riflesso di me stesso nello specchio. Allora mio marito mi ha stretto forte la mano e ha detto: "Sei ancora carina ai miei occhi...". <u>Le lacrime sono salite</u> nei miei occhi nel momento in cui l'ho sentito.

#######

Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. It's been a long time since I've been out with my husband. I left home wearing the most comfortable clothes I had worn before marriage. I was happy to eat out and drink coffee in warm weather. Since the season has changed, I stopped by the department store to see some clothes. My husband also told me to buy clothes while I was out, so I started to choose clothes happily. I chose and wore dresses, skirts, and pants for more than an hour, but none of them fit properly. The crying baby and not being able to pick out the clothes I liked ruined my self-esteem. My old self disappeared and I stood there with my head bowed, upset at the strange reflection of myself in the mirror. Then my husband held my hand tightly and said, 'You are still pretty in my eyes...'. <u>Tears welled up</u> in my eyes the moment I heard it.
g_text_translate.fr:
Le week-end dernier, mon mari m'a demandé, qui restais toute la journée à la maison à m'occuper de mes enfants après l'accouchement, d'aller prendre l'air car c'était le printemps. Cela fait longtemps que je ne suis pas sortie avec mon mari. J'ai quitté la maison avec les vêtements les plus confortables que j'avais portés avant le mariage. J'étais heureux de manger dehors et de boire du café par temps chaud. Depuis que la saison a changé, je me suis arrêté au grand magasin pour voir des vêtements. Mon mari m'a aussi dit d'acheter des vêtements pendant mon absence, alors j'ai commencé à choisir des vêtements avec plaisir. J'ai choisi et porté des robes, des jupes et des pantalons pendant plus d'une heure, mais aucun d'entre eux ne m'allait correctement. Le bébé qui pleurait et le fait de ne pas pouvoir choisir les vêtements qui me plaisaient ont ruiné mon estime de soi. Mon ancien moi a disparu et je suis resté là, la tête baissée, bouleversé par l'étrange reflet de moi-même dans le miroir. Ensuite, mon mari m'a serré la main et m'a dit : "Tu es toujours jolie à mes yeux...". <u>Les larmes sont montées dans mes yeux</u> au moment où je l'ai entendu.

#######

g_text_translate.es:
El fin de semana pasado, mi marido, que me quedaba en casa todo el día concentrándome en el cuidado de los niños después de dar a luz, me pidió que fuera a tomar un poco de aire fresco porque era primavera. Ha pasado mucho tiempo desde que salí con mi marido. Salí de casa con la ropa más cómoda que había usado antes de casarme. Estaba feliz de salir a comer y tomar café cuando hacía calor. Como la temporada ha cambiado, pasé por los grandes almacenes para ver algo de ropa. Mi esposo también me dijo que comprara ropa mientras estaba fuera, así que comencé a elegir ropa felizmente. Elegí y usé vestidos, faldas y pantalones durante más de una hora, pero ninguno me quedaba bien. El llanto del bebé y el no poder elegir la ropa que me gustaba arruinaron mi autoestima. Mi antiguo yo desapareció y me quedé allí con la cabeza gacha, molesto por el extraño reflejo de mí mismo en el espejo. Entonces mi marido me tomó la mano con fuerza y ​​me dijo: 'Aún eres bonita a mis ojos...'. <u>Las lágrimas brotaron</u> de mis ojos en el momento en que lo escuché.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Akhir pekan lalu, suami saya mengajak saya, yang seharian tinggal di rumah berkonsentrasi mengasuh anak setelah melahirkan, untuk pergi mencari udara segar karena saat itu musim semi. Sudah lama sekali aku tidak keluar bersama suamiku. Saya meninggalkan rumah dengan mengenakan pakaian paling nyaman yang pernah saya kenakan sebelum menikah. Saya senang makan di luar dan minum kopi dalam cuaca hangat. Karena musim telah berganti, saya mampir ke department store untuk melihat beberapa pakaian. Suamiku juga menyuruhku membeli pakaian saat aku keluar, jadi aku mulai memilih pakaian dengan gembira. Saya memilih dan mengenakan gaun, rok, dan celana selama lebih dari satu jam, tetapi tidak ada satupun yang pas. Bayi yang menangis dan tidak bisa memilih pakaian yang saya suka merusak harga diri saya. Diriku yang lama menghilang dan aku berdiri di sana dengan kepala tertunduk, kesal melihat bayangan aneh diriku di cermin. Lalu suamiku menggenggam erat tanganku dan berkata, 'Kamu masih cantik di mataku...'. <u>Air mata menggenang</u> di mataku saat aku mendengarnya.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
先週末、産後育児に集中して一日中家にいた私に、夫が「春だから外の空気を吸いに行こう」と誘ってくれました。夫と出かけるのは久しぶりです。私は結婚前に着ていた最も快適な服を着て家を出ました。暖かい気候の中、外食したりコーヒーを飲んだりするのが幸せでした。季節が変わったので、洋服を見にデパートに立ち寄りました。夫も「私が出かけている間に服を買ってね」と言ってくれたので、楽しく服を選ぶようになりました。ワンピース、スカート、パンツを選んで1時間以上着ましたが、どれもぴったりとはいきませんでした。赤ちゃんが泣いたり、好きな服を選ぶことができなかったりしたことで、私の自尊心は台無しになりました。昔の自分は消え、鏡に映る自分の奇妙な姿に動揺して、頭を下げてそこに立っていました。すると夫が私の手をぎゅっと握って、「私の目にはまだ綺麗だよ…」と言いました。それを聞いた瞬間に<u>涙が溢れてきました</u>。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Letztes Wochenende bat mich mein Mann, der nach der Geburt den ganzen Tag zu Hause blieb und sich auf die Kinderbetreuung konzentrierte, etwas frische Luft zu schnappen, weil es Frühling war. Es ist schon lange her, dass ich mit meinem Mann unterwegs war. Ich verließ das Haus und trug die bequemste Kleidung, die ich vor der Heirat getragen hatte. Bei warmem Wetter ging ich gerne auswärts essen und Kaffee trinken. Da sich die Jahreszeit geändert hat, bin ich im Kaufhaus vorbeigekommen, um mir ein paar Klamotten anzusehen. Mein Mann sagte mir auch, ich solle Kleidung kaufen, während ich unterwegs war, also begann ich, glücklich Kleidung auszuwählen. Ich habe mehr als eine Stunde lang Kleider, Röcke und Hosen ausgewählt und getragen, aber keines davon passte richtig. Das weinende Baby und die Tatsache, dass ich nicht die Kleidung auswählen konnte, die mir gefiel, ruinierten mein Selbstwertgefühl. Mein altes Ich verschwand und ich stand mit gesenktem Kopf da und war verärgert über das seltsame Spiegelbild meiner selbst. Dann hielt mein Mann meine Hand fest und sagte: „Du bist immer noch hübsch in meinen Augen…“. <u>Tränen stiegen</u> in meinen Augen, als ich es hörte.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
上周末，丈夫让产后整天在家专心照顾孩子的我出去呼吸一下新鲜空气，因为春天到了。我已经很久没有和我丈夫出去了。我穿着婚前穿过的最舒服的衣服离开家。我很高兴在温暖的天气里出去吃饭、喝咖啡。由于换季了，我顺便去百货公司看一些衣服。老公在外出的时候也叫我去买衣服，我就开始高高兴兴地选衣服了。我选了裙子、裙子和裤子，穿了一个多小时，但没有一件合适。哭闹的婴儿和无法挑选出我喜欢的衣服毁了我的自尊。过去的自己消失了，我低着头站在那里，对镜子里奇怪的自己感到不安。然后老公紧紧握住我的手说：“我眼里的你还是很漂亮……”。当我听到这句话的那一刻，<u>泪水涌出</u>。

#######

Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. g_text_translate.zh-TW:
上週末，丈夫讓產後整天在家專心照顧孩子的我出去呼吸一下新鮮空氣，因為春天到了。我已經很久沒有和我丈夫出去了。我穿著婚前穿過的最舒服的衣服離開家。我很高興在溫暖的天氣裡出去吃飯、喝咖啡。由於換季了，我順便去百貨公司看一些衣服。老公在外出的時候也叫我去買衣服，我就開始高高興興地選衣服了。我選了裙子、裙子和褲子，穿了一個多小時，但沒有一件合適。哭鬧的嬰兒和無法挑選出我喜歡的衣服毀了我的自尊。過去的自己消失了，我低著頭站在那裡，對鏡子里奇怪的自己感到不安。然後老公緊緊握住我的手說：“我眼裡的你還是很漂亮……”。當我聽到這句話的那一刻，<u>淚水湧出</u>。

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
В прошлые выходные мой муж попросил меня, которая весь день сидела дома, сосредоточившись на уходе за детьми после родов, пойти подышать свежим воздухом, потому что была весна. Я давно не встречалась с мужем. Я вышла из дома в самой удобной одежде, которую носил до свадьбы. Я был рад пойти куда-нибудь поесть и выпить кофе в теплую погоду. Поскольку сезон сменился, я зашел в универмаг, чтобы посмотреть одежду. Мой муж также посоветовал мне покупать одежду, пока меня нет дома, поэтому я с радостью начала выбирать одежду. Я выбирала и носила платья, юбки и брюки больше часа, но ни одно из них мне не подошло. Плачущий ребенок и невозможность выбрать понравившуюся одежду подорвали мою самооценку. Мое прежнее «я» исчезло, и я стоял, опустив голову, расстроенный своим странным отражением в зеркале. Потом муж крепко взял меня за руку и сказал: «Ты все еще красивая в моих глазах…». <u>Слезы навернулись</u> у меня на глазах, как только я это услышал.

g_text_translate.th:
สุดสัปดาห์ที่ผ่านมาสามีของฉันขอให้ฉันซึ่งอยู่บ้านทั้งวันโดยเน้นดูแลลูกหลังคลอดให้ออกไปสูดอากาศบริสุทธิ์เพราะเป็นฤดูใบไม้ผลิ เป็นเวลานานแล้วตั้งแต่ฉันได้ออกไปเที่ยวกับสามี ฉันออกจากบ้านโดยสวมเสื้อผ้าที่สบายที่สุดที่ฉันเคยใส่ก่อนแต่งงาน ฉันมีความสุขที่ได้ออกไปกินข้าวนอกบ้านและดื่มกาแฟท่ามกลางอากาศอบอุ่น เนื่องจากฤดูกาลเปลี่ยนไป ฉันจึงแวะห้างสรรพสินค้าเพื่อดูเสื้อผ้า สามีของฉันก็บอกให้ซื้อเสื้อผ้าตอนที่ฉันออกไปข้างนอกด้วย ฉันก็เลยเริ่มเลือกเสื้อผ้าอย่างมีความสุข ฉันเลือกและสวมชุดเดรส กระโปรง และกางเกงเป็นเวลานานกว่าหนึ่งชั่วโมง แต่ไม่มีชุดใดที่พอดีเลย การร้องไห้ของทารกและไม่สามารถเลือกเสื้อผ้าที่ฉันชอบได้ทำลายความภาคภูมิใจในตนเอง ตัวตนเก่าๆ ของฉันหายไป และฉันยืนก้มหัวเสียใจกับภาพสะท้อนแปลกๆ ของตัวเองในกระจก จากนั้นสามีของฉันก็จับมือฉันแน่นแล้วพูดว่า 'เธอยังสวยในสายตาฉัน...' <u>น้ำตาไหลออกมา</u>ในดวงตาของฉันทันทีที่ได้ยิน

#######

Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. It's been a long time since I've been out with my husband. I left home wearing the most comfortable clothes I had worn before marriage. I was happy to eat out and drink coffee in warm weather. Since the season has changed, I stopped by the department store to see some clothes. My husband also told me to buy clothes while I was out, so I started to choose clothes happily. I chose and wore dresses, skirts, and pants for more than an hour, but none of them fit properly. The crying baby and not being able to pick out the clothes I liked ruined my self-esteem. My old self disappeared and I stood there with my head bowed, upset at the strange reflection of myself in the mirror. Then my husband held my hand tightly and said, 'You are still pretty in my eyes...'. <u>Tears welled up</u> in my eyes the moment I heard it.
g_text_translate.pt:
No fim de semana passado, meu marido me pediu, que ficava em casa o dia todo concentrada nos cuidados com os filhos após o parto, para ir tomar um pouco de ar fresco porque era primavera. Já faz muito tempo que não saio com meu marido. Saí de casa vestindo as roupas mais confortáveis ​​que usava antes do casamento. Fiquei feliz em comer fora e tomar café em dias quentes. Como a estação mudou, passei na loja de departamentos para ver algumas roupas. Meu marido também me disse para comprar roupas enquanto eu estivesse fora, então comecei a escolher as roupas com alegria. Escolhi e usei vestidos, saias e calças por mais de uma hora, mas nenhum deles serviu bem. O choro do bebê e a impossibilidade de escolher as roupas que eu gostava arruinaram minha autoestima. Meu antigo eu desapareceu e fiquei ali com a cabeça baixa, chateado com o estranho reflexo de mim mesmo no espelho. Aí meu marido segurou minha mão com força e disse: 'Você ainda é bonita aos meus olhos...'. <u>Lágrimas brotaram</u> dos meus olhos no momento em que ouvi isso.

#######

Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. It's been a long time since I've been out with my husband. I left home wearing the most comfortable clothes I had worn before marriage. I was happy to eat out and drink coffee in warm weather. Since the season has changed, I stopped by the department store to see some clothes. My husband also told me to buy clothes while I was out, so I started to choose clothes happily. I chose and wore dresses, skirts, and pants for more than an hour, but none of them fit properly. The crying baby and not being able to pick out the clothes I liked ruined my self-esteem. My old self disappeared and I stood there with my head bowed, upset at the strange reflection of myself in the mirror. Then my husband held my hand tightly and said, 'You are still pretty in my eyes...'. <u>Tears welled up</u> in my eyes the moment I heard it.
g_text_translate.hi:
पिछले सप्ताहांत, मेरे पति ने मुझसे, जो बच्चे को जन्म देने के बाद पूरे दिन घर पर रहकर बच्चे की देखभाल पर ध्यान केंद्रित करती थी, कुछ ताज़ी हवा लेने के लिए कहा क्योंकि वसंत का मौसम था। मुझे अपने पति के साथ बाहर गए काफी समय हो गया है। मैं सबसे आरामदायक कपड़े पहनकर घर से निकली जो मैंने शादी से पहले पहने थे। गर्म मौसम में बाहर खाना खाने और कॉफ़ी पीने से मुझे ख़ुशी हुई। चूँकि मौसम बदल गया है, मैं कुछ कपड़े देखने के लिए डिपार्टमेंट स्टोर के पास रुका। मेरे पति ने भी मुझे बाहर रहने के दौरान कपड़े खरीदने के लिए कहा, इसलिए मैंने खुशी-खुशी कपड़े चुनना शुरू कर दिया। मैंने एक घंटे से अधिक समय तक कपड़े, स्कर्ट और पैंट चुने और पहने, लेकिन उनमें से कोई भी ठीक से फिट नहीं हुआ। रोते हुए बच्चे और मेरे पसंद के कपड़े न चुन पाने के कारण मेरा आत्मसम्मान नष्ट हो गया। मेरा पुराना व्यक्तित्व गायब हो गया और मैं दर्पण में अपने अजीब प्रतिबिंब को देखकर परेशान होकर सिर झुकाए वहीं खड़ा हो गया। तभी मेरे पति ने मेरा हाथ कस कर पकड़ लिया और बोले, 'तुम अब भी मेरी नजरों में खूबसूरत हो...'। जैसे ही मैंने यह सुना, मेरी आँखों में आँसू आ गए।

#######

Last weekend, my husband asked me, who stayed at home all day concentrating on childcare after giving birth, to go get some fresh air because it was spring. Since the season has changed, I stopped by the department store to see some clothes. My husband also told me to buy clothes while I was out, so I started to choose clothes happily. I chose and wore dresses, skirts, and pants for more than an hour, but none of them fit properly. The crying baby and not being able to pick out the clothes I liked ruined my self-esteem. My old self disappeared and I stood there with my head bowed, upset at the strange reflection of myself in the mirror. Then my husband held my hand tightly and said, 'You are still pretty in my eyes...'. <u>Tears welled up</u> in my eyes the moment I heard it.
g_text_translate.tr:
Geçtiğimiz hafta sonu eşim, doğumdan sonra tüm gün evde kalıp çocuk bakımına odaklanan benden bahar olduğu için biraz temiz hava almamı istedi. Kocamla dışarı çıkmayalı uzun zaman oldu. Evlenmeden önce giydiğim en rahat kıyafetleri giyerek evden çıktım. Sıcak havalarda dışarıda yemek yemekten ve kahve içmekten mutluydum. Mevsim değiştiği için birkaç kıyafet görmek için mağazaya uğradım. Eşim de ben dışarıdayken kıyafet almamı söyledi, ben de mutlu bir şekilde kıyafet seçmeye başladım. Bir saatten fazla elbise, etek ve pantolon seçip giydim ama hiçbiri üzerime tam oturmadı. Ağlayan bebek ve beğendiğim kıyafetleri seçememek özgüvenimi yerle bir etti. Eski benliğim yok oldu ve aynadaki garip yansımam karşısında başım öne eğik bir şekilde orada durdum. Sonra eşim sıkıca elimi tuttu ve 'Benim gözümde hâlâ çok güzelsin...' dedi. Bunu duyduğum anda gözlerimden <u>yaşlar aktı</u>.

#######

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 나는 남편의 말 때문에 상처를 받았다.
나는 결혼 한 후에 몸매가 망가졌다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn trên.
1. Tôi mệt mỏi vì việc lựa chọn quần áo.
2. Tôi bị tổn thương vì lời nói của chồng.
3. Thân hình tôi đã bị mất dáng sau khi kết hôn.
4. Tôi đã giảm cân để đi ra ngoài.
--------------------
Nhân vật 'tôi' không chọn được bộ quần áo nào vừa với mình, cô cũng thấy đau lòng khi nhìn mình trong gương. 3 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. I was tired because I had a hard time choosing clothes.
2. I was hurt by my husband's words.
3. I got out of shape after I got married.
4. I went on a diet to go out.
--------------------
This person couldn't choose clothes that fit her, she was also upset when she looked at herself in the mirror. 3 is the correct answer.

3. Sono andato fuori forma dopo essermi sposato.
4. Sono andato a dieta per uscire.
--------------------
Questa persona non riusciva a scegliere i vestiti che le stavano bene, inoltre era turbata quando si guardava allo specchio. 3 è la risposta corretta.

#######
